blocks:
Ayah

الٓمٓ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Mim. (1)

Key Points

In the first volume of the commentary, it was stated regarding the Ayah «بِسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم» that it holds a special status and is an independent Ayah itself. Reciting it at the beginning of every task is recommended, and its repetition at the start of every Surah is a sign of its importance. «بسم اللَّه» is not exclusive to Islam; other Prophets also used this Ayah with the same phrase. The fact that «بسم اللَّه» begins with the letter 'Bā' (ب), which signifies seeking aid and assistance, indicates that no task can be accomplished without His will and desire.

Seeking assistance from Allah means seeking help from all of His attributes. He is Rahmān (the Most Compassionate) and Rahīm (the Most Merciful), and our hopeful gaze is fixed upon His mercy—a vast and universal mercy for this world and the Hereafter. Indeed! Others either lack mercy, or their mercy is limited to specific individuals, times, or places, and their goal in showing mercy is reciprocity, similar to giving grass to an animal or grain to a bird in order to benefit from it. A vast and eternal mercy for everyone, without the intention of return, belongs exclusively to Allah. Therefore, let us seek aid from such a Rahmān and Rahīm God and say: «بسم اللّه الرّحمن الرّحیم»

As mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah, «الم» is one of the disconnected letters (Hurūf Muqaṭṭa‘ah). Perhaps the most famous and best view regarding the disconnected letters is that they are a secret code between Allah and His Prophet. (1) Or, it may be an announcement that the Quran, the Divine Word, is also composed of these very alphabet letters, yet it remains a miracle. If you can, bring forth a book like the Quran using these same letters. Just as Divine power creates man from dust, while humans make pottery, bricks, and jars from dust. This is the difference between Divine power and human power.

The disconnected letters «الم» appear at the beginning of six Surahs: Al-Baqarah, Āl ‘Imrān, Al-‘Ankabūt, Ar-Rūm, Luqmān, and As-Sajdah.

1) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «الم» at the beginning of Surah Āl ‘Imrān is the code for the phrase: «اَنا اللّه المجید» (I am Allah, the Glorious). Tafsir Nūr ath-Thaqalayn, Vol. 1, p. 309.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Āl ‘Imrān

Surah Āl ‘Imrān contains 200 Ayahs, and its verses possess a specific order and harmony. This Surah was revealed in Medina at a time when the call of Islam was rising in the world and enemies had become sensitive and vigilant. As stated in Tafsir Al-Mīzān, a group of Christians from the Najrān region of Yemen came to Medina to hear the words of the Prophet. The first eighty-something Ayahs of this Surah were revealed all at once to explain the teachings of Islam to them, concluding with the event of Mubāhalah (mutual cursing). Furthermore, the event of the Battle of Uhud and the call for Muslims to exercise patience and steadfastness are discussed in this Surah. The name of this Surah is based on Ayahs 33 and 35, which describe the story of the birth of Mary, daughter of ‘Imrān. Therefore, the intent of Āl ‘Imrān (The Family of ‘Imrān) in this Surah refers to Lady Maryam علیها السلام and Prophet Jesus عیسى عليه السلام, although the name of Prophet Moses’ father was also ‘Imrān.

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْحَىُّ ٱلْقَيُّومُ (۲)
Allah—there is no god except Him—is the Living One, the All-sustainer. (2)

Key Points

Al-Qayyūm (The Self-Subsisting/Sustainer) is applied to one who does not rely on anyone else, but others rely on Him, or He is the sustainer (source of existence) of all things. Allah has complete control in creation, planning (governance), and dominance. Material factors and means become instruments of existence, but they are not independent, and their causality does not originate from themselves. Other beings do not possess independent knowledge, life, or power. He alone is the Living One whose life sustains every living being. In the supplication of Jawshan Kabir, we read: He is Living before every living thing and Living after every living thing. No living being is His partner, and He needs no one. The life (soul) of all living things and their sustenance are in His hand. He is the Living One who did not inherit His life from anyone.

Messages

1- Monotheism (Tawhid) is the heading of the heavenly scriptures. «لااله الاّ هو»

2- The condition for being worshipped is possessing inherent perfection, and only Allah is Self-Subsisting and absolutely independent. «الحىّ القیّوم»

Ayah

نَزَّلَ عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ مُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَأَنزَلَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ (۳)
He has sent down to you the Book with the truth, confirming what was [revealed] before it, and He had sent down the Torah and the Evangel (3)

Key Points

The word "Torah" (تورات), in the Hebrew language, means Sharia (law) and statute. The current Torah has five sections named: 1- The Book of Genesis 2- The Book of Exodus 3- The Book of Leviticus 4- The Book of Numbers 5- The Book of Deuteronomy. Since the Torah discusses the death and burial of Prophet Moses عليه السلام, it is clear that these contents were compiled after Prophet Moses.

The word "Injil" (انجیل) has Greek roots and means glad tidings. The Injil is the name of the Christians' book, and wherever the Quran mentions it, it uses the singular form. However, today there are multiple Gospels among Christians, all of which were written after Prophet Jesus عليه السلام by his companions and followers. These Gospels, which are known by their names, are: 1- The Gospel of Matthew. 2- The Gospel of Mark. 3- The Gospel of Luke. 4- The Gospel of John.

Messages

1- The advent of heavenly books is a manifestation of Allah's Sustainer-ship (Qayyumiyyah). «القیّوم نزّل علیك الكتاب»

2- The revelation of the Quran upon the Prophet relates to his personality and Allah's honoring of him. «نزل علیك الكتاب» The word ‘alayka (upon you) precedes the word al-Kitāb (the Book).

3- The Quran is immune from all falsehood and conforms to the truth. «بالحقّ»

4- The heavenly books of the Prophets confirm one another. «مصدّقا لما بین یدیه»

5- Confirming previous heavenly books is a factor for the unity of monotheists and one of the methods for inviting others. «مصدّقاً»

6- The Quran's confirmation of the Torah and the Injil is a great right owed to the previous religions; otherwise, with the superstitions that entered the Torah and the Injil, their heavenly origin would have been completely forgotten. «مصدّقاً»

7- Although the stages of Divine training, and their tools and instructions, have differed in every time and circumstance, they possess a progressive rhythm and unity of purpose. «مصدقاً»

Ayah

مِن قَبْلُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَأَنزَلَ ٱلْفُرْقَانَ ۗ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَزِيزٌ ذُو ٱنتِقَامٍ (٤)
before as guidance for mankind, and He has sent down the Criterion. Indeed, there is a severe punishment for those who deny the signs of Allah, and Allah is all-mighty, avenger. (4)

Key Points

One of the names of the Qur'an is Al-Furqan. Al-Furqan means that which causes separation, distinction, and differentiation. Since the Qur'an is the means of distinguishing truth from falsehood in all matters, it has been called Al-Furqan. Severe punishment is not exclusive to the Hereafter. This Ayah does not state that the punishment of the disbelievers is related only to the Hereafter, because sometimes preoccupation, greed for accumulating wealth, and heedlessness of Allah and spirituality are themselves a punishment. In Ayah 85 of Surah At-Tawbah, He says: Allah gives wealth and children to a group so that He may punish them through them. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "انزل الفرقان" (He sent down the Criterion): Every firm matter is Al-Furqan, and the Book (الكتاب) in "انزل الكتاب" (He sent down the Book) refers to the entire Qur'an. [2]

2) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 162.

Messages

1- Guiding people has been an eternal Divine tradition. "من قبل" (before)

2- The audience of the Divine Books is the general public, not a specific tribe or race. "للنّاس" (for mankind)

3- The Qur'an is the means of recognizing truth from falsehood. "و انزل الفرقان" (and He sent down the Criterion)

4- Wherever we become confused among tastes, thoughts, and beliefs in any way, the final word belongs to the Qur'an. (Even if we become confused among narrations, we must pay attention to the narration that conforms to the Qur'an.) "الفرقان" (The Criterion)

5- After the revelation of all those Books and means of guidance, the recompense for the obstinate person is severe punishment. "عذاب شدید" (severe punishment)

6- Divine punishment follows the completion of the argument (proof) upon the people. "هدى للناس... الّذین كفروا... لهم عذاب شدید" (guidance for mankind... those who disbelieved... for them is a severe punishment)

7- This Ayah is a source of encouragement and consolation for the Prophet and the believers, assuring them that Allah will settle the account with the stubborn disbelievers. "عزیز ذوانتقام" (Mighty, Possessor of Retribution)

8- The source of His penalty and retribution is our own actions; otherwise, He is Mighty and lacks nothing. "عزیز ذوانتقام" (Mighty, Possessor of Retribution)

9- The disbelief of people has no effect on Allah. "كفروا، واللَّه عزیز" (they disbelieved, and Allah is Mighty)

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَخْفَىٰ عَلَيْهِ شَىْءٌ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ (٥)
Nothing is indeed hidden from Allah in the earth or in the sky. (5)

Key Points

In the preceding verses, the discussion centered on Allah being al-Qayyūm (the Self-Subsisting, the Sustainer). This verse addresses the manifestness of all things to Him, which is a manifestation of His Qayyūmiyyah (Sustaining Power).

Messages

1- Allah is aware of everything. We and all of existence are constantly in the presence of Allah; let us not sin in His presence. Considering the previous verse, it is understood that the disbelief and disobedience of no one is hidden from Allah. «كفروا... لا یخفى علیه شى ء»

2- The multiplicity of individuals, the multiplicity of deeds, and the multiplicity of places and times do not conceal any matter from Allah. «فى الارض ولا فى السماء»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُصَوِّرُكُمْ فِى ٱلْأَرْحَامِ كَيْفَ يَشَآءُ ۚ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٦)
It is He who forms you in the wombs [of your mothers] however He wishes. There is no god except Him, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (6)

Key Points

The formation of the fetus in the womb is another sign of Allah's Self-Subsistence (Qayyum), which we read about in the previous verses. The topic of human formation in the mother's womb is mentioned between two verses discussing the revelation of the Book. Perhaps the secret behind this is that the God Who organizes and shapes you in the mother's womb also organizes your future and eternal growth through the enactment of divine laws and the sending of heavenly Books. The influence of hereditary, hygienic, nutritional, or psychological factors on the shaping of individuals' faces and bodies is also part of the divine traditions and falls within the scope of His power, planning, and wisdom.

Messages

1- The source of creation (Takwin) and legislation (Tashri') is one. He Who sent the Book for your guidance is the same One Who created you. «هدىً للنّاس... یصوّركم»

2- The shaping of man in the mother's womb is solely by His Hand. «هو الّذى یصوّركم»

3- Now that the shaping of forms is in His Hand, let us not blame one another because of shape or appearance. «هوالّذى یصوّركم»

4- His display of power is not limited to a specific place or time. «یصوّركم فى الارحام»

5- Reflection upon one's own creation is a factor leading to faith in Allah. «یصوّركم... لااله الاّ هو»

6- Although Allah is capable of everything; «كیف یشاء» (as He wills), He does not do anything contrary to wisdom. «هوالعزیزالحكیم»

7- The diverse forms of human beings are a sign of His power and wisdom. «یصوّركم... العزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِنْهُ ءَايَـٰتٌ مُّحْكَمَـٰتٌ هُنَّ أُمُّ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَأُخَرُ مُتَشَـٰبِهَـٰتٌ ۖ فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ زَيْغٌ فَيَتَّبِعُونَ مَا تَشَـٰبَهَ مِنْهُ ٱبْتِغَآءَ ٱلْفِتْنَةِ وَٱبْتِغَآءَ تَأْوِيلِهِۦ ۗ وَمَا يَعْلَمُ تَأْوِيلَهُۥٓ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَٱلرَّٰسِخُونَ فِى ٱلْعِلْمِ يَقُولُونَ ءَامَنَّا بِهِۦ كُلٌّ مِّنْ عِندِ رَبِّنَا ۗ وَمَا يَذَّكَّرُ إِلَّآ أُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۷)
It is He who has sent down to you the Book. Parts of it are definitive verses, which are the mother of the Book, while others are metaphorical. As for those in whose hearts is deviance, they pursue what is metaphorical in it, courting temptation, and seeking its interpretation. But no one knows its interpretation except Allah and those firmly grounded in knowledge; they say, ‘We believe in it; all of it is from our Lord.’ And none takes admonition except those who possess intellect. (7)

Key Points

In this Ayah, several questions arise: Question: In one place, the Quran considers all its verses muhkam (firmly established) and states: «كتاب اُحكمت آیاته» (A Book whose verses have been made firm) (3), and in another place, it considers all of them mutashābih (similar/consistent) and states: «كتاباً متشابهاً»[۴] (A Book consistent [with itself]), but in this Ayah, it introduces some as muhkam and some as mutashābih. What is the matter? Answer: Where it stated that all verses of the Quran are muhkam, it means that there is no weak or baseless statement in any Ayah. And where it stated that all verses are mutashābih, it means that the system and rhythm of the Quranic verses are harmonious, uniform, and similar to one another. However, despite this, in terms of public understanding, not all verses are the same; some are explicit, clear, and understandable by everyone, while others possess lofty and complex meanings, which causes doubt and confusion for them. Question: Why are mutashābih verses used in the Quran? Answer: Firstly: The existence of mutashābih verses provides a basis for thought and contemplation on the verses. Secondly: It causes people to refer to the divine leaders. Indeed, if every lesson were easy, the student would not feel the need for a teacher. Thirdly: The mutashābihāt are a means of testing people. A group with crooked thoughts uses them to pursue their sinister goals, while a group of thinkers, according to Imam Reza عليه السلام, discovers the correct meaning of the verses by referring to the muhkamāt (firm verses).[5] Question: What are examples of mutashābih verses? Answer: We read in the Quran: «الى ربّها ناظرة»[6] (Looking toward their Lord) (On the Day of Judgment, eyes will look upon their Lord). Since sound intellect deems corporeality impossible for Allah, and we also read in other verses of the Quran: «لاتدركه الابصار»[7] (Vision comprehends Him not), we understand that the intent of looking upon the Lord is looking upon His grace and reward on the Day of Judgment. Similarly, the intent of "the Hand of Allah" in the Ayah «یداللّه فوق ایدیهم» (The Hand of Allah is above their hands) is His power, just as we say in Persian: "So-and-so has a hand in that city or office," meaning he has power. Otherwise, Allah is not a body to have hands and feet. The Quran also states: «لیس كمثله شى ء»[8] (There is nothing like Him). Such verses have caused the deviation of simple-minded people who do not pay attention to other verses and have separated themselves from the true interpreters, namely the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام. Perhaps it is because of these dangers that the Quran itself advises us to seek refuge with Allah from the evil of Satan when reciting the Quran. «فاذا قرأت القرآن فاستعذ باللّه من الشیطان الرجیم»[9] (So when you recite the Quran, seek refuge with Allah from Satan, the rejected). Question: What is the meaning of Ta'wil (ultimate interpretation) of the Quran? Answer: The intent of Ta'wil is the goals, knowledge, and hidden secrets that become clear later. Similar to the interpretation of the dream that Prophet Joseph saw, which became clear later, or the secrets involved in Khidr and Moses breaking the ship, which became clear later. We read in narrations: The abrogating verses (nāsikh) are among the muhkamāt, and the abrogated verses (mansūkh) are among the mutashābihāt of the Quran. And the infallible Imams علیهم السلام are the clear examples of those firmly rooted in knowledge (al-rāsikhūn fī al-‘ilm).[10] Just as those who revolt against the Islamic ruler are the crooked-minded who pursue the mutashābih verses.[11] Imam Ali عليه السلام praises the rāsikhūn fī al-‘ilm in Nahj al-Balaghah (12). They are those who confess their scientific limitations and refrain from delving into matters that are unnecessary.

3) Hud, 1. 4) Az-Zumar, 23. 5) «من ردّ متشابه القرآن الى محكمه هدى الى صراط مستقم». Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 2, p. 185. 6) Al-Qiyamah, 23. 7) Al-An'am, 103. 8) Ash-Shura, 11. 9) An-Nahl, 98. 10) Tafsir Nur ath-Thaqalayn. 11) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 148. 12) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 91.

Messages

1- Not everyone understands all verses. «و اُخر متشابهات»

2- Deviant hearts are the source of corruption and discord (fitnah). «فى قلوبهم زیغ فیتّبعون ما تشابه منه ابتغاء الفتنة»

3- Fitnah (discord) is not only military and physical unrest; interpretation based on personal opinion and the distortion of the culture and meanings of the verses is also fitnah. «ابتغاء الفتنة»

4- Sometimes truth becomes a pretext for falsehood. «ابتغاء الفتنة»

5- The ultimate goal and highest purpose (ta'wil) of the divine verses are known only by Allah and those firmly rooted in knowledge (al-rāsikhūn fī al-‘ilm). «ومایعلم تأویله الا اللّه و الراسخون فى العلم»

6- The name of those firmly rooted in knowledge is mentioned alongside the sacred Name of Allah, and their status is after the divine status. «ومایعلم تأویله الا اللّه و الراسخون فى العلم»

7- Knowledge has degrees, the highest of which is familiarity with ta'wil and reaching the ultimate goal. «ومایعلم تأویله الا اللّه و الراسخون فى العلم»

8- True scholars are humble. «والراسخون فى العلم یقولون امنّا به»

9- Inclination toward falsehood prevents the establishment of knowledge. «الراسخون فى العلم» is contrasted with «فى قلوبهم زیغ».

10- We should not deny what we do not understand. «كل من عند ربّنا»

11- The revelation of muhkam and mutashābih verses is for the purpose of spiritual training. «كل من عند ربّنا»

Ayah

رَبَّنَا لَا تُزِغْ قُلُوبَنَا بَعْدَ إِذْ هَدَيْتَنَا وَهَبْ لَنَا مِن لَّدُنكَ رَحْمَةً ۚ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلْوَهَّابُ (۸)
[They say,] ‘Our Lord! Do not make our hearts swerve after You have guided us, and bestow Your mercy on us. Indeed, You are the All-munificent. (8)

Messages

1- Let us not be arrogant about our knowledge and learning; let us ask Allah for guidance. «ربّنا لاتزغ قلوبنا...»

2- The sign of true knowledge and deep grounding in knowledge is turning to Allah and seeking His aid. «الاّ الراسخون فى العلم... ربّنا لاتزغ قلوبنا»

3- The axis of guidance and misguidance is the human heart and thoughts. «لاتزغ قلوبنا»

4- Being placed on the path is not the most important thing; remaining on the path is of great importance. «بعد اذ هدیتنا...»

5- The permanent and unmerited gift and bestowal (wahbah) belongs exclusively to Him. «انّك انت الوهّاب»

Ayah

رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ جَامِعُ ٱلنَّاسِ لِيَوْمٍ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُخْلِفُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ (۹)
Our Lord! You will indeed gather mankind on a day in which there is no doubt. Indeed Allah does not break His promise.’ (9)

Key Points

Whoever believes in the Day of Resurrection—«لیوم لاریب فیه» (a Day about which there is no doubt)—becomes concerned about having a bad outcome for himself and seeks help from Allah. The source of breaking a promise is negligence, inability, fear, ignorance, or regret, none of which can be attributed to the sacred Divine Essence. «لایخلف المیعاد» (He does not break the promise).

Messages

1- Those firmly grounded in knowledge look toward the future and the Day of Resurrection. «انك جامع الناس لیوم...»

2- Attention to spiritual states in this world—«لاتزغ قلوبنا» (Do not let our hearts deviate)—and accountability for deeds in the Hereafter are signs of true scholars. «لیوم لاریب فیه»

3- The Day of Resurrection is both the Day of Gathering, when people are gathered in one place for accountability; «جامع الناس» (Gatherer of mankind), and the Day of Separation (Fasl), when, after the judgment, each person goes separately to his destination. «انّ یوم الفصل كان میقاتاً» (13)

4- Those firmly grounded in knowledge are certain of the Day of Resurrection, «لاریب فیه» (there is no doubt about it), because they are certain of Allah's fulfillment of His promise. «لایخلف المیعاد» (31) Naba' 17

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَن تُغْنِىَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمْ وَقُودُ ٱلنَّارِ (۱٠)
As for the faithless, neither their wealth nor their children shall avail them anything against Allah; it is they who will be fuel for the Fire; (10)

Key Points

Waqūd (وَقود) means combustible material (fuel). The fuel for the Hellfire consists of two things: stones and humans: «وقودها الناس و الحجارة» (14). Characteristics, thoughts, and deeds transform human identity, turning man into the fuel for the fire, and making him the agent of his own burning. No one other than Him can fulfill man's true needs, neither in this world nor in the Hereafter, and man is always in need of Him.

14) Baqarah, 24.

Messages

1- Do not be arrogant because of wealth and children. Attachment to anything other than Allah, whatever it may be, is condemned. «لن تغنى عنهم اموالهم ولا اولادهم»

2- Disbelief removes man from the orbit of humanity and lowers him to the level of material and natural objects. «اولئك هم وقود النّار»

Ayah

كَدَأْبِ ءَالِ فِرْعَوْنَ وَٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَأَخَذَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ شَدِيدُ ٱلْعِقَابِ (۱۱)
as in the case of Pharaoh’s clan and those who were before them, who denied Our signs. So Allah seized them for their sins, and Allah is severe in retribution. (11)

Messages

1- After stating general principles, providing examples is necessary. «كفروا... كدأب آل فرعون»

2- Relying on material power, wealth, and children is the custom of Pharaoh. «كدأب آل فرعون»

3- History and the experience of those who passed away are the best lessons. «كدأب آل فرعون...»

Pharaoh and the family of Pharaoh, who were champions of power and might, could not resist the wrath of Allah for a moment; therefore, do not boast about your meager wealth and power.

4- Sometimes the role of the tyrant's entourage is no less significant than the tyrant himself. «كدأب آل فرعون»

5- Sin is bad, but worse than that is the habit of sin and the spirit of sinfulness. «كدأب آل فرعون و...»

6- Similar characteristics, methods, deeds, and sins result in similar recompense and reward. «كدأب آل فرعون»

7- The cause of the destruction of nations was their sins. «فأخذهم اللّه بذنوبهم»

8- Where sin becomes part of the spirit and habit, the punishment of the Lord is severe. «كدأب... شدید العقاب»

Ayah

قُل لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ سَتُغْلَبُونَ وَتُحْشَرُونَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ ۚ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ (۱۲)
Say to the faithless, ‘You shall be overcome and mustered toward hell, and it is an evil resting place.’ (12)

Key Points

As mentioned in the commentaries Majma' al-Bayan, Kabir, and Maraghi: When the Muslims were victorious in the Battle of Badr in the second year of the Hijra, a group of Jews said: This victory is a sign of the truthfulness of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, because it is stated in the Torah: He will be victorious over His enemies. Another group of Jews said: Do not rush, perhaps he will be defeated in other battles. When the Battle of Uhud occurred in the third year and the Muslims were defeated, this group of Jews rejoiced. The Ayah was revealed, stating that you should not judge hastily; in the near future, you will be defeated. It was not long before the Muslims crushed the Jews of Banu Qurayzah and Banu Nadir in Medina, and they also eliminated the polytheists during the Conquest of Mecca.

Messages

1- Comforting the believers and threatening the disbelievers is a sign of sound leadership and faith in the goal. «قل للذین كفروا ستغلبون»

2- One of the miracles of the Quran is its truthful prophecies. «ستغلبون»

3- Truth is victorious, and disbelief is doomed to defeat. «ستغلبون»

4- Confront the enemy's rumors and propaganda. «ستغلبون»

5- Be concerned about defeats that lead to Hell (Jahannam); otherwise, the possibility of defeat exists in every war. «ستغلبون وتحشرون الى جهنّم»

Ayah

قَدْ كَانَ لَكُمْ ءَايَةٌ فِى فِئَتَيْنِ ٱلْتَقَتَا ۖ فِئَةٌ تُقَـٰتِلُ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَأُخْرَىٰ كَافِرَةٌ يَرَوْنَهُم مِّثْلَيْهِمْ رَأْىَ ٱلْعَيْنِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يُؤَيِّدُ بِنَصْرِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَعِبْرَةً لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَبْصَـٰرِ (۱۳)
There was certainly a sign for you in the two hosts that met: one host fighting in the way of Allah and the other faithless, who saw them as visibly twice as many. Allah strengthens whomever He wishes with His help. There is indeed a moral in that for those who have insight. (13)

Key Points

This Ayah relates to the Battle of Badr, where two groups, the Muslims and the disbelievers, stood against each other. In this battle, the number of Muslims was 313, of whom 77 were from the Muhajirin (Emigrants) and 236 were from the Ansar (Helpers). The standard-bearer of the Muhajirin was Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, and the standard-bearer of the Ansar was Sa'd ibn Ubadah. The Muslims had 70 camels, 2 horses, 6 coats of mail, and 8 swords, while the army of disbelief had more than 1,000 fighters and 100 cavalrymen. In that war, the Muslims, having given 22 martyrs—14 from the Muhajirin and 8 from the Ansar—were victorious over the disbelieving enemy, who suffered 70 killed and 70 captured. The Battle of Badr was not planned beforehand. The goal was to confiscate the property of the disbelievers in retaliation for the confiscation of the property of the Muslim emigrants from Mecca, which ultimately led to war and military confrontation. «فى فئتین التقتا»

Question: In this Ayah, we read that the disbelievers saw the Muslims as double their number; «یرونهم مثلیهم» (they saw them as twice their number), whereas in Ayah 44 of Surah Al-Anfal, we read that the Muslims appeared few in the eyes of the disbelievers; «یقلّلكم فى اعینهم» (He made you appear few in their eyes). How is such a thing possible?

Answer: At the beginning of the war, they saw the Muslims as few so that they would gain the courage to attack and would not seek help from the disbelievers of Mecca. However, during the battle, they saw them as numerous and lost heart. Therefore, seeing them as few and seeing them as numerous occurred in two stages and two different times, so there is no contradiction.[15]

15) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The goal of war must be Allah and the religion of Allah. «فى سبیل اللّه»

2- Muslim fighters have a single goal. «فئة تقاتل فى سبیل اللّه» (a party fighting in the way of Allah). (But the disbelieving fighters came to war, each for a different purpose.)

3- Where Allah wills, sights, perceptions, and thoughts change. With the very same eyes, they see the same group as double their number. «یرونهم مثلیهم رأى العین»

4- One of the unseen Divine aids is the creation of terror in the hearts of the enemies. «یرونهم مثلیهم... و اللّه یؤیّد»

5- The Battle of Badr showed that the Will of Allah prevails over the will of creation, and material resources alone are not the factor for victory. «یؤیّد بنصره من یشاء»

6- Although Allah helps whomever He wills; «من یشاء» (whom He wills), the condition for His help is that people rise up to support His religion.[16] «فى سبیل اللّه»

7- The purpose of relating history must be to draw lessons and admonition. «لعبرة»

8- Although events are a source of admonition and lesson for everyone; «كان لكم آیة» (there was a sign for you), drawing lessons from signs requires insight and vision. «لعبرة لأولى الأبصار»

9- Those who do not learn lessons from the Battle of Badr do not possess insight. «لعبرةلاولى الابصار»

16) In another place, He also says: «ان تنصروا اللّه ینصركم» (If you support Allah, He will support you) (Muhammad, 7).

Ayah

زُيِّنَ لِلنَّاسِ حُبُّ ٱلشَّهَوَٰتِ مِنَ ٱلنِّسَآءِ وَٱلْبَنِينَ وَٱلْقَنَـٰطِيرِ ٱلْمُقَنطَرَةِ مِنَ ٱلذَّهَبِ وَٱلْفِضَّةِ وَٱلْخَيْلِ ٱلْمُسَوَّمَةِ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ وَٱلْحَرْثِ ۗ ذَٰلِكَ مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَٱللَّهُ عِندَهُۥ حُسْنُ ٱلْمَـَٔابِ (۱٤)
The love of [worldly] allures, including women and children, accumulated piles of gold and silver, horses of mark, livestock, and farms has been made to seem decorous to mankind. Those are the wares of the life of this world, but the goodness of one’s ultimate destination lies near Allah. (14)

Key Points

Qanāṭīr is the plural of qinṭār, meaning great wealth. The inclusion of the word muqanṭarah immediately following it is for greater emphasis, similar to saying ālāf wa ulūf (thousands upon thousands).

Khayl is used to mean both horses and horsemen. Musawwamah means marked or branded. Horses that acquired special distinction due to their physical beauty or the training they received were called khayl musawwamah.

The beautification (glamorization) of the world in the eyes of man sometimes occurs through personal delusions, sometimes by Satan, and sometimes by flattering associates. Through personal delusions: «یحسبون انّهم یُحسِنون صُنعاً»(17) and «فرَآه حسَناً»(18). By Satan: «زُیّن لهم الشیطان اعمالهم»(19). And by flattering associates: «زُیّن لفرعون سوء عمَله»(20).

The examples of the world's allure mentioned in the Ayah are specific to the time of its revelation and can have new manifestations in every era. Gold and silver are metaphors for wealth accumulation, and horses are metaphors for mounts and means of transportation.

Question: Since Allah has accepted that wealth and children are adornments; «المال و البنون زینة الحیوة الدنیا»(21), why is this Ayah presented in a tone of criticism?

Answer: Something being an adornment is different from being attached to it. The criticism in this Ayah is directed at intense attachment, which is referred to as ḥubb ash-shahawāt (love of desires).

The word banīn (sons) also includes daughters, similar to when we say: "Respected passersby should use the sidewalk," which includes female passersby.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, citing «حبّ الشهوات من النساء...», said: People do not derive pleasure from anything in this world or the Hereafter more than women... The inhabitants of Paradise also desire marriage more than food and drink.(22)

This narration explains the reason for the precedence of an-nisā’ (women) over the other items.

17) Kahf, 104.

18) Fatir, 8.

19) An‘am, 43.

20) Ghafir, 37.

21) Kahf, 46.

22) Kafi, Vol. 5, p. 321.

Messages

1- A natural inclination toward material things exists in the nature of every human being. What is dangerous is being deceived by its adornments and allure, and the failure to control one's attachments. «زُیّن للنّاس...»

2- The allure of the world is for ordinary people, not for the wise.(23) «زُیّن للناس»

3- Excessive attachment to wives and children, more than anything else, causes man's deep attachment to the world. «حبّ الشهوات من النساء والبنین»

4- We must be careful that the adornments and allure of the world are a prelude to heedlessness regarding the Hereafter. «زُیّن للناس... واللّه عنده حسن المآب»

5- One of the factors leading to a bad end is infatuation and attachment to the world, because the good return is only with Allah. «زُیّن للناس... واللّه عنده حسن المآب»

6- One of the methods of moral training is the belittling of material things and the declaration of the greatness of spiritual matters. «ذلك متاع الحیوة الدنیا واللّه عنده حسن المآب»

7- The best way to control destructive attachments is to compare them with the eternal favors of Allah. «واللّه عنده حسن المآب»

23) In the view of Pharaoh's wife, who is a wise figure in history, his palace and gold are worthless. «نجّنى من فرعون و عمله». Tahrim, 11.

Ayah

۞ قُلْ أَؤُنَبِّئُكُم بِخَيْرٍ مِّن ذَٰلِكُمْ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا وَأَزْوَٰجٌ مُّطَهَّرَةٌ وَرِضْوَٰنٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ (۱٥)
Say, ‘Shall I inform you of something better than that? For those who are Godwary there will be gardens near their Lord, with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], and chaste mates, and Allah’s pleasure.’ And Allah watches His servants. (15)

Key Points

In the last sentence of the previous Ayah, we read that the good outcome is with Allah; «واللّه عنده حسن المآب» This Ayah portrays a picture of the Paradise for the righteous.

Messages

1- When inviting people to the truth, we should invite them to compare this world and the Hereafter. «قل أؤنبّئكم بخیر...»

2- Where is the permanence and stability of Paradise and the satisfaction of Allah, and where are the temporary and unstable pleasures of this world? «ذلك متاع الحیوة الدنیا... قل أونبّئكم»

3- Pious individuals are not captivated by the glitter and glamour of the world. The term «متّقین» (the pious) in this Ayah is used in contrast to «ناس» (people) in the previous Ayah.

4- The criterion for attaining the blessings of the Hereafter is piety (Taqwa). «للذین اتقوا»

5- Encouragement must be consistent with natural and innate desires. «انهار، ازواج، خالدین»

6- Chastity and purity are the highest values. «أزواج مطهرة»

7- The pleasures of the pious are not limited only to material delights; Divine satisfaction is the highest spiritual pleasure. «رضوان من اللّه»

8- We should minimize claims of piety; Allah is All-Seeing and All-Knowing. «واللّه بصیر بالعباد»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ (۱٦)
Those who say, ‘Our Lord! Indeed, we have faith. So forgive us our sins, and save us from the punishment of the Fire.’ (16)

Messages

1- The pious are constantly engaged in repentance and supplication. «یقولون»

2- Faith is the basis for Divine forgiveness. «آمنّا فاغفرلنا»

3- Forgiveness is among the aspects of Lordship (Rububiyyah) and a necessity for nurturing (Tarbiyah). «ربّنا... فاغفرلنا»

4- Fear of Allah’s wrath and punishment is among the signs of piety. «قنا عذاب النار»

5- Do not rely on your good deeds. Even though you are people of piety, you must still fear Divine wrath. «قنا عذاب النار»

Ayah

ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ وَٱلصَّـٰدِقِينَ وَٱلْقَـٰنِتِينَ وَٱلْمُنفِقِينَ وَٱلْمُسْتَغْفِرِينَ بِٱلْأَسْحَارِ (۱۷)
[They are] patient and truthful, obedient and charitable, and they plead for [Allah’s] forgiveness at dawns. (17)

Key Points

Individuals possessing piety (Taqwa) have the following characteristics: 1- Patience (Sabr) in the face of difficulties, patience in abandoning sins, and patience in performing obligations. 2- Truthfulness (Sadaqah) in speech and conduct. 3- Humility and lowliness in carrying out commands, without arrogance or self-superiority. 4- Spending (Infaq) from whatever Allah has provided for them. 5- Supplications (Munajat) at dawn and seeking forgiveness from Allah. Indeed! Dawn (Sahar) is the most suitable time for supplication [24]. In some narrations, it is stated: If someone continuously seeks forgiveness (Istighfar) seventy times in the Qunut of the Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl) for one year, he will be included under this Ayah [25]. Abi Basir asked Imam Sadiq عليه السلام concerning "المستغفرین بالسحار" (those who seek forgiveness at dawn), and the Imam replied: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله used to seek forgiveness seventy times in the Witr prayer [26]. Mufaddal ibn Umar says: I said to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: My Night Prayer is missed, and I make it up (Qada) after the Morning Prayer. The Imam replied: There is no problem, but do not reveal it to your family, because they might consider it a Sunnah (established practice), and in that case, it would invalidate the practice based on the saying of Allah: "المستغفرین بالاسحار" [27].

24) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) at dawn is prayer at that time. Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 25) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan; Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 309. 26) Tahdhib al-Ahkam, Vol. 2, p. 120. 27) Mustadrak, Hadith 3259.

Messages

1- Both attending to creation and worshipping the Creator. "والمنفقین و المستغفرین بالاسحار"

2- Piety (Taqwa) does not mean isolation, ignorance, or seclusion; the pious person (Muttaqi) must be comprehensive in perfections. "الصابرین والصادقین و..."

Ayah

شَهِدَ ٱللَّهُ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَأُو۟لُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمِ قَآئِمًۢا بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۱۸)
Allah, maintainer of justice, the Almighty and the All-wise, besides whom there is no god, bears witness that there is no god except Him, and [so do] the angels and those who possess knowledge. (18)

Key Points

Allah, by creating a unified system in existence, testifies to the oneness of His Essence. That is, the harmony and order present in creation all bear witness to the sovereignty of a single Power over existence, just as we read in Du'a al-Sabah: «یا مَن دلّ على ذاته بذاته» (O He Who proved His Essence by His Essence). The sun came as the proof of the sun; if you need proof, do not turn your face away from it. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of "ulul-'ilm" (those endowed with knowledge) in the Ayah refers to the Prophets and the Successors (Awsiya'). [28] Wherever there is multiplicity, there is also limitation. An infinite house cannot be more than one. If we say that a certain lamp has infinite light, there remains no place for a second lamp. God is infinite, because if He had an end, He would encounter non-existence, and nothing infinite is capable of multiplicity. Therefore, Allah is not capable of multiplicity, and He is Unique.

28) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Practical testimony is the best type of testimony. The harmony among creations is the best example of bearing witness to the Oneness of Allah. «شهد اللّه انّه لااله الاّ هو»

2- The path to faith in Allah is knowledge, and true knowledge acquaints man with the source of existence. «واولوا العلم»

3- Scholars are placed alongside the angels. «والملائكة واولوا العلم»

4- Divine Justice is mentioned alongside Monotheism (Tawhid). Contrary to other powerful entities who resort to oppression whenever they feel unique and without rival or partner, He is the Unique One Who upholds justice. «لااله الاّ اللّه... قائماً بالقسط»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلدِّينَ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمُ ۗ وَمَا ٱخْتَلَفَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعِلْمُ بَغْيًۢا بَيْنَهُمْ ۗ وَمَن يَكْفُرْ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۱۹)
Indeed, with Allah religion is Islam, and those who were given the Book did not differ except after knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves. And whoever denies Allah’s signs [should know that] Allah is swift at reckoning. (19)

Messages

1- Faith in the Oneness, Justice, Might, and Wisdom of Allah (which was mentioned in the previous Ayah) is the basis for human submission. «ان الدّین عند اللّه الاسلام»

2- The requirement of submission before Allah is the acceptance of Islam as the final divine religion. «ان الدّین عند اللّه الاسلام»

3- Transgression beyond the boundaries of truth causes the emergence of disagreement. «وما اختلف الّذین... بغیاً»

4- The source of many religious disagreements is jealousies and injustices, not ignorance or unawareness. «من بعد ما جائهم العلم»

5- Envy is the basis for disbelief. «بغیاً... و من كفر»

6- The Book and knowledge alone do not cause salvation. «اوتوا الكتاب، جائهم العلم، من یكفر»

7- Those who knowingly create disagreement will soon face the consequences. «سریع الحساب»

Ayah

فَإِنْ حَآجُّوكَ فَقُلْ أَسْلَمْتُ وَجْهِىَ لِلَّهِ وَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَنِ ۗ وَقُل لِّلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْأُمِّيِّـۧنَ ءَأَسْلَمْتُمْ ۚ فَإِنْ أَسْلَمُوا۟ فَقَدِ ٱهْتَدَوا۟ ۖ وَّإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّمَا عَلَيْكَ ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ (۲٠)
If they argue with you, say, ‘I have submitted my will to Allah, and [so has] he who follows me.’ And say to those who were given the Book and the uninstructed ones, ‘Do you submit?’ If they submit, they will certainly be guided; but if they turn away, then your duty is only to communicate, and Allah watches His servants. (20)

Key Points

The saying, "A word that comes from the heart will inevitably settle in the heart," is not a universal rule. Because the Prophet's speech came from his heart, yet it did not settle in the hearts of the disbelievers (Kuffar). Although the Prophet stated from the depth of his heart: «اَسلمتُ وجهى» (I have submitted my whole self), meaning, "I am completely submitted to Allah," yet a group turned away; «وان تولّوا». The main axis of religion is Islam, meaning submission to the Truth (Haqq), which has been mentioned four times in the previous Ayah and this Ayah: «الاسلام، اسلمت، اسلمتم، اسلموا».

Messages

1- Dispute and argumentation (muhajjah) are characteristics of the opponents of the Prophets. «فان حاجّوك»

2- Conveying the message (risalah) and providing evidence is permissible, but contention with stubborn individuals is forbidden. «فان حاجّوك فقل اسلمتُ»

3- Put an end to fruitless and futile arguments. «فان حاجّوك فقل اسلمتُ»

4- In discussions, we should clearly state our position and the position of our followers. «فان حاجّوك فقل اسلمتُ... ومن اتبعن»

5- The true followers of the Prophet are those who are submitted to Allah. «اسلمتُ وجهى و من اتبعن»

6- What protects a person against malicious discussions is connection to Allah. «اسلمت وجهى للّه»

7- The Prophets are devoted to Allah with their whole being, with love and enthusiasm. «وجهى للّه»

8- The leader's attention must be directed both toward the educated people and the common folk. «اوتوا الكتاب و الامیّین»

9- Heavenly Books are valuable cultural documents for human societies. In the Ayah, people are divided into two categories: the educated (or people of the Book) and the common folk. «اوتوا الكتاب و الامیّین»

10- True guidance is achieved through submission to Allah. «فان اسلموا فقد اهتدوا»

11- Man is free to choose his path, not compelled. «فان اسلموا... و ان تولّوا...»

12- Knowledge and the Book alone are not sufficient; there may be knowledge, but no submission. «اوتوا الكتاب... وان تولّوا»

13- We are commissioned to perform our duty, not to guarantee the result. «انمّا علیك البلاغ»

14- Allah completes the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah) with people through the Prophets. «فانما علیك البلاغ»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْقِسْطِ مِنَ ٱلنَّاسِ فَبَشِّرْهُم بِعَذَابٍ أَلِيمٍ (۲۱)
Those who deny Allah’s signs and kill the prophets unjustly and kill those who call for justice from among the people, inform them of a painful punishment. (21)

Key Points

We read in Tafsir Kabir, Majma' al-Bayan, and Qurtubi: The Children of Israel, early in the day and within a single hour, martyred forty-three divine prophets and one hundred and twelve individuals who enjoined good (Amiran bi-Ma'ruf). Of course, it is obvious that during the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, there was no group that would martyr prophets. However, since the living were pleased with the actions of their ancestors, Allah, in this Ayah, criticizes and threatens those who are partners in the crime of their ancestors due to their inner satisfaction, using the address «بشّرهم» (give them glad tidings).

Question: One of the conditions for the obligation of enjoining good and forbidding evil (Amr bi-Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'an al-Munkar) is that there should be no danger involved. However, in this Ayah, those who stood firm to forbid evil, even to the point of death, are praised. What is the reason?

Answer: Firstly: The conditions of individuals and the type of good and evil differ. Sometimes the evil (Munkar) is the rule of Yazid, which Imam Husayn عليه السلام went to Karbala to forbid and was martyred for. He said: My goal in this movement and uprising is to enjoin good and forbid evil. But sometimes the evil is not of this magnitude; rather, it is a sin where one must compare the danger and corruption of the sin with the loss of wealth, life, and honor, and act according to importance and priority. Secondly: Perhaps those who are praised in this Ayah are those who did not themselves anticipate martyrdom, but the oppressors martyred them.

Messages

1- Dangerous actions, such as the murder and slaughter of prophets, stem from deviant and blasphemous beliefs. «یكفرون بآیات اللّه ویقتلون النبیین...»

2- Under certain circumstances, declaring the truth is necessary, even if it comes at the cost of the martyrdom of prophets and saints (Awliya).

3- Enemies resort to killing the prophets in order to conceal the truth. «یقتلون النبیّین»

4- Sometimes tyrants (Taghuts), in order to kill the wise, make their actions appear rightful through propaganda, rumors, and justifications. «بغیر حقّ»

5- The names of those who invite to justice, and those who enjoin good and forbid evil (Amirin bi-Ma'ruf wa Nahiyan 'an al-Munkar), are mentioned in the same rank as the prophets. Therefore, the punishment for their murderers is like that of the murderers of the prophets. «فبشرهم بعذاب الیم»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ ٱلَّذِينَ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَـٰلُهُمْ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۲۲)
They are the ones whose works have failed in this world and the Hereafter, and they will have no helpers. (22)

Messages

1- Sometimes one deviation nullifies all of a person's deeds. «حبطت اعمالهم»

2- For sins such as killing the prophets, there will be no intercession. «ما لهم من ناصرین»

Ayah

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ نَصِيبًا مِّنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ يُدْعَوْنَ إِلَىٰ كِتَـٰبِ ٱللَّهِ لِيَحْكُمَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ يَتَوَلَّىٰ فَرِيقٌ مِّنْهُمْ وَهُم مُّعْرِضُونَ (۲۳)
Have you not regarded those who were given a share of the Book, who are summoned to the Book of Allah in order that it may judge between them, whereat a part of them refuse to comply and they are disregardful? (23)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir) that a man and a woman from the Jews committed adultery while being married (having spouses). They were supposed to be stoned according to the law of the Torah, but because they were from the nobility (ashraf), they tried to escape the execution of the Torah's command. They came to the Prophet of Islam so that He might judge. The Prophet also commanded stoning and said: The ruling of Islam is the same as the ruling of the Torah. They denied the ruling of the Torah. Ibn Suriya, who was one of the Jewish scholars, was summoned from Fadak to Medina to read the Torah. Since Ibn Suriya was aware of the incident, while reading the verses of the Torah, he placed his hand over certain phrases so that the verse regarding stoning would not be seen. Abdullah ibn Salam, who was one of the Jewish scholars that day and was present in that session, understood the matter and exposed the issue. [29] This Ayah is a warning to Muslims that they should not, like the Jews, turn away from the Divine command when implementing the law of the Quran, nor should they discriminate among people.

29) The ruling for the execution and stoning of the adulterer is mentioned in the Book of Deuteronomy, Chapter 22, and the Book of Leviticus, Chapter 20.

Messages

1- The book of law, judgment, and ruling is the Heavenly Book. «یُدعَون الى كتاب اللّه لیحكم»

2- Islam is a religion of fairness and respect for others. It invites the scholars of other religions to make their own book the judge. «یُدعَون الى كتاب اللّه لیحكم»

3- Not all scholars of the People of the Book were bad. «یتولّى فریق منهم»

4- More dangerous than turning away is the intention to reject and show obstinacy. «یتولّى... معرضون»

5- The law must be implemented equally for all individuals. (Considering the occasion of revelation)

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا۟ لَن تَمَسَّنَا ٱلنَّارُ إِلَّآ أَيَّامًا مَّعْدُودَٰتٍ ۖ وَغَرَّهُمْ فِى دِينِهِم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۲٤)
That is because they say, ‘The Fire shall not touch us except for a number of days,’ and they have been misled in their religion by what they used to fabricate. (24)

Key Points

In the Quran, the empty slogans and false fantasies of the Jews have been narrated repeatedly. They used to say: We are the superior race and the beloved of Allah, and in the Hereafter, no punishment will reach us except for those forty days when our ancestors worshipped the calf. And these very fantasies caused their arrogance and deviation. Today's Israel also believes in its racial superiority and refrains from no injustice in order to achieve it.

Messages

1- The source of turning away (aversion) is superstitious and baseless beliefs. «هم معرضون ذلك بانّهم...»

2- Self-superiority, whether based on religion or based on race, is forbidden and condemned. «لن تمسّنا النار»

3- The feeling of security and immunity against punishment is a cause of misguidance. «لن تمسّنا النار»

4- The Jews accepted the Hereafter and Hellfire and confessed to their sinfulness. «لن تمسّنا النار الاّ ایّاماً معدودة»

5- All people are equal in the Divine court. «لن تمسّنا النار... غرّهم فى دینهم»

Ayah

فَكَيْفَ إِذَا جَمَعْنَـٰهُمْ لِيَوْمٍ لَّا رَيْبَ فِيهِ وَوُفِّيَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۲٥)
But how will it be [with them] when We gather them on a day in which there is no doubt, and every soul shall be recompensed fully for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged? (25)

Messages

1- Be mindful of the Day of Resurrection and abandon superstitions. «لن تمسّنا النار... فكیف اذا جمعنا»

2- Punishment and reward are based on deeds, not wishes or assumptions. «لن تمسّنا النار... وفیّت كل نفس ما كسبت»

3- No deed is erased or destroyed; rather, it is returned to its doer. «وفیّت... ما كسبت»

4- The Divine court is established based on justice, and everyone receives their recompense. «وفیّت كل نفس... هم لا یظلمون»

Ayah

قُلِ ٱللَّهُمَّ مَـٰلِكَ ٱلْمُلْكِ تُؤْتِى ٱلْمُلْكَ مَن تَشَآءُ وَتَنزِعُ ٱلْمُلْكَ مِمَّن تَشَآءُ وَتُعِزُّ مَن تَشَآءُ وَتُذِلُّ مَن تَشَآءُ ۖ بِيَدِكَ ٱلْخَيْرُ ۖ إِنَّكَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲٦)
Say, ‘O Allah, Master of all sovereignty! You give sovereignty to whomever You wish, and strip of sovereignty whomever You wish; You make mighty whomever You wish, and You degrade whomever You wish; all choice is in Your hand. Indeed You have power over all things.’ (26)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries (Tafasir) that after the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله conquered Mecca, He promised the Muslims the conquest of Iran and Rome. At that time, the hypocrites looked at each other in astonishment, and this Ayah was revealed. A group of commentators (Mufassirun) believe the Ayah was revealed during the digging of the Trench (Khandaq). At that time, when the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله struck the stone with a pickaxe and a spark arose from it, He said: "I received the news of the conquest of the palaces of Mada'in and Yemen from Gabriel in these sparks." When the hypocrites heard this, they smiled mockingly, and this Ayah was revealed. What is mentioned in this Ayah regarding the granting of honor (Izzah) and humiliation (Dhull) by Allah is according to His laws and Sunnah (way), and Allah does not grant honor or humiliate anyone without reason or cause. For example, we read in narrations (Riwayat): "Whoever shows humility and modesty for the sake of Allah, Allah honors him, and whoever acts arrogantly, Allah humiliates him." (30) Therefore, honor and humiliation belong to Allah, but creating the grounds for them is in our own hands.

30) Bihar, Vol. 16, p. 265.

Messages

1- Allah is the true Owner of all governments. Sovereignty (Mulk) for anyone other than Allah is temporary and limited. «مالك الملك» (Owner of Sovereignty)

2- Since He is the Owner, others are merely trustees and must act according to the will of the original Owner. «مالك الملك»

3- If man is not the owner of sovereignty, why should he become arrogant upon possessing it or despair upon losing it? «مالك الملك»

4- Allah grants governance to whomever is worthy and deserving, just as He bestowed it upon Solomon, Joseph, Talut, and Dhul-Qarnayn. «تؤتى الملك من تشاء» (You give sovereignty to whom You will)

5- Governance and rule bring attachment. Naza' (نزع) means to pull out or strip away, indicating a type of attachment.

6- Honor and humiliation are in the hand of Allah; we should not expect honor from others. (31) «تعزّ من تشاء و تذلّ من تشاء» (You honor whom You will and You humiliate whom You will)

7- Monotheism (Tawhid) in supplication (Du'a) and worship is a necessity. (32) «بیدك الخیر» (In Your hand is all good)

8- Whatever comes from Him, whether giving or taking, is all good, even if we do not understand its philosophy in our hasty judgments. «بیدك الخیر»

9- The source of evils is helplessness and inability. Nothing but good proceeds from One who has power over everything. «بیدك الخیر انك على كل شى ء قدیر» (In Your hand is all good. Indeed, You are over all things competent)

31) The Quran severely criticizes those who seek honor from others besides Allah, saying: «أیبتغون عندهم العزّة فانّ العزّة للّه جمیعاً» (Do they seek honor with them? But indeed, all honor belongs to Allah.) An-Nisa, 139.

32) In the Munajat Sha'baniyyah we read: «الهى بیدك لابید غیرك زیادتى و نقصى» (My God, in Your hand, not in the hand of another, is my increase and my decrease).

Ayah

تُولِجُ ٱلَّيْلَ فِى ٱلنَّهَارِ وَتُولِجُ ٱلنَّهَارَ فِى ٱلَّيْلِ ۖ وَتُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَتُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ ۖ وَتَرْزُقُ مَن تَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۲۷)
‘You make the night pass into the day and You make the day pass into the night. You bring forth the living from the dead and You bring forth the dead from the living, and You provide for whomever You wish without any reckoning.’ (27)

Key Points

This Ayah and the preceding Ayah have mentioned the signs of Allah's power twelve times in order to cultivate the spirit of Monotheism (Tawhid) within man. The meaning of inserting the night into the day and the day into the night is either the increase and decrease in the length of the hours of night and day during different seasons, or the gradual sunset and sunrise. Of course, the first meaning is clearer. An example of Allah's demonstration of power in bringing forth the living from the dead is the creation of a living cell from lifeless food materials. Sometimes, believing and living-hearted children emerge from dead-hearted disbelievers, and disbelieving and dead-hearted children emerge from living-hearted believers (33), which is one of the manifestations of the Ayah. The meaning of provision without measure (رزق بى حساب) is abundant and countless provision, not that its accounting is beyond His grasp. And it is possible that the meaning is that His provision is outside your calculations, and He provides from a way you do not expect. The difference among people in provision is one of the Divine wisdoms, so that people remain dependent on one another, form a social life, and the qualities of generosity, altruism (Ithar), patience (Sabr), and contentment (Qana'ah) may flourish in them. Because if everyone were equal, these qualities would not flourish. But if one person possesses a blessing and another possesses less or a different blessing, people gather together to compensate for the deficiencies, and the qualities of cooperation and generosity flourish on the part of the possessor of the blessing, while the qualities of contentment, chastity ('Iffah), and patience flourish on the part of the needy.

33) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan, Vol. 2, p. 728.

Messages

1- The changes of night and day and the emergence of seasons are among the blessings and Divine favors. «بیدك الخیر... تولج اللیل فى النهار»

2- In addition to creation, every kind of change and management (Tadbir) is in His hand. «تولج اللیل فى النهار»

3- Allah's power is not limited. He brings forth the living from the dead and the dead from the living. «تخرج الحىّ من المیّت و تخرج المیّت من الحىّ»

4- The source of Divine favor is endless. Provision without measure (رزق بى حساب) means there is no limitation on the source of provision. «ترزق من تشاء بغیر حساب»

Ayah

لَّا يَتَّخِذِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ أَوْلِيَآءَ مِن دُونِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ ۖ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ فِى شَىْءٍ إِلَّآ أَن تَتَّقُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ تُقَىٰةً ۗ وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسَهُۥ ۗ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۲۸)
The faithful should not take the faithless for allies instead of the faithful, and Allah will have nothing to do with those who do that, except when you are wary of them, out of caution. Allah warns you to beware of [disobeying] Him, and toward Allah is the return. (28)

Key Points

Considering the infinite Divine power mentioned in the previous verses, there is no room for accepting the dominance of the disbelievers. «بیدك الخیر، تولج اللیل، لایتخذ...» This verse outlines the characteristics of foreign policy, the method of dealing with disbelievers, the conditions for Taqiyyah (dissimulation), and the prevention of the misuse of Taqiyyah. Taqiyyah means concealing the true belief out of fear of harm from opponents, and abandoning struggle against them in order to avoid a greater loss or danger. Taqiyyah is sometimes obligatory and sometimes forbidden (haram). The Quran speaks about Taqiyyah in verse 106 of Surah An-Nahl, the occasion of revelation for which concerns Ammar ibn Yasir.

Messages

1- The acceptance of the guardianship (wilayah) of disbelievers by believers is forbidden. «لایتخذ المؤمنون الكافرین اولیاء» (If the Muslims of the world had acted solely upon this verse, the situation of Islamic countries would not be as it is now.)

2- In an Islamic society, faith (Iman) is the primary condition for management and guardianship. «لایتّخذ المؤمنون الكافرین اولیاء»

3- Political relations must not lead to accepting dominance or forming a heartfelt bond with the disbelievers. «لایتخذ المؤمنون الكافرین اولیاء»

4- Relations or the severance of relations must be based on thought and belief, not on familial, tribal, or racial ties. «المؤمنین، الكافرین»

5- Whoever turns to the disbelievers, Allah abandons him to his own devices and deprives him of His unseen aids. «و من یفعل ذلك فلیس من اللّه فى شى ء»

6- Outward relations with disbelievers, for the purpose of achieving higher goals, are permissible in certain cases. «الا ان تتقوا منهم تقیة»

7- Taqiyyah is for the preservation of religion. Beware lest you be drawn to the disbelievers under the pretext of Taqiyyah and misuse the name of Taqiyyah. «یحذّركم اللّه نفسه»

8- In cases where the foundation of religion is in danger, everything must be sacrificed, and one must fear only God (Allah). «یحذّركم اللّه نفسه»

9- The remembrance of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the best factor for piety (Taqwa). «یحذّركم اللّه نفسه و الى اللّه المصیر»

10- Beware lest you accept the dominance of the disbelievers for the sake of a few days of worldly enjoyment and comfort, for the return of all of you is to Him. «و الى اللّه المصیر»

Ayah

قُلْ إِن تُخْفُوا۟ مَا فِى صُدُورِكُمْ أَوْ تُبْدُوهُ يَعْلَمْهُ ٱللَّهُ ۗ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۲۹)
Say, ‘Whether you hide what is in your hearts or disclose it, Allah knows it, and He knows whatever there is in the heavens and whatever there is in the earth; and Allah has power over all things.’ (29)

Key Points

The appearance of this Ayah following the Ayah of Taqiyyah (dissimulation) perhaps indicates that you should not establish secret ties with the disbelievers in the name of Taqiyyah; rather, know that Allah is aware of your thoughts and intentions.

Messages

1- Warn those who seek connection with the disbelievers in the name of Taqiyyah. «قل ان تخفوا»

2- The human chest is the repository of his secrets. «تخفوا ما فى صدوركم»

3- Allah’s knowledge of the manifest and the hidden, of the earth and the heavens, is uniform. «تخفوا... تبدوا... یعلمه اللّه»

4- Attention to Allah’s knowledge causes the religious conscience to awaken and prevents sin and its justification. «یعلمه اللّه»

5- What can be hidden from a God Who is aware of the secrets of all the heavens? «یعلم ما فى السموات»

6- The absolute power of Allah is the guarantee supporting His threat against opponents. «ان تخفوا... و اللّه على كل شى قدیر»

Ayah

يَوْمَ تَجِدُ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا عَمِلَتْ مِنْ خَيْرٍ مُّحْضَرًا وَمَا عَمِلَتْ مِن سُوٓءٍ تَوَدُّ لَوْ أَنَّ بَيْنَهَا وَبَيْنَهُۥٓ أَمَدًۢا بَعِيدًا ۗ وَيُحَذِّرُكُمُ ٱللَّهُ نَفْسَهُۥ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ رَءُوفٌۢ بِٱلْعِبَادِ (۳٠)
The day when every soul will find present whatever good it has done; and as for the evil, it has done, it will wish there were a far distance between it and itself. Allah warns you to beware of [disobeying] Him, and Allah is most kind to [His] servants. (30)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the knowledge and power of Allah. This Ayah presents an example of Divine knowledge and power. This Ayah is similar to Ayah 49 of Surah Al-Kahf, which states: On the Day of Resurrection, people will find all their deeds present before them. It is mentioned in Tafsir Burhan that Imam Sajjad عليه السلام used to recite this Ayah for the people every Friday during his sermon in the Prophet's Mosque (Masjid an-Nabawi).

Messages

1- Human deeds are not erased; they will be present before him on the Day of Resurrection. «محضرا»

2- Belief in the presence of deeds on the Day of Resurrection prevents sin. «محضرا»

3- Sinners will be ashamed of their deeds on the Day of Resurrection, but what good will that do? «تودّ لو انّ بینها»

4- Many deeds that a person desires in this world will become repulsive to him on the Day of Resurrection. «تودّ لو انّ بینها و بینه أمداً بعيداً»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, regret is useless, and wishes are unattainable. The word (لو - law) is used for unattainable wishes. «لو انّ بینها و بینه أمداً بعيداً»

6- Fear of Allah prevents sin. «یحذّركم اللّه»

7- The source of Divine warnings is His love and compassion. «یحذّركم اللّه نفسه واللّه رؤف بالعباد»

8- Fear and hope together play an educational role. Hope alone leads to arrogance, and fear alone leads to despair. «یحذّركم اللّه نفسه... واللّه رؤف بالعباد»

9- The grace and compassion of Allah encompass all His servants. «رؤف بالعباد»

Ayah

قُلْ إِن كُنتُمْ تُحِبُّونَ ٱللَّهَ فَٱتَّبِعُونِى يُحْبِبْكُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ذُنُوبَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۳۱)
Say, ‘If you love Allah, then follow me; Allah will love you and forgive you your sins, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (31)

Key Points

Regarding the status of the Prophet and the necessity of obeying him, we read in the Quran: «مَن یطع الرّسول فقد اطاع اللّه»[34] Whoever obeys the Prophet has certainly obeyed Allah. And in another place: «انّ الّذین یبایعونك انّما یبایعون اللّه»[35] Allegiance to you is allegiance to Allah. A person said to Imam Sadiq عليه السلام: May my life be sacrificed for you, we name our children after you and your fathers. Is this beneficial for us? The Imam replied: Is religion anything other than love? Allah has said: «ان كنتم تحبّون اللّه فاتّبعونى»[36]

34) An-Nisa, 80. 35) Al-Fath, 10. 36) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 167.

Messages

1- Every claim is proven by action.[37] «ان كنتم تحبّون اللّه فاتّبعونى»

2- The prerequisite for faith in Allah is following the friends (Awliya) of Allah. «فاتّبعونى» (Yes, Islam minus true spirituality is condemned.)

3- Whoever is weak in action, the foundation of his love is, in reality, weak. «تحبّون... فاتّبعونى»

4- In the path of reforming society, we should utilize emotions. «ان كنتم تحبّون اللّه»

5- The action, speech, and silence of the Messenger of Allah are authoritative (Hujjah) and worthy of being followed. «فاتبعونى»

6- The Prophet is infallible (Ma'sum), because an absolute and unquestioning command to follow someone who is not infallible is not wise. «قل... فاتبعونى»

7- If you want Allah to love you, you must follow the Sunnah (tradition) of the Messenger of Allah. «فاتّبعونى یحببكم اللّه»

8- A person can reach a status where his satisfaction is the satisfaction of Allah, and following him is following Allah. «فاتّبعونى یحببكم اللّه»

9- The advantage of the Divine religion over human laws is the existence of the element of love, a practical role model, and the competence of the Lawgiver. «تحبّون... فاتبعونى یحببكم اللّه»

10- The best rewards are spiritual rewards. Being loved by Allah and receiving forgiveness are the best rewards for the believers. «یحببكم اللّه»

11- A sign of friendship (love) is covering up faults and dealing with pardon and mercy. «یحببكم... یغفر لكم»

12- Good deeds lead to the forgiveness of sins. «فاتبعونى... یغفرلكم ذنوبكم»

13- Obedience and following the Prophet result in receiving Divine pardon. «فاتّبعونى... یغفر لكم»

37) Work is achieved through effort, not mere claim. «لیس للانسان الاّ ما سعى»

Ayah

قُلْ أَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ ۖ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۳۲)
Say, ‘Obey Allah and the Apostle.’ But if they turn away, indeed Allah does not like the faithless. (32)

Key Points

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, besides the divine verses, had governmental instructions. Instructions that would change according to the time, place, individuals, and circumstances. Of course, those were also under the light of divine revelation, but they were not mentioned in the Quran. And the meaning of obedience to the Messenger alongside obedience to Allah is acting upon those very prophetic instructions.

Messages

1- The behavior, speech, and actions of the Prophet are authoritative (Hujjah), because obedience to the Prophet is alongside obedience to Allah. «اطیعوا اللّه و الرسول»

2- Becoming beloved or detested by the Lord is in our own hands. «فان تولّوا فانّ اللّه لا یحبّ الكافرین»

3- Disobeying the command of the Prophet is equivalent to disbelief (Kufr). «انّ اللّه لایحبّ الكافرین»

Ayah

۞ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰٓ ءَادَمَ وَنُوحًا وَءَالَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَءَالَ عِمْرَٰنَ عَلَى ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۳۳)
Indeed Allah chose Adam and Noah, and the progeny of Abraham and the progeny of Imran above all the nations; (33)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the command to obey the Messenger was issued. This Ayah considers the reason for this [selection] to be the choice of the Wise God (Allah), based on their superiority over other people in terms of perfections (excellences). The selection of the prophets is through revelation (Wahy), knowledge, faith, and infallibility ('Ismah). (38) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding "انّ اللّه اصطفى... ذریّة...": We are among those selected and the remainder of that progeny ('Itrah). (39)

38) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 117. 39) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 168.

Messages

1- Humans are not all on the same level, and the Wise God (Allah) has chosen some to bear the heavy responsibility of the message (Risalah). "انّ اللّه اصطفى"

2- The first human was a Prophet of Allah so that mankind would never remain without guidance. "اصطفى آدم"

3- The process of prophethood (Bi'that) has continued throughout human history. "اصطفى آدم و نوحاًو..."

4- The mission (Risalah) of some prophets was universal. "على العالَمین"

Ayah

ذُرِّيَّةًۢ بَعْضُهَا مِنۢ بَعْضٍ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۳٤)
some of them are descendants of the others, and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (34)

Messages

1- Throughout history, the prophetic lineage has been from the generation of the pure. «اصطفى... على العالمین ذریّة...»

2- Some characteristics and perfections are transferred through inheritance. «بعضها من بعض»

3- Allah even supervises the speech and conduct of His chosen ones. «اصطفى... سمیع علیم»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَتِ ٱمْرَأَتُ عِمْرَٰنَ رَبِّ إِنِّى نَذَرْتُ لَكَ مَا فِى بَطْنِى مُحَرَّرًا فَتَقَبَّلْ مِنِّىٓ ۖ إِنَّكَ أَنتَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (۳٥)
When the wife of Imran said, ‘My Lord, I dedicate to You in consecration what is in my belly. Accept it from me; indeed You are the All-hearing, the All-knowing.’ (35)

Key Points

As mentioned in the exegeses (tafasir) and narrative books (kutub ruwa'i): Two sisters named "Hannah" and "Ishya'," the first being the wife of "Imran," one of the prominent figures of the Children of Israel, and the second being the wife of the Prophet Zachariah (Zakariyya), were unable to conceive children. One day, "Hannah" was sitting under a tree and saw a bird feeding its chicks. Maternal affection ignited the fire of love for a child within her. Immediately, she prayed, and her prayer was answered. On the other hand, her husband was inspired that their child would be one of the friends of Allah (awliya' Allah), would heal the sick, and revive the dead. When "Hannah" became pregnant, she predicted that the child who would perform these extraordinary deeds would be a boy. Therefore, she vowed (made a nadhr) that her child would become a servant of Bayt al-Maqdis. But when the infant was born, they saw that it was a girl. They understood that the divine inspiration was not about Mary herself, but rather about her son. [40]

40) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 535.

Messages

1- The spiritual growth of a woman reaches such a height that, after years of waiting, she vows her child to the service of the House of Allah. «امرأت عمران ربّ انّى نذرت لك»

2- Far-sighted individuals consider the path of service for their child even before birth. «نذرت لك ما فى بطنى محرّراً»

3- We should sacrifice our own desires and spend only in the way of Allah. «نذرتُ لك»

4- Service in the mosque is so valuable that the friends of Allah (awliya' Allah) vow their dearest ones to it before birth. «نذرت لك...»

5- The subject of the vow (nadhr) has a very long history in divine religions. «ربّ انّى نذرت»

6- The mother possesses a type of guardianship (wilayah) over her child. «نذرت لك ما فى بطنى»

7- There is a relationship between sacrificing one's child and being chosen by Allah. «اصطفى... آل عمران... نذرت لك ما فى بطنى محرّراً»

8- Those who dedicate their entire being to service, not just a portion of their time, are more successful in religious services. «محرّراً»

9- The child's service to the mother is the mother's natural right and is forgivable (i.e., she can waive this right). «محرّرا»

10- If you give the dearest and most beloved things, also be mindful of their acceptance (by Allah). «فتَقبَّل»

Ayah

فَلَمَّا وَضَعَتْهَا قَالَتْ رَبِّ إِنِّى وَضَعْتُهَآ أُنثَىٰ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا وَضَعَتْ وَلَيْسَ ٱلذَّكَرُ كَٱلْأُنثَىٰ ۖ وَإِنِّى سَمَّيْتُهَا مَرْيَمَ وَإِنِّىٓ أُعِيذُهَا بِكَ وَذُرِّيَّتَهَا مِنَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنِ ٱلرَّجِيمِ (۳٦)
When she bore her, she said, ‘My Lord, I have borne a female [child]’—and Allah knew better what she had borne, and the male [child she expected] was no match for the female [child she had borne] —‘and I have named her Mary, and I commend her and her offspring to Your care against [the evil of] the outcast Satan.’ (36)

Key Points

The mother expected her child to be a boy so that he could become a servant of the Holy House (Bayt al-Maqdis). Therefore, when the infant was born a girl, she said regretfully: My Lord! I have given birth to a daughter. Now how can I fulfill my vow? But Allah, to console this mother, says: The son you expected is not as good as this daughter, because this daughter possesses perfections and will become the mother of a son who will also be a source of blessing. Thus, your share of the blessed lineage will be multiplied many times over.

"Maryam" (Mary) means "worshipper" ('abidah) and "servant" (khadimah), and this name appears 34 times in the Quran.

Messages

1- Human desires are not important; the expediency and will of Allah are important. «واللّه اعلم بما وضعت»

2- Sometimes a daughter reaches a level of purity and chastity that a son never reaches. «لیس الذكر كالانثى»

3- Lady Maryam holds a high status. «لیس الذكر كالاُنثى» The intent of al-untha (the female) here is a specific woman.

4- Choose good names for your children. «انّى سمّیتها مریم»

5 - The mother has the right to name her child. «انّى سمّیتها»

6- Minor changes should not distance you from Allah and high goals. Although the child was a girl, contrary to expectation, she named her Maryam, which means "servant." «سمّیتها مریم»

7- Before anything else, think about the child's safety from the evil of Satan. «اعیذها»

8 - Do not consider only immediate children; with foresight, consider subsequent generations as well. «اعیذها بك و ذرّیتها»

9- To ensure the child's happiness, do not rely solely on your own upbringing; entrust her to Allah, for our capabilities are limited and the factors of deviation are numerous. «اعیذها بك»

10- Maryam's mother holds a special status. This conclusion is drawn from the statement of her vow, her sincerity, her naming, and her seeking refuge (for the child). «نذرت لك، سمّیتها، اعیذها بك»

11- Satan is the ancient enemy of the human race. «اعیذها... من الشیطان الرجیم»

Ayah

فَتَقَبَّلَهَا رَبُّهَا بِقَبُولٍ حَسَنٍ وَأَنۢبَتَهَا نَبَاتًا حَسَنًا وَكَفَّلَهَا زَكَرِيَّا ۖ كُلَّمَا دَخَلَ عَلَيْهَا زَكَرِيَّا ٱلْمِحْرَابَ وَجَدَ عِندَهَا رِزْقًا ۖ قَالَ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ أَنَّىٰ لَكِ هَـٰذَا ۖ قَالَتْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ (۳۷)
Thereupon her Lord accepted her with a gracious acceptance, and made her grow up in a worthy fashion, and He charged Zechariah with her care. Whenever Zechariah visited her in the sanctuary, he would find provisions with her. He said, ‘O Mary, from where does this come for you?’ She said, ‘It comes from Allah. Allah provides whomever He wishes without any reckoning.’ (37)

Key Points

Whoever steps onto the path of Allah, his worldly needs are also secured. The mother of Maryam vowed her child for Allah. Allah says in this Ayah: We caused her body to grow, We appointed a guardian for her like Zachariah (Zakariyya), and We sent down heavenly sustenance (Mā’idah) upon her. Sustenance without measure (Rizq bi-ghayri hisāb) does not mean that it lacks accountability, but rather that it is outside of common calculations. «یرزق من یشاء بغیر حساب» (He provides sustenance to whom He wills without measure.) Lady Maryam had complete and excellent physical and outward growth. «فانبتها نباتاً حسناً» (And her Lord accepted her with a gracious acceptance and caused her to grow in a good manner.) A mother like Maryam, a place of upbringing like the mosque, a guardian like the Prophet Zachariah, and heavenly nourishment—the product of this combination is a child like Jesus (‘Īsā). The effective factors in upbringing are: The pure soul of the mother. «تقبّلها ربّها بقبول حسن» (Her Lord accepted her with a gracious acceptance.) A healthy body. «نباتاً حسناً» (Caused her to grow in a good manner.) Divine education and training. «كفّلها زكریّا» (And entrusted her to the care of Zachariah.) Pure nourishment. «وجد عندها رزقاً» (He found sustenance with her.) It is recorded in history: Once, a famine struck Medina. One day, Lady Fatimah علیها السلام brought some bread and meat to the Prophet. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله asked: Where did this food come from during the famine? She replied: «هو من عنداللّه» (It is from Allah). The Prophet said: Thank Allah, Who made you like Maryam. Then the Prophet gathered Lady Ali, Imam Hassan, and Imam Hussain علیهم السلام, and they all ate from that food and also gave some to the neighbors.(41)

41) Tafsir Rūḥ al-Bayān, Vol. 2, p. 29.

Messages

  1. The acceptance of prayer is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «تقبّلها ربّها»
  2. Acceptance by Allah has degrees. Allah accepted the vow of Maryam’s mother in the best manner. «بقبول حسن»
  3. Whoever is sincere (Ikhlāṣ), Allah accepts his deed graciously. «بقبول حسن»
  4. The mother of Maryam vowed that her child would be a servant of the House of Allah. In response, Allah appointed His Prophet as the servant and guardian of this child. «كفّلها زكریّا»
  5. Whoever comes under the guardianship of the Prophets is kept far from Satan. «اعیذها... من الشیطان الرّجیم... و كفّلها زكریّا»
  6. Worship should not be seasonal or temporary. «كلّما دخل»
  7. The condition of guardianship is supervision and investigation. «كفّلها... دخل علیها... انّى لك هذا»
  8. Worship is a war against Satan, and the place of worship (Miḥrāb) means the place of battle. «دخل علیها زكریّا المحراب»
  9. Pure sustenance is found in the shade of worship. «المحراب وجد عندها رزقا»
  10. A woman can reach such a station that she causes a Prophet of Allah to be astonished. «اَنّى لكِ هذا»
  11. The issuance of miracles (Karāmāt) from the friends of Allah (Awliyā’) is not a difficult matter. «هو من عنداللّه»
  12. Know that blessings are from Him, not from your own efforts. «هو من عنداللّه»
  13. Allah can provide sustenance outside of material means. «هذا من عند اللّه»
  14. In a healthy environment and under proper conditions, men and women can speak with each other. «انّى لك هذا قالت هو من عنداللّه»

Ayah

هُنَالِكَ دَعَا زَكَرِيَّا رَبَّهُۥ ۖ قَالَ رَبِّ هَبْ لِى مِن لَّدُنكَ ذُرِّيَّةً طَيِّبَةً ۖ إِنَّكَ سَمِيعُ ٱلدُّعَآءِ (۳۸)
Thereat Zechariah supplicated his Lord. He said, ‘My Lord! Grant me a good offspring from You! Indeed You hear all supplications.’ (38)

Key Points

As mentioned previously, the mother of Maryam (Mary) and the wife of Zachariah (Zakariyya) were sisters, and both were barren and infertile. The mother of Maryam was deeply moved upon seeing a bird feeding its chicks, and she prayed to God; Allah then granted her Maryam. Here too, when Prophet Zachariah saw the status and rank of Maryam, he requested a child from Allah.

Messages

  1. The state of the person and the timing of the supplication are effective in its acceptance. «هنالك دعا زكریّا»

  2. Envy (ghibta) is perfection, but jealousy (hasad) is a defect. Zachariah عليه السلام was captivated and felt envy upon seeing the status of Maryam علیها السلام, and he raised his hands in supplication. «هنالك دعا»

  3. A woman can influence a Prophet of God. «هنالك دعا زكریّا»

  4. Observing the perfections of others is a prelude to requesting perfections and turning one's attention toward Allah. «هنالك دعا زكریّا»

  5. Requesting a pure child and progeny is the tradition and method of the Prophets. «هب لى... ذرّیة طیّبة»

  6. The value of progeny and children lies in their purity. «ذُرّیّةً طیّبةً»

  7. In supplication, one must glorify Allah. «انّك سمیع الدعاء»

Ayah

فَنَادَتْهُ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ وَهُوَ قَآئِمٌ يُصَلِّى فِى ٱلْمِحْرَابِ أَنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكَ بِيَحْيَىٰ مُصَدِّقًۢا بِكَلِمَةٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَسَيِّدًا وَحَصُورًا وَنَبِيًّا مِّنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۳۹)
Then, as he stood praying in the sanctuary the angels called out to him: ‘Allah gives you the good news of John, as a confirmer of a Word of Allah, eminent and chaste, a prophet, and one of the righteous.’ (39)

Key Points

History records that Prophet Yahya (John) was 6 months older than Prophet Isa (Jesus). Because he was known among the people for his asceticism and piety, his belief in his cousin, Prophet Isa, had a profound effect on drawing people's attention to the Messiah.
