It is interesting that the meaning of 'Isa' and 'Yahya' is the same, both meaning "to remain alive" (or "living"). Some have interpreted hasoor (حَصور) to mean Yahya's abandonment of marriage due to extensive travels, but Imam Baqir عليه السلام says: Yahya's chastity caused him not to marry and to keep away from women.[42]

42) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Sincere prayer from a pure heart is answered. «دعا زكریّا... فنادته الملائكة»

2- Prayer (Salat) existed in previous religions as well. «یصلى فى المحراب»

3- The Mihrab (sanctuary) of the mosque possesses a special sanctity. It is the place where heavenly sustenance descends; «وجد عندها رزقاً», and where prayers are answered. «فنادته الملائكة... فى المحراب»

4- Prayer is the context for the descent of angels. «نادته الملائكة و هو یصلّى فى المحراب»

5- The Will of Allah overcomes everything. The old age of the father and the barrenness of the mother are not obstacles to having a child. «انّ اللّه یبشّرك بیحیى»

6- Sometimes the naming of individuals comes from Allah. «یُبشّرُك بیحیى»

7- A child is among the blessings and glad tidings from Allah. «یبشّرك بیحیى»

8- Prophets confirm one another. «مصدقا بكلمة من اللّه»

9- Prophet Isa is a sign of Allah's power, not the son of God. «بكلمة من اللّه»

10- Allah knows the future of every person. «سیّداً و حصوراً و نبیّاً»

11- Chastity and the control of sexual desires are praised by Allah. «حصوراً»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِى غُلَـٰمٌ وَقَدْ بَلَغَنِىَ ٱلْكِبَرُ وَٱمْرَأَتِى عَاقِرٌ ۖ قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ ٱللَّهُ يَفْعَلُ مَا يَشَآءُ (٤٠)
He said, ‘My Lord, how shall I have a son while old age has overtaken me and my wife is barren?’ Said He, ‘So it is that Allah does whatever He wishes.’ (40)

Messages

1- In despair, there is great hope. «انّى یكون لى غلام... كذلك یفعل ما یشاء»

2- When stating weaknesses, we should begin with ourselves. «بلغنى الكبر و امرأتى عاقر»

3- The Will of Allah is above natural means and causes. «یفعل ما یشاء»

Ayah

قَالَ رَبِّ ٱجْعَل لِّىٓ ءَايَةً ۖ قَالَ ءَايَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ ٱلنَّاسَ ثَلَـٰثَةَ أَيَّامٍ إِلَّا رَمْزًا ۗ وَٱذْكُر رَّبَّكَ كَثِيرًا وَسَبِّحْ بِٱلْعَشِىِّ وَٱلْإِبْكَـٰرِ (٤۱)
He said, ‘My Lord, grant me a sign.’ Said He, ‘Your sign is that you will not speak to people for three days except in gestures. Remember Your Lord much, and glorify Him morning and evening.’ (41)

Messages

1- The God Who can seal the tongue when speaking to people and open it when remembering Him, can also bring forth a child from an old father and a barren mother. «كذلك اللّه یغعل ما یشاء... الاّ تكلّم الناس»

2- Prophets seek to attain the station of certainty and direct witnessing. «اجعل لى آیة»

3- Wherever Allah wills, He renders the means ineffective, and the tongue loses the power to speak. «لاتكلّم الناس»

4- The more divine grace increases, the more His remembrance must also increase. «واذكر ربّك كثیراً»

5- The more the remembrance of Allah, the better. «واذكر ربّك كثیراً»

6- Among the forms of remembering Allah, glorification holds a special status. «واذكر ربّك... و سبّح»

Ayah

وَإِذْ قَالَتِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ ٱصْطَفَىٰكِ وَطَهَّرَكِ وَٱصْطَفَىٰكِ عَلَىٰ نِسَآءِ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (٤۲)
And when the angels said, ‘O Mary, Allah has chosen you and purified you, and He has chosen you above the world’s women. (42)

Key Points

The word "اِصطفى" (Iṣṭafā), wherever it is accompanied by the particle "على" ('alā), means being foremost and excelling, and wherever it is without it, it means selection (or choice). We read in the commentaries Al-Manār, Qurṭubī, Marāghī, Rūḥ al-Bayān, and Kabīr that the foremost women of the world are four: Maryam (Mary), Āsiyah, Khadījah, and Fāṭimah (علیهم السلام). It is also mentioned in the narrations of the Ahl al-Bayt (علیهم السلام) that Maryam was the foremost woman of her time, but Fāṭimah (علیها السلام) is the foremost woman throughout history. [43] Of course, whenever the Wise Allah chooses a person, it is due to his or her special merits and capabilities.

43) Tafsīr Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Angels speak to people other than the Prophets as well. «قالت الملائكة یا مریم»

2- A woman can reach the station of Waliyyat Allāh (Friend of Allah), and Allah can send a message to her. «انّ اللّه اصطفاكِ»

3- Hadrat Maryam (Mary) was chosen both in terms of perfections and was also given superiority over other women. For this reason, the phrase «اصطفاكِ» is repeated twice.

4- Hadrat Maryam (Mary) was infallible (ma'ṣūm). «طهّركِ»

5- Hadrat Maryam (Mary) is a role model for women. «اصطفاكِ على نساء العالمین»

Ayah

يَـٰمَرْيَمُ ٱقْنُتِى لِرَبِّكِ وَٱسْجُدِى وَٱرْكَعِى مَعَ ٱلرَّٰكِعِينَ (٤۳)
O Mary, be obedient to your Lord and prostrate and bow down with those who bow [in worship].’ (43)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah mentioned three perfections granted to Maryam علیها السلام: Divine selection; «اصطفاكِ» Divine purification; «طهّركِ» and becoming superior to others; «اصطفاكِ على نساء العالمین». In this Ayah, He demands three responsibilities from her: devotion (Qunut); «اُقنُتى» prostration (Sujud); «اُسجُدى» and bowing (Ruku'); «اِركعى». Thus, every blessing is followed by a responsibility.

Messages

1- Eminent individuals must have greater humility and worship. «یا مریم اقنتى...» (Special acts of worship are enjoined upon the friends of Allah, just as the night prayer was obligatory for the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله.)

2- Prayer (Salat), bowing (Ruku'), and prostration (Sujud) are the best ways to show gratitude. In the previous Ayah, Allah's selection was mentioned, which deserves gratitude. «اصطفاك... اقنتى»

3- Worship must be solely for the Lord. «لربّك»

4- Worship is the source of human nurturing. «ربّك»

5- Congregational prayer and collective worship existed even before Islam. «مع الراكعین»

6- The presence of women in religious gatherings and gatherings where men are also present is recommended. «مع الراكعین» provided that women behave like Maryam (Mary).

7- The chosen and selected individuals must be alongside the masses and within the fabric of society, not separate or isolated from the people. «اركعى مع الراكعین»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ أَنۢبَآءِ ٱلْغَيْبِ نُوحِيهِ إِلَيْكَ ۚ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يُلْقُونَ أَقْلَـٰمَهُمْ أَيُّهُمْ يَكْفُلُ مَرْيَمَ وَمَا كُنتَ لَدَيْهِمْ إِذْ يَخْتَصِمُونَ (٤٤)
These accounts are from the Unseen, which We reveal to you, and you were not with them when they were casting lots [to see] which of them would take charge of Mary’s care, nor were you with them when they were contending. (44)

Key Points

As was mentioned, before the birth of Maryam (Mary), her mother had vowed that her child would be a servant of the Mosque of Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem Temple). Therefore, after the birth, she wrapped the infant in a cloth, brought her to the Mosque, and told the elders of the Children of Israel: This child is dedicated to the Mosque. Since this mother belonged to a great and respected family, a severe dispute [44] arose over who would take custody of the infant, and it was decided by drawing lots.

44) The repetition of the phrase «ما كنت لدیهم...» (You were not with them...) implies that the dispute was severe.

Messages

1- Prophets are informed by Allah about a portion of the Unseen (Ghayb). «من انباء الغیب» [45]

2- The stories of the Quran were revealed to the Messenger of Allah only through revelation (Wahy); before that, they were neither in a book nor preserved in memory. «من انباء الغیب»

3- One of the ways to know the history of the predecessors is through revelation. «ذلك من انباء الغیب»

4- One of the ways to resolve disputes is drawing lots (Qur'ah). «یلقون اقلامهم» [46]

5- One sacrifice by the mother resulted in all this honor for the child. [47] «نذرت... ایّهم یكفل مریم»

6- Great figures compete with one another in accepting sacred responsibilities. «ایّهم یكفل مریم»

45) One of the manifestations of the miracle (I'jaz) of the Quran is its reports concerning the Unseen and its narration of hidden historical facts.

46) Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The first person for whom lots were drawn was Maryam (Mary) علیها السلام, and this drawing of lots occurred during the time of her orphanhood. (Man La Yahdhur, Vol. 3, p. 51; Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 192.)

47) Although Maryam's mother had been childless for years, due to the vow she had made, she gave up her beloved child, and in return, the elders of the Children of Israel fiercely competed for her guardianship.

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَتِ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يَـٰمَرْيَمُ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُبَشِّرُكِ بِكَلِمَةٍ مِّنْهُ ٱسْمُهُ ٱلْمَسِيحُ عِيسَى ٱبْنُ مَرْيَمَ وَجِيهًا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَمِنَ ٱلْمُقَرَّبِينَ (٤٥)
When the angels said, ‘O Mary, Allah gives you the good news of a Word from Him whose name is Messiah, Jesus son of Mary, distinguished in the world and the Hereafter and one of those brought near [to Allah]. (45)

Key Points

In the Quran, Jesus (حضرت عیسى) is referred to as "كلمة" (Word), which, in the Quranic culture, means "creature" (مخلوق). Similar to Ayah 109 of Surah Al-Kahf, where He says: «قل لو كان البحر مداداً لكلمات ربّى لَنَفد البحر قبل ان تنفد كلمات ربّى» If the seas were ink for writing the Divine Words, the seas would be exhausted, but the Divine Words would not come to an end. The description «وجیهاً فى الدنیا و الآخرة» (highly honored in this world and the Hereafter) is mentioned in the Quran only for Jesus عليه السلام, and this attribute is not stated regarding any other person.

Messages

1- The status of a woman reaches the point where Allah speaks to her through the angels. «اذ قالت الملائكة یا مریم»

2- A child is a blessing. «یبشّرك»

3- Jesus is not the son of God; He is a creature of God, but a very great and unknown creature.(48) «كلمة منه»

4- Allah names His chosen ones before their birth. «اسمه المسیح»

5- How can someone who was born of Mary and passed through the embryonic stage be the son of God? «عیسى بن مریم»

6- Worldly honor is also a value, and acquiring it is not prohibited. «وجیهاً فى الدنیا»

(48) The word «كلمة» is used in the indefinite form, which is a symbol of greatness and unknowability.

Ayah

وَيُكَلِّمُ ٱلنَّاسَ فِى ٱلْمَهْدِ وَكَهْلًا وَمِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (٤٦)
He will speak to the people in the cradle and in adulthood, and will be one of the righteous.’ (46)

Key Points

Speaking in the cradle is both a prophecy of the future and a miracle of Christ (حضرت مسیح), who spoke to the people while in the cradle. Speaking in adulthood is another prophecy that He will live until adulthood. Just as the news of His worthiness and piety is also a prophecy and glad tidings.

Messages

1- He who can grant Mary a child without a husband can open the tongue of her child to speak in the cradle. «یكلّم النّاس فى المهد»

2- Where Allah wills to clear a chaste woman from accusation, He opens the closed tongue of a child. «یكلّم النّاس فى المهد»

3- Even in childhood, one can convey the Divine message to others.(49) «یكلّم النّاس فى المهد»

4- Where Allah wills, a child speaks like adults. «فى المهد وكهلا»

5- From a righteous mother like Mary, a son like Jesus emerges. «من الصالحین»

49) Some Imams, such as Imam Jawad, Imam Hadi, and Imam Mahdi علیهم السلام, attained the station of Imamate in childhood.

Ayah

قَالَتْ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِى وَلَدٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِى بَشَرٌ ۖ قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ ٱللَّهُ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (٤۷)
She said, ‘My Lord, how shall I have a child seeing that no human has ever touched me?’ He said, ‘So it is that Allah creates whatever He wishes. When He decides on a matter He just says to it ‘‘Be!’’ and it is. (47)

Key Points

The Will of Allah is the cause of the emergence and creation of beings, and He can create even without material means and causes. He is the Creator of causes and the Nullifier of causes; sometimes He removes the effect of a thing, and sometimes He grants effect to a thing. The emergence, survival, and effects of everything depend on His Will and desire. Allah said in response to Zachariah: "یفعل مایشاء" (He does what He wills), and in response to Mary, He said: "یخلق مایشاء" (He creates what He wills). Perhaps the secret behind this is that a child for a woman without a husband is more astonishing than a child for an old man.

Messages

1- If the root of the question and astonishment is not denial or obstinacy, there is no objection to it. "أَنّى یكون لى ولد" (How shall I have a son?). The friends of Allah also understand the Will of Allah in the affairs of the world to be based on natural causes and means, and therefore, if a special glad tiding comes contrary to that, they ask Allah about its manner (of occurrence).

2- Creation by Allah through unconventional means is not a new thing, and natural factors are not limited to known matters. "كذلك" (Thus).

3- The Hand of Allah is open for creation. Creating through natural means and non-natural means is the same for Allah. "یخلق ما یشاء" (He creates what He wills).

4- The condition for creation is the definite Divine Will. "قضى امراً... كن فیكون" (He decrees a matter... 'Be,' and it is).

Ayah

وَيُعَلِّمُهُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ وَٱلْإِنجِيلَ (٤۸)
He will teach him the Book and wisdom, the Torah and the Evangel, (48)

Key Points

We read in both Shia and Sunni exegeses that the purpose of teaching the Book (تعلیم كتاب) is instruction in script and writing, and the purpose of wisdom (حكمت) is awareness of the benefits and harms of things, actions, morals, and beliefs, whether their effects are worldly or related to the Hereafter.

Messages

  1. One of the principles and conditions of divine leadership is possessing the necessary knowledge. This knowledge is based on science, wisdom, and heavenly books. «یعلّمه الكتاب و...»

  2. The leader of every era must also be aware of past events and laws. Allah taught Jesus عليه السلام the Torah of Moses عليه السلام. «والتوراة»

Ayah

وَرَسُولًا إِلَىٰ بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ أَنِّى قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ أَنِّىٓ أَخْلُقُ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلطِّينِ كَهَيْـَٔةِ ٱلطَّيْرِ فَأَنفُخُ فِيهِ فَيَكُونُ طَيْرًۢا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُبْرِئُ ٱلْأَكْمَهَ وَٱلْأَبْرَصَ وَأُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا تَأْكُلُونَ وَمَا تَدَّخِرُونَ فِى بُيُوتِكُمْ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَةً لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (٤۹)
and [he will be] an apostle to the Children of Israel, [and he will declare,] “I have certainly brought you a sign from your Lord: I will create for you the form of a bird out of clay, then I will breathe into it, and it will become a bird by Allah’s leave. I heal the blind and the leper and I revive the dead by Allah’s leave. I will tell you what you have eaten and what you have stored in your houses. There is indeed a sign in that for you, should you be faithful. (49)

Key Points

Wherever danger is sensed, greater attention must be paid to the matter. In reviving the dead, healing the blind and other sick people, there is a danger of doctrinal deviation and exaggeration (ghuluww). Therefore, the issue of Allah's permission (idhn) is mentioned twice in this Ayah and four times in Ayah 110 of Surah Al-Ma'idah. The apostleship (risalah) of 'Isa (Jesus) عليه السلام, which was accompanied by divine grace or wrath, was specific to the Children of Israel: «رسولاً الى بنى اسرائیل» (a messenger to the Children of Israel). However, His prophethood (nubuwwah), which was the station of propagation and guidance, was for all people. Similar to Prophet Musa (Moses), to whom it is addressed: «اذهب الى فرعون انّه طغى» (Go to Pharaoh; indeed, he has transgressed) [50]. But when the city's sorcerers saw the miracle of Prophet Musa, they believed in Him. Therefore, Prophet Musa had one specific mission concerning Pharaoh himself, and another mission for the general guidance of all people. [51] Every prophet must have a miracle, and His miracle must be appropriate to the conditions and beliefs of the people of that era. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: 'Isa عليه السلام appeared at a time when diseases were widespread and people needed physicians. [52] Therefore, His miracles were aimed at healing the sick. If a friend (wali) of Allah creates a bird from clay, then the resurrection of the dead on the Day of Judgment by divine power is not a difficult task.

50) Taha, 24. 51) Tafsir Al-Mizan concerning the Ayah. 52) Uyun Akhbar Al-Ridha, Vol. 2, p. 80.

Messages

1- The miracle is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rububiyyah) and is in line with the guidance and training of human beings. «جئتكم بایة من ربّكم»

2- The friends (awliya') of Allah, by His permission, have the power to influence and change the system of creation. «انفخ فیه فیكون طیراً باذن اللّه»

3- Prophets possess knowledge of the unseen ('ilm al-ghayb) and are even aware of the details of people's lives. [53] «انبئكم بما تأكلون و ما تدّخرون فى بیوتكم»

53) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Prophet 'Isa, at the age of seven, would inform people of what they consumed or stored in their homes. Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 251.

Ayah

وَمُصَدِّقًا لِّمَا بَيْنَ يَدَىَّ مِنَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ وَلِأُحِلَّ لَكُم بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى حُرِّمَ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ وَجِئْتُكُم بِـَٔايَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (٥٠)
And [I come] to confirm [the truth of] that which is before me of the Torah, and to make lawful for you some of the things that were forbidden you. I have brought you a sign from your Lord; so be wary of Allah and obey me. (50)

Key Points

This verse addresses respect for the previous law, the good news of lightening the obligation (duty), emphasis on Divine piety (Taqwa), and obedience to leadership.

In Ayah 146 of Surah Al-An'am, Allah says: Because of the oppression of the Jews, We punished them and forbade every animal with undivided hoofs (or claws) upon them, and We also forbade the fat and suet of cattle and sheep, except what adheres to their bones, intestines, or loins. Perhaps the prohibitions that are made lawful in this verse with the coming of 'Isa (Jesus) عليه السلام are these very cases.

Wherever the foundation is based on reform and guidance, certain points must be observed:

A: Accept the common and correct principles of others. «مصدّقاً»

B: Respect their sanctities as much as possible. «من التوراة»

C: Give the good news of open space and freedoms. «لاحلّ لكم»

D: Act within the framework of Divine laws. «فاتّقوا اللّه»

Messages

1- Prophets accept and confirm one another. «و مصدّقا...»

2- Religion is a continuous flow, not a spark, and all Prophets share a single goal. «و مصدّقا لما بين يدىّ من التوراة»

3- The promise of removing deprivation, lifting restrictions, and granting correct and reasonable freedoms is the key to gaining public acceptance. «لاحلّ لكم بعض الّذى حرّم عليكم»

4- Some restrictions in previous religions were temporary Divine penalties, not permanent Divine rulings. «لاحلّ لكم بعض الّذى حرّم عليكم»

5- Just as the Prophets possess Wilayah Takwini (cosmic authority) and the power to influence existence; «اخلق لكم» they also possess Wilayah Tashri'i (legislative authority) and establish laws. «لاحلّ لكم...»

6- Only one who is a Messenger of Allah and possesses a miracle has the right to change the law. The phrase «جئتكم بآية» follows the phrase «احلّ لكم».

7- The miracle is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah) and is for the guidance and training of the people. «آية من ربّكم»

8- Anyone who is prejudiced and rigid and does not accept appropriate changes lacks piety (Taqwa). «لاحلّ لكم... اتّقوا اللّه»

9- The prerequisite of piety is obedience to the Prophet. «فاتّقوا اللّه و اطيعون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّى وَرَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۗ هَـٰذَا صِرَٰطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ (٥۱)
Indeed Allah is my Lord and your Lord; so worship Him. This is a straight path.” ’ (51)

Key Points

Contrary to the currently distorted Gospels, which refer to God as the father of Jesus (عليه السلام), the Quran quotes the phrase «ربّى و ربّكم» (My Lord and your Lord) from the tongue of Prophet Jesus, in order to combat any false thinking or claim of divinity regarding Prophet Jesus. This is because Allah is the Lord of Jesus and all other people.

Now that we are meant to traverse a path, let us accept the path of Allah and servitude to Him, which is a straight path without deviation. Whereas the paths of others contain pitfalls, limitations, and are subject to the inner desires of the tyrants (Taghuts).

Messages

1- The philosophy behind our worship is the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah. «انّ اللّه ربّى و ربّكم فاعبدوه»

2- Jesus (عليه السلام), in being a creation and being under nurturing, is like all other people. «ربّى و ربّكم»

3- Worship and servitude to Allah is the straight path to felicity. «فاعبدوه هذا صراط مستقیم»

Ayah

۞ فَلَمَّآ أَحَسَّ عِيسَىٰ مِنْهُمُ ٱلْكُفْرَ قَالَ مَنْ أَنصَارِىٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ قَالَ ٱلْحَوَارِيُّونَ نَحْنُ أَنصَارُ ٱللَّهِ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَٱشْهَدْ بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ (٥۲)
When Jesus sensed their faithlessness, he said, ‘Who will be my helpers [on the path] toward Allah?’ The Disciples said, ‘We will be helpers of Allah. We have faith in Allah, and you be witness that we have submitted [to Him]. (52)

Key Points

Even though the Children of Israel witnessed the raising of the dead and the healing of the blind by Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام), they persisted in obstinacy and uttered disbelief (kufr), to the extent that, according to Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام), Jesus used to hear their blasphemous words. [54] "حَواریّون" (Hawariyyun) is the plural of "حَوارىّ" (Hawari), meaning one who changes the path. The Hawariyyun were those who abandoned the misguided path of the people and joined the way of truth. [55] Their number was twelve, and their names are mentioned in the Gospels of Matthew and Luke. Imam Reza (عليه السلام) said: They were individuals who both purified and illuminated themselves and strove to purify others. [56] It is narrated in Al-Kafi from Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) that He said: The Hawariyyun of Jesus abandoned him at the stage of action, but our Hawariyyun embrace all kinds of tribulations. [57]

54) Bihar, Vol. 14, p. 373. 55) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran. 56) Tafsir Namuneh. 57) Kafi, Vol. 8, p. 268.

Messages

1- Awareness in recognizing the thoughts and beliefs of the people is a prerequisite for leadership. «فلمّا أحسّ عیسى منهم الكفر»

2- The breath of Jesus revives the dead and heals the congenitally blind, but obstinate people gain no benefit from it. «فلما احس عیسى منهم الكفر...»

3- The Prophets were afflicted by obstinate disbelievers and had few helpers. «احسّ عیسى منهم الكفر»

4- Lack of piety (taqwa) and disobedience to leadership is a form of disbelief (kufr). «فاتّقوا اللّه واطیعون فلما احسّ عیسى منهم الكفر»

5- Recognizing loyal forces, organizing and concentrating them, and separating the front of truth from falsehood is essential for the leader and the continuation of His movement. «من أنصارى الى اللّه»

6- Renewing allegiance (bay'ah) to the leadership holds political, social, and religious value. «نحن انصاراللّه»

7- The Prophets seek people for the sake of Allah, not for themselves. «من انصارى الى اللّه...نحن انصاراللّه»

8- Aiding the divine callers is aiding Allah. «نحن انصار اللّه»

9- Precedence in supporting religious leaders holds value. Although Prophet Jesus had supporters, Allah praised the faith of the Hawariyyun, and this is due to their precedence and forthrightness. «من انصارى... نحن انصاراللّه»

10- The station of submission (taslim) to Allah comes after the stage of faith (iman). «امنَّا باللَّه... مسلمون»

11- The Prophets are witnesses and testifiers on the Day of Judgment. «واشهد بانّا مسلمون»

Ayah

رَبَّنَآ ءَامَنَّا بِمَآ أَنزَلْتَ وَٱتَّبَعْنَا ٱلرَّسُولَ فَٱكْتُبْنَا مَعَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (٥۳)
Our Lord, we believe in what You have sent down, and we follow the apostle, so write us among the witnesses.’ (53)

Key Points

In the phrase «مع الشاهدین» (with the witnesses), there is a meaning of support which is not present in the phrase «من الشاهدین» (from/among the witnesses). This is similar to the difference between «آمنوا به» and «آمنوا معه». Since in the previous Ayah, Prophet Jesus (عليه السلام) was presented as a witness, being with the witnesses means being an associate and helper of the Prophets.

Messages

1- The prerequisite of faith is obedience to the Prophet. «آمنّا... واتّبعنا الرسول»

2- Belief in all Divine commands is necessary. «بما انزلت»

3- We should ask Allah to stabilize our faith and make us among the supporters of the Prophets. «فاكتبنا مع الشاهدین»

Ayah

وَمَكَرُوا۟ وَمَكَرَ ٱللَّهُ ۖ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرُ ٱلْمَـٰكِرِينَ (٥٤)
Then they plotted [against Jesus], and Allah also devised, and Allah is the best of devisers. (54)

Key Points

Imam Ridha عليه السلام said: The intent of Allah's 'makr' (devising) is the punishment for plotting, otherwise, He is not given to plotting (makr). [58] Although the Ayah refers to one of the Divine traditions (Sunan), considering the preceding verses where Prophet Isa (Jesus) felt the disbelief of the Children of Israel, and considering the subsequent verse where Allah raised Isa عليه السلام to the heavens, it is understood that the intent of 'makr' here is the conspiracy to murder Prophet Isa. A group during his time devised plans and schemes to extinguish His call, even setting a reward for His imprisonment, arrest, and identification, and preparing the groundwork for His execution and crucifixion. However, Allah foiled their plans and saved Him in the best possible way.

58) Uyūn Akhbār al-Ridha, Vol. 1, p. 126.

Messages

1- Allah is the supporter of His saints (Awliyā'). «ومكروا ومكر اللّه»

2- Human planning and action are the prelude to Allah's wrath or His grace. «ومكروا ومكر اللّه»

3- Divine punishments are proportionate and consistent with human crimes. «و مكروا و مكر اللّه»

4- The Will and planning of Allah are superior to every effort, action, and scheme. «واللّه خیرالماكرین»

Ayah

إِذْ قَالَ ٱللَّهُ يَـٰعِيسَىٰٓ إِنِّى مُتَوَفِّيكَ وَرَافِعُكَ إِلَىَّ وَمُطَهِّرُكَ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَجَاعِلُ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوكَ فَوْقَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَىَّ مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَأَحْكُمُ بَيْنَكُمْ فِيمَا كُنتُمْ فِيهِ تَخْتَلِفُونَ (٥٥)
When Allah said, ‘O Jesus, I shall take you[r soul], and I shall raise you up toward Myself, and I shall clear you of [the calumnies of] the faithless, and I shall set those who follow you above the faithless until the Day of Resurrection. Then to Me will be your return, whereat I will judge between you concerning that about which you used to differ. (55)

Key Points

The practical and tangible example of Divine planning against the enemies' deceit is stated in this Ayah. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The ascension (Mi'raj) of Prophet Isa عليه السلام took place on the night of the 21st of the blessed month of Ramadan. (59)

59) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- There was also an ascension (Mi'raj) for other prophets. «رافعك الىّ»

2- Living among the disbelievers leads to contamination, and distance from them leads to purity and cleansing (taharah). «مطهّرك من الّذین كفروا»

3- The unseen prophecy regarding the dominance of Christianity over Judaism is one of the miracles of the Quran. «جاعل الّذین اتّبعوك فوق الّذین كفروا»

4- Following the prophets is the secret to victory. «جاعل الّذین اتّبعوك فوق الّذین كفروا»

5- The universality of Islam does not contradict the existence of religious minorities under the protection of Islam. It is understood from the phrase «الى یوم القیامة» that Jews and Christians will exist until the Day of Resurrection (Qiyamah).

6- The return of all is towards Allah, and He is the unquestionable Judge. «الىّ مرجعكم»

Ayahs

فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فَأُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (٥٦)
As for the faithless, I will punish them with a severe punishment in the world and the Hereafter; and they will have no helpers.’ (56)
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فَيُوَفِّيهِمْ أُجُورَهُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥۷)
But as for those who have faith and do righteous deeds, He will pay them in full their rewards, and Allah does not like the wrongdoers. (57)

Key Points

In Tafsir Atayeb al-Bayan, an example of the worldly punishments inflicted upon the Children of Israel is mentioned as follows: 37 years after Prophet Christ (Jesus), a person named Titus, who was one of the Caesars of Rome, gained dominance over the Jews. He killed millions of them and took thousands captive. He ordered their properties to be burned or plundered, and their captives to be fed to wild beasts. Alongside threatening the disbelievers, encouraging the people of faith is necessary, just as performing good deeds is necessary alongside faith.

Messages

1- The disbelievers deal directly with Allah Himself. «فاعذبّهم»

2- The greater the completion of the proof (Hujjah), the greater Allah's wrath will be upon the deniers. Those who witness the resurrection of the dead by Jesus but still do not believe deserve every kind of torment. «فاعذبهم عذاباً الیماً»

3- Sometimes Allah also inflicts punishment in this world. «فى الدنیا و الاخرة»

4- Against the wrath of Allah, no power can be a barrier. «و ما لهم من ناصرین»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ نَتْلُوهُ عَلَيْكَ مِنَ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ وَٱلذِّكْرِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (٥۸)
These that We recite to you are from the signs and the Wise Reminder. (58)

Messages

1- For correct and decisive leadership, divine signs and firm, reasoned articulation are necessary. «الایات والذكر الحكیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ مَثَلَ عِيسَىٰ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَثَلِ ءَادَمَ ۖ خَلَقَهُۥ مِن تُرَابٍ ثُمَّ قَالَ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ (٥۹)
Indeed the case of Jesus with Allah is like the case of Adam: He created him from dust, then said to him, ‘Be,’ and he was. (59)

Key Points

A group of Christians (Nasara) entered Medina and came into the presence of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. In their conversation with the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, they cited the birth of Jesus عليه السلام without a father as a sign and proof of his divinity. This Ayah was revealed and provided their answer thus: If birth without a father is proof of being God or the son of God, then the creation of Adam, which is more significant, should also be considered, since he had neither a father nor a mother. So why do you not consider Adam to be God or the son of God?!

Messages

1- We should invite opponents to the truth through the very path they have accepted. Christians accept that Adam is a creation of Allah, even though he had neither a father nor a mother. «مَثَل عیسى... كمَثل آدم»

2- Relying on history and past experiences and presenting tangible examples is the best method of invitation. «انّ مَثَل عیسى عنداللّه كمَثل آدم»

3- The power of Allah in creation is not limited. «كن فیكون»

Ayah

ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ (٦٠)
This is the truth from your Lord, so do not be among the skeptics. (60)

Key Points

The word al-mumtarīn (الممترین) is derived from miryah (مِرْیة), meaning doubt and skepticism. The exact same Ayah has also appeared in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 147. Through this Ayah, it becomes clear that the truthful and enduring word is only from the side of Allah, Who Himself is the Truth (al-Haqq) and the Constant (al-Thābit). Otherwise, from human beings who lack constancy amidst the storms of desires and instincts, one cannot expect an enduring law or word.

Messages

1- Stating the truth is the secret to Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and nurturing. «الحقّ من ربّك»

2- Constancy and truthfulness do not exist except in the path of Allah, the Word of Allah, and the Law of Allah. «الحقّ من ربّك»

3- The large number of opponents, their efforts and activities, and their wealth and propaganda should not affect the truthfulness of our path and beliefs. «فلا تكن من الممترین»

Ayah

فَمَنْ حَآجَّكَ فِيهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَكَ مِنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ فَقُلْ تَعَالَوْا۟ نَدْعُ أَبْنَآءَنَا وَأَبْنَآءَكُمْ وَنِسَآءَنَا وَنِسَآءَكُمْ وَأَنفُسَنَا وَأَنفُسَكُمْ ثُمَّ نَبْتَهِلْ فَنَجْعَل لَّعْنَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰذِبِينَ (٦۱)
Should anyone argue with you concerning him, after the knowledge that has come to you, say, ‘Come! Let us call our sons and your sons, our women and your women, our souls and your souls, then let us pray earnestly, and call down Allah’s curse upon the liars.’ (61)

Key Points

The word Nabtahil (نَبتَهل) is derived from Ibtihal (اِبتهال), meaning to raise the hands and elbows towards the sky for supplication. This Ayah is known as the Ayah of Mubahalah because of this word. Mubahalah means the turning and humble supplication of two opposing groups to Allah, requesting a curse and destruction upon the opposing side, which they deem to be followers of falsehood (batil). (60)

We read in Shia and Sunni commentaries (Tafasir) and some books of Hadith and history that in the tenth year of the Hijra, individuals were sent by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله to propagate Islam in the region of Najran in Yemen. The Christians of Najran also dispatched a delegation to Medina to converse with the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله. Despite the discussions held between them and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, they continued to make excuses and expressed doubt regarding the truth of Islam. This Ayah was revealed, addressing the Prophet: Tell those who argue and dispute with you, and shirk from accepting the truth: Come, let us call upon Allah, bringing our sons, our women, and ourselves, and with humility and supplication (Ibtihal), let us curse the liars. Whichever group the curse falls upon, it will become clear that their path is false, and this means will end the discussion and dispute.

When the representatives of the Christians of Najran heard the proposal of Mubahalah from the Noble Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله, they looked at each other, bewildered. They requested a delay to think, reflect, and consult about the matter. The leader of the Christians told them: Accept the proposal, but if you see the Prophet coming for the curse with noise and a massive crowd, do not worry, and know that there is nothing to fear. However, if He comes to the field with only a few individuals, abandon the Mubahalah and make a settlement with Him. On the day of Mubahalah, they saw the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله emerge accompanied by two children, one young man, and one woman. Those two children were Hasan and Husayn عليهما السلام, that young man was Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام, and that woman was Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام, the daughter of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. The Christian Bishop said: I see faces that, if they ask Allah to uproot a mountain, it will be uprooted. If these individuals curse, not a single Christian will remain on earth. Thus, they announced their withdrawal from the Mubahalah and agreed to a settlement. (61)

This incident, besides being in Shia commentaries, is also mentioned in reliable Sunni sources. (62) The Day of Mubahalah was the twenty-fourth or twenty-fifth of the month of Dhu al-Hijjah. Its location, during the time of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, was outside the city of Medina, but it is now located inside the city, and a mosque named "Masjid al-Ijaba" has been built there. The distance between this mosque and Masjid al-Nabi is approximately two kilometers. Allahumma arzuqna ziyaratahu wa shafa'atahu (O Allah, grant us His visitation and His intercession). Based on a narration in Tafsir al-Mizan, the invitation to Mubahalah was not exclusive to the Christians, and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله also invited the Jews to Mubahalah. Mubahalah was not specific to the time of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله; rather, based on some narrations, other believers can also perform Mubahalah. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام has given instructions regarding this. (63)

Question: In this incident, only Fatimah al-Zahra عليها السلام was present, so why did the Quran use the plural word Nisa'ana (our women)? Answer: There are instances in the Quran where Allah refers to a single person in the plural, such as Ayah 181 of Surah Aal-e-Imran, where one person said insultingly, "Allah is poor," but the Ayah uses the plural form: «الّذین قالوا انّ اللّه فقیر» (Those who said, 'Indeed, Allah is poor'). Similarly, the Quran says about Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام that Ibrahim was an Ummah (nation), even though he was only one person. Although the Prophet could have cursed personally and not involved Ali, Fatimah, Hasan, and Husayn علیهم السلام, Allah and the Messenger, through this action, made us understand that these individuals are the helpers and partners of the Messenger of Allah in the call to truth and His objective. They were ready to welcome danger alongside Him and are the continuers of His movement.

60) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran. 61) The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: I swear by Him who made me the true Prophet, if the Mubahalah had taken place, that valley would have rained fire upon them. Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan; Manaqib Ibn Maghazi, p. 263. 62) The author of Tafsir al-Mizan, in Volume 3, page 257, states that the incident of Mubahalah has been unanimously narrated by 51 companions. Tafasir Kabir, Alusi, and Maraghi under the Ayah, in the book Al-Kamil by Ibn Athir Volume 2, page 293, Mustadrak Hakim Volume 3, page 150, Musnad Ahmad Hanbal Volume 1, page 185, as well as Tafasir Ruh al-Bayan, Al-Manar, and Ibn Kathir, and many other Shia and Sunni sources have recorded and narrated this event. In the book Ihqaq al-Haqq Volume 3, page 46, the names of sixty major Sunni scholars are listed, all of whom stated: This Ayah is about the greatness of the Prophet and His Ahl al-Bayt علیهم السلام. 63) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn Vol 1, p. 351; Usul al-Kafi, Vol 2, Chapter on Mubahalah.

Messages

  1. If a person has faith in the goal, he is willing to expose himself and his closest relatives to danger. «من بعد ما جائك من العلم» (After what has come to you of knowledge).
  2. The believer's final winning card and sharpest weapon is supplication (Du'a). «فقل تعالوا ندع» (Then say, 'Come, let us call').
  3. A daughter's child is like one's own son. «ابنائنا» (Our sons). Therefore, Imam Hasan and Imam Husayn عليهما السلام are the Prophet's sons.
  4. Men and women are mentioned side-by-side in various religious arenas. «نسائنا» (Our women).
  5. In supplication, the state of the supplicants is important, not their number. The Mubahalah group was only five people. «ابنائنا، نسائنا، انفسنا» (Our sons, our women, ourselves).
  6. Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام is the soul/self of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله. «انفسنا» (Ourselves).
  7. We should bring children with us to gatherings of supplication. «ابنائنا» (Our sons).
  8. The Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet علیهم السلام have their prayers answered (Mustajab al-Da'wah). «ابنائنا، نسائنا، انفسنا» (Our sons, our women, ourselves).
  9. Seeking help from the unseen (Ghayb) comes after utilizing ordinary capabilities. «نبتهل» (We humbly supplicate).
  10. Whoever is not subdued by logic, reasoning, and miracle to accept the truth, Mubahalah must be performed against him. «تعالوا... نبتهل» (Come... let us humbly supplicate).
  11. If believers stand firm, the enemy retreats because of the falsehood of their position. «ندع... نبتهل» (We call... we humbly supplicate).
  12. Argument must be answered with argument, but contention and obstinacy must be suppressed. «لعنة اللّه على الكاذبین» (The curse of Allah be upon the liars).

Ayahs

إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَهُوَ ٱلْقَصَصُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۚ وَمَا مِنْ إِلَـٰهٍ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (٦۲)
This is indeed the true account, for sure. There is no god but Allah, and indeed Allah is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (62)
فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (٦۳)
But if they turn away, indeed Allah knows best the agents of corruption. (63)

Key Points

Stories that are commonly spoken about are of three types: 1- Novels, legends, and myths that are based on imagination and are unreal. 2- Historical stories which are sometimes true and documented, and sometimes mixed with lies and incorrect matters. 3- Events that have reached us through the language of revelation (Wahy) are based on truth (Haqq). All Quranic stories are of this type, and illusion, imagination, lies, or falsehood have no place in them.

Messages

1- If the Quran had not existed, the true face of Jesus Christ would have remained tainted by superstitions and distorted. «انّ هذا لهو القصص الحق»

2- Repeating the slogan of Monotheism (Tawhid) and steadfastness against superstitions is necessary. «ما من اله الاّ اللَّه»

3- Any story about the Prophets that contradicts Monotheism (Tawhid) is fabricated and false. «لهو القصص الحق و ما من اله الا اللّه»

4- Man's turning away from the word of truth is a sign of his free will. «فان تولّوا»

5- Turning away from the truth (Haqq) is an example of corruption, and the one who turns away is a corrupter (mufsid). «تولّوا... مفسدین»

6- Allah's knowledge of the actions of the corrupters is the greatest warning. «علیم بالمفسدین»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ تَعَالَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ كَلِمَةٍ سَوَآءٍۭ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ أَلَّا نَعْبُدَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهَ وَلَا نُشْرِكَ بِهِۦ شَيْـًٔا وَلَا يَتَّخِذَ بَعْضُنَا بَعْضًا أَرْبَابًا مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ فَإِن تَوَلَّوْا۟ فَقُولُوا۟ ٱشْهَدُوا۟ بِأَنَّا مُسْلِمُونَ (٦٤)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! Come to a common word between us and you: that we will worship no one but Allah, that we will not ascribe any partner to Him, and that some of us will not take some others as lords besides Allah.’ But if they turn away, say, ‘Be witnesses that we have submitted [to Allah].’ (64)

Key Points

Invitation towards Monotheism (Tawhid) and truth is necessary, whether through argumentation (Istidlal), mutual imprecation (Mubahalah), or by inviting them to common grounds.

It is stated in Tafsir al-Maraghi: After embracing Islam, Adi ibn Hatim said to the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله: When we were Christians, we never took one another as our 'Lord' (Rabb). So, what is meant by the phrase, "لایتّخذ بَعضُنا بَعضاً ارباباً" (that none of us shall take others as lords)? The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Did your scholars not change the rulings of Allah? He said: Yes! He صلى الله علیه وآله said: Following a scholar who changes the law of Allah is a type of servitude and bondage to him.

Messages

1- Muslims must reach an agreement with the People of the Book regarding common grounds. "قل یا اهل الكتاب تعالوا الى كلمة..."

2- One must take the initiative in inviting to unity. "قل یا اهل الكتاب"

3- If you do not achieve all your rightful goals, do not refrain from striving to achieve some of them. "تعالوا الى كلمة..."

4- One stage of propagation (Tabligh) is inviting people to common grounds. "كلمة سواء بیننا و بینكم" The Quran can be a good common axis for invitation, as it contains the history of the prophets and has remained safe from distortion.

5- In propagating and inviting others, one must respect the true beliefs and sacred things of the opposing party. "كلمة سواء بیننا و بینكم"

6- Monotheism and aversion to polytheism (Shirk) are common grounds among all heavenly religions. "كلمة سواء... الاّ نعبد الاّ اللّه"

7- Monotheism (Tawhid) causes human elevation. "تعالوا" (Come) is used in a context where the invitation is towards growth and rising up. "تعالوا... الاّ نعبد الاّ اللّه"

8- Unquestioning obedience to other human beings is a sign of accepting a type of Lordship (Rububiyyah) for them and a type of servitude ('Ubūdiyyah) for us, whereas all human beings are equal to one another. "لایتّخذ بعضنا بعضاً ارباباً"

9- Free thought and independent personality are the slogans of the Quran. "لایتخذ بعضنا بعضاً ارباباً"

10- The turning away and disobedience of opponents should not affect our resolve and faith. "فان تولّوا فقولوا اشهدوا بانّا مسلمون"

11- Every propagator must anticipate the disobedience and turning away of people so that he does not become disappointed. "فان تولّوا فقولوا اشهدوا..."

12- After presenting evidence and proof, cease the argument. "فان تولّوا فقولوا..."

13- Servitude to Allah, the negation of polytheism (Shirk), and the rejection of the sovereignty of anyone other than Allah are characteristics of a true Muslim. "اشهدوا بانّا مسلمون"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِمَ تُحَآجُّونَ فِىٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَمَآ أُنزِلَتِ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةُ وَٱلْإِنجِيلُ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (٦٥)
O People of the Book! Why do you argue concerning Abraham? Neither the Torah nor the Evangel were sent down until [long] after him. Do you not exercise your reason? (65)

Key Points

Each of the Jews and Christians considered Abraham (عليه السلام) to belong to them. The market for this claim was so intense that the Quran states in the two subsequent verses: «ما كان ابراهیم یهودیّا ولا نصرانیّا». This verse, regarding the futility of their claim, asks: How can you consider Abraham, who existed before the Torah and the Gospel, to be a follower of those two books? A book that has not yet been revealed has no followers. Are you not willing to think even this much, so that at least your statement aligns with history?

Messages

1- When preaching, dignified titles and cultural appellations appropriate for the audience should be used. «یا اهل الكتاب»

2- Knowledge and the Book possess such value that those affiliated with them are also respected: People of the Book, people of the pen, people of knowledge. «یا اهل الكتاب»

3- Do not try to prove your righteousness by attributing great personalities to yourselves. «لِمَ تحاجّون فى ابراهیم» Instead of disputing the attribution of noble personalities, follow their thought.

4- Make your claims documented and consistent with logic, innate nature (Fitrah), and history. «و ما اُنزلت التورات»

Ayah

هَـٰٓأَنتُمْ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ حَـٰجَجْتُمْ فِيمَا لَكُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ فَلِمَ تُحَآجُّونَ فِيمَا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ وَأَنتُمْ لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٦٦)
Ah! You are the very ones who argue about that of which you have knowledge. But why do you argue about that of which you have no knowledge? And Allah knows and you do not know. (66)

Key Points

This Ayah is a reminder and a warning to the People of the Book, that you raise objections and ask unwarranted questions regarding matters about which you possess knowledge and awareness. Even though you have witnessed the natural life of Prophet Jesus and His need for food, shelter, and clothing, you still engage in disputes concerning Him. One group considers Him a liar, and another group considers Him the son of God. [64] Or you engage in debate and discussion regarding the recognition of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله, whose signs have appeared in the Torah and the Gospel and is known to you. [65] Since you are people of dispute and debate regarding known facts and fail to reach a point of agreement, why do you concern yourselves with an issue about which you have no knowledge? Such as, what was the religion of Prophet Abraham?

64) Tafsirs Al-Mizan, Fi Zilal, Maraghi, and Al-Manar. 65) Tafsirs Majma‘ al-Bayan and Qurtubi.

Messages

1- Belittle arrogant and obstinate individuals. «هاانتم هؤلاء»

2- Obstinate individuals engage in conflict even regarding clear matters. «حاججتم فیمالكم به علم»

3- If the source of discussions is research, it is valuable, but if it is for the purpose of evasion, it is subject to criticism. «حاججتم فیما لكم به علم»

Ayah

مَا كَانَ إِبْرَٰهِيمُ يَهُودِيًّا وَلَا نَصْرَانِيًّا وَلَـٰكِن كَانَ حَنِيفًا مُّسْلِمًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (٦۷)
Abraham was neither a Jew nor a Christian. Rather, he was a Hanif, a Muslim, and he was not one of the polytheists. (67)

Key Points

Hanaf means inclination towards the Truth (Haqq), and opposite to it is the word Janaf, which means inclination and tendency towards falsehood (Batil). Therefore, Hanif is said of someone who is on the straight path, but idolaters used it for themselves, and polytheists were also called Hunafa.

This Ayah, by bringing the word Musliman next to the word Hanifan, has both purified the reputation of Abraham from the taint of polytheism (Shirk) and purified the reputation of this sacred word from the taint of the polytheists. [66]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, in interpreting the phrase Hanifan Musliman, said: «خالصاً مخلصاً لیس فیه شى ء من عبادة الاوثان» meaning Abraham was a pure and chosen person in whom there was not an atom of idol worship. [67]

66) Hadrat Ali عليه السلام said: The religion of Abraham was the same as the religion of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله. Bihar, Vol. 12, p. 11.

67) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 15.

Ayah

إِنَّ أَوْلَى ٱلنَّاسِ بِإِبْرَٰهِيمَ لَلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوهُ وَهَـٰذَا ٱلنَّبِىُّ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَلِىُّ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٦۸)
Indeed the nearest of all people to Abraham are those who follow him and this prophet and those who have faith, and Allah is the guardian of the faithful. (68)

Key Points

This Ayah clarifies that the bond of faith is superior and stronger than the bond of kinship. Individuals who share the same thought, path, and goal are closer to one another than those who are outwardly relatives but are separated in terms of thought and belief. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said to one of his loyal companions: «انتم واللّه من آل محمّد» (By Allah, you are from the Household of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله). He then recited the above Ayah. [68] The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله also said concerning Salman al-Farsi: «سلمان منّا اهل البیت» [69] (Salman is from us, the People of the Household). Although the Ayah stated that the closest people to Abraham are his followers, it mentioned the name of the Prophet of Islam and the Muslims separately, in order to show the world the best example of followership in the person of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims. It is narrated in a tradition: «انّ ولىّ محمّد مَن اطاع اللّه و انْ بَعُدتْ لَحمَته، و انّ عدوّ محمّد مَن عَصى اللّه و انْ قَرُبتْ قَرابته» [70] (Verily, the supporter of Muhammad is he who obeys Allah, even if his kinship is distant, and the enemy of the Prophet is he who disobeys Allah, even if his relationship is close). The friend and supporter of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله is he who obeys Allah, even if he is distant from him in terms of family, and the enemy of the Prophet is he who disobeys Allah, even if he has a relationship and closeness to the Prophet. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of «الذّین آمنوا» (those who believed) in the Ayah refers to the Imams and their followers. [71]

68) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 69) Bihar, Vol. 10, p. 123. 70) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 71) Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 416.

Messages

1- The bond between the people and the leader is an ideological bond, not a tribal, linguistic, regional, or racial bond. «انّ أولَى النّاس بابراهیم لَلّذین اتّبعوه»

2- The criterion for closeness to the Prophets is obedience to them. «أولَى النّاس بابراهیم لَلّذین اتّبعوه»

3- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله and the Muslims are on the path of Abraham, sharing the same ideology and goal as him. «أولَى النّاس بابراهیم... وهذا النّبى»

Ayah

وَدَّت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَوْ يُضِلُّونَكُمْ وَمَا يُضِلُّونَ إِلَّآ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَمَا يَشْعُرُونَ (٦۹)
A group of the People of the Book were eager to lead you astray; yet they lead no one astray except themselves, but they are not aware. (69)

Key Points

We see the portrait of cultural invasion and the conspiracies of the enemy to de-religionize Muslims in this Ayah and the three Ayahs that follow. This Ayah reports on doctrinal grudges, the next Ayah reports on obstinacy and disbelief (kufr), the seventy-first Ayah reports on the method of concealing the truth and conscious suppression, and the seventy-second Ayah reports on the deceptive tactic of the enemy. Collectively, this constitutes a cultural invasion arising from a dark inner self, employing methods of concealment, questioning, and weakening and destabilizing the beliefs of the masses.

Messages

1- Allah exposes the plots of the enemies of religion and disgraces them. «ودّت طائفة...»

2- Knowing the enemy and their plans is necessary to remain safe from potential harm. «ودّت طائفة...»

3- The danger of intellectual and cultural invasion is serious. «ودّت طائفة...»

4- Do not trust the hypocritical statements of the opponents; they inwardly desire your deviation. «ودّت طائفة...»

5- Do not forget fairness in judgments. A group of the People of the Book harbors such a wish, not all of them. «ودّت طائفة من اهل الكتاب»

6- You must act in a way that ensures the enemy takes the wish for your deviation to the grave. The word «لو» (if/would that) is used for impossible scenarios. «لو یضلّونكم»

7- Those who seek to mislead others first commit deceit, hypocrisy, malice, slander, and conspiracy themselves. «و مایضلّون الاّ انفسهم»

8- The desire to mislead others is itself a great moral deviation. «ودّت... و مایضلّون الاّ انفسهم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَنتُمْ تَشْهَدُونَ (۷٠)
O People of the Book! Why do you deny Allah’s signs while you testify [to their truth]? (70)

Key Points

Apparently, this Ayah refers to the glad tidings that the People of the Book had read in the Torah and the Gospel concerning Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, but they ignored all those Divine signs in order to preserve their social standing and material interests.

Messages

1- Appealing to the conscience is the best method of invitation (Da'wah). «لِمَ تكفرون...»

2- Mere knowledge is not sufficient; acceptance is also necessary. «تكفرون... تشهدون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِمَ تَلْبِسُونَ ٱلْحَقَّ بِٱلْبَـٰطِلِ وَتَكْتُمُونَ ٱلْحَقَّ وَأَنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۷۱)
O People of the Book! Why do you mix the truth with falsehood, and conceal the truth while you know [it]? (71)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "و انتم تعلمون" (while you know): It means that the People of the Book are aware of the attributes mentioned for the Prophet in the Torah, (but they conceal it).[72] Many of those who distort the truth, under the guise of being Islamic scholars, orientalists, historians, and tourists, manipulated books and the recording of historical places and times. In encyclopedias, acting as researchers, they painted such a portrait of Islam that the reader would not contemplate faith or reflect upon Islamic beliefs.

72) Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 105.

Messages

1- Talbīs (confusing the truth), making things ambiguous, and concealing the truth are two powerful levers used by the enemy to create deviation among the believers. In the two previous verses, He said: "یضلّونكم..." (they lead you astray...). In this verse, He says: "تلبسون" (you confuse), "تكتمون" (you conceal).

2- Concealing the truth is forbidden (harām), and manifesting it is obligatory (wājib). "تكتمون الحقّ..." (you conceal the truth...).

Ayah

وَقَالَت طَّآئِفَةٌ مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَجْهَ ٱلنَّهَارِ وَٱكْفُرُوٓا۟ ءَاخِرَهُۥ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ (۷۲)
A group of the People of the Book say, ‘Believe in what has been sent down to the faithful at the beginning of the day, and disbelieve at its end, so that they may turn back [from their religion].’ (72)

Key Points

Twelve Jewish scholars decided that, in order to create instability and doubt among the Muslims, they would come to the presence of the Prophet Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله in the morning and declare their faith, but then abandon Islam at the end of the day and say: "We saw Muhammad and his religion, but it does not conform to what is stated in the Torah and the Gospel." With this cunning plan, they wanted to make it appear to their co-religionists that if Islam were a good school of thought, the people of knowledge and the Book would not have abandoned it. By doing this, they aimed both to create doubt among the Muslims and to prevent other Jews from converting to Islam. Allah the Almighty also exposed their plot by revealing this Ayah. A narration from Imam Sadiq علیه السلام states: When the Messenger of Allah علیه السلام announced the command to change the Qibla from Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem) to the Ka'bah during the noon prayer, the Jews said: "Believe in what was [commanded] at the beginning of the day, but disbelieve in what came at the end of the day (the change of Qibla), so that they may return from that Qibla and pray towards Bayt al-Maqdis." [73]

73) Mustadrak, vol. 3, p. 169.

Messages

1- Enemies plan to mislead Muslims. In Ayah 69, we read that the People of the Book wish to mislead you; this Ayah addresses their plot to achieve that goal. «یضلّونكم... قالت طائفة»

2- It is possible that a group, in the name of Islam, infiltrates the ranks of Muslims and stabs them in the back; therefore, one must be vigilant. «آمنوا... واكفروا... لعلّهم یرجعون»

3- A Muslim should not be naive or gullible, trusting every declaration of faith. «آمنوا... واكفروا»

4- In sensitive stages, Allah unveils the secrets and conspiracies of the enemies. «آمنوا... واكفروا»

5- We must reach a stage of faith where the apostasy of some Muslims does not cause our own instability. «واكفروا آخره لعلهم یرجعون»

6- One of the enemies' policies is to establish a connection and then sever it, with the aim of creating instability in society. «لعلّهم یرجعون»

Ayah

وَلَا تُؤْمِنُوٓا۟ إِلَّا لِمَن تَبِعَ دِينَكُمْ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْهُدَىٰ هُدَى ٱللَّهِ أَن يُؤْتَىٰٓ أَحَدٌ مِّثْلَ مَآ أُوتِيتُمْ أَوْ يُحَآجُّوكُمْ عِندَ رَبِّكُمْ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ وَٰسِعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۷۳)
And [they say,] ‘Do not believe anyone except him who follows your religion.’ Say, ‘Indeed [true] guidance is the guidance of Allah.’ [They also say.] ‘[Do not believe] that anyone may be given the like of what you were given, or that he may argue with you before your Lord.’ Say, ‘Indeed all grace is in Allah’s hand; He grants it to whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-bounteous, all-knowing. (73)

Key Points

The People of the Book were worried about the inclination of the Jews towards Islam and campaigned to prevent it. «لا تؤمنوا الا لمَن تبع دینكم»

Messages

1- The enemies of Islam recommend secrecy in their plots and do not trust anyone outside their own group. «لا تؤمنوا الا لمَن تبع دینكم»[74]

2- Divine guidance has been a continuous process throughout history and is not exclusive to any specific nation. «ان الهدى هدى اللّه»

3- Fanaticism and self-superiority are among the afflictions of religiosity. «الاّ لمَن تبع دینكم»

4- The favors of the Lord are not restricted to a specific group. Prophethood and guidance are Divine grace which He grants to whomever He wills. «انّ الفضل بید اللّه»

5- The selection of Prophets by Allah is based on His knowledge. «واسع علیم»

74) One of the meanings of Iman (faith) is trust and assurance.

Ayah

يَخْتَصُّ بِرَحْمَتِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (۷٤)
He singles out for His mercy whomever He wishes, and Allah is dispenser of a great grace.’ (74)

Key Points

This verse negates the false notion of the Jews that Allah does not place any nation under His favor like He favors the Jews, and states: Allah knows best whom to entrust with His mission (Risalah). He chooses the most worthy individual among His servants and places him under His special favor. Allah, both His favor is expansive and His wisdom is selective.

Ayah

۞ وَمِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ مَنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِقِنطَارٍ يُؤَدِّهِۦٓ إِلَيْكَ وَمِنْهُم مَّنْ إِن تَأْمَنْهُ بِدِينَارٍ لَّا يُؤَدِّهِۦٓ إِلَيْكَ إِلَّا مَا دُمْتَ عَلَيْهِ قَآئِمًا ۗ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ قَالُوا۟ لَيْسَ عَلَيْنَا فِى ٱلْأُمِّيِّـۧنَ سَبِيلٌ وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۷٥)
Among the People of the Book is he who if you entrust him with a quintal will repay it to you, and among them is he who, if you entrust him with a dinar will not repay it to you unless you stand persistently over him. That is because they say, ‘We have no obligation to the non-Jews.’ But they attribute lies to Allah, and they know [it]. (75)

Key Points

It is stated in the narrations that you must return the trust (deposit) to its owner, even if the owner of the trust is a transgressor (fāsiq).[75]

75) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān, Vol. 2, p. 778.

Messages

1- Observe fairness regarding your opponents and do not consider everyone a traitor. «من اهل الكتاب... یؤدّه الیك»

2- Trustworthiness is a criterion for evaluating individuals. «یؤدّه... لایؤدّه»

3- Moral values are stable. Preserving a trust is good in the view of everyone, and betraying it is ugly regardless of who the betrayal is against. Returning a trust is a value, even if it comes from the Jews. «یؤدّه... لایؤدّه»

4- Uprising and resistance are necessary to secure one's right. «لایؤدّه الیك الا ما دمت علیه قائماً»

5- Exploitation, deception (making others foolish), and racism are forbidden. «لیس علینا فى الاُمّیین سبیل»

6- The Jews and Christians considered themselves intellectual, and the Muslims illiterate (unlettered) and ummī. «لیس علینا فى الامیّین سبیل»

7- Worse than the sin itself is justifying the sin. The Jews wrongfully consumed people's wealth and claimed: Allah is pleased with this action. «یقولون على اللّه الكذب»

Ayah

بَلَىٰ مَنْ أَوْفَىٰ بِعَهْدِهِۦ وَٱتَّقَىٰ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۷٦)
Yes, whoever fulfills his commitments and is wary of Allah—Allah indeed loves the Godwary. (76)

Key Points

Fulfillment of the covenant is necessary in all the following cases: A: The covenant that Allah has made with humans through innate nature (Fitrah) or through the Prophets. [76] B: The covenant that a person makes with Allah. [77] C: The covenant that a person makes with people. [78] D: The covenant of the leader with the community (Ummah) and vice versa. [79]

76) ألم أعهد الیكم یا بنى آدم...» Yasin, 60. 77) و منهم من عاهد اللّه لئن آتانا من فضله...» Tawbah, 75. 78) و الموفون بعهدهم اذا عاهدوا...» Baqarah, 177. 79) الذین عاهدت منهم ثم ینقضون عهدهم» Anfal, 56.

Messages

1- Take a stand against incorrect thoughts. «بلى» [80]

2- Piety (Taqwa) safeguards a person from lying against Allah and violating the rights of people. «یقولون... الكذب... بلى من اوفى و اتّقى»

3- Slogans are not effective; action and piety are necessary. «اوفى واتّقى»

4- Fulfillment of the covenant is among the signs of piety. «اوفى واتّقى»

5- Fulfillment of the covenant and piety are the criteria for being loved (by Allah), not merely being People of the Book. «یحبّ المتّقین»

80) The word «بلى» (Bala) is generally used in cases where it refutes a previous logic or thought.

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَشْتَرُونَ بِعَهْدِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَيْمَـٰنِهِمْ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَا خَلَـٰقَ لَهُمْ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ وَلَا يُكَلِّمُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَلَا يَنظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَلَا يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۷۷)
There shall be no share in the Hereafter for those who sell Allah’s covenant and their oaths for a paltry gain, and on the Day of Resurrection Allah will not speak to them nor will He [so much as] look at them, nor will He purify them, and there is a painful punishment for them. (77)

Key Points

This Ayah threatens those who break covenants with five types of Divine wrath: lack of share in the Hereafter, deprivation of Divine address, deprivation of His merciful gaze, deprivation of purification from sin, and affliction with painful torment. It is narrated in Shi'a and Sunni traditions that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «لا ایمان لِمَن لااَمان له و لا دین لِمَن لاعهد له» (He who does not observe trust has no faith, and he who is not loyal to his covenant has no religion.) [81] In some narrations, it is stated that the meaning of «عهد اللّه» (the Covenant of Allah) refers to the truths concerning the Prophet of Islam in the Torah, which were distorted by Jewish scholars. Let us compare the situation of those who are greeted with peace by Allah; «سلامٌ قولاً من ربّ رحیم» [82] (Peace—a word from a Merciful Lord) with those to whom Allah will not speak; «لایكلّمهم اللّه» (Allah will not speak to them), and if they utter a cry, they will be silenced with the phrase «اخسئوا فیها ولاتكلمون» [83] (Be humiliated therein and do not speak to Me). The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever unjustly consumes the property and wealth of his brother through an oath is subject to Divine wrath. He then recited this Ayah. [84] Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The meaning of Allah's gaze on the Day of Judgment; «لاینظر الیهم» (He will not look at them) is a gaze of mercy (not a look with an eye). [85]

81) Tafsir Maraghi and Nur al-Thaqalayn. 82) Ya Sin, 58. 83) Al-Mu'minun, 108. 84) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 85) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 293.

Messages

1- The trust placed by people with an individual is a Divine covenant. In the previous verses, we read that some people do not return the trusts of others; in this Ayah, the term «عهداللّه» (Covenant of Allah) is used instead of the people's trust.

2- Breaking covenants is among the major sins. This type of successive threat is not mentioned in the Qur'an regarding any other sin. «یشترون بعهد اللّه... لا خلاق لهم...»

3- False oaths lead to deprivation on the Day of Judgment. «یشترون... ایمانهم»

4- Worldliness is the root of covenant-breaking. «ثمناً»

5- Whatever the price of breaking a covenant, it is little. «ثمناً قلیلاً»

6- The punishments of the Day of Judgment are commensurate with our own actions. Our disregard for Divine commitments causes Allah's disregard for us. «لا یكلّمهم، لا ینظر الیهم، لایزكّیهم»

7- The punishments of the Hereafter are both physical and spiritual. They include both humiliation; «لایكلّمهم اللّه» and torture and penalty. «ولهم عذاب الیم»

Ayah

وَإِنَّ مِنْهُمْ لَفَرِيقًا يَلْوُۥنَ أَلْسِنَتَهُم بِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِتَحْسَبُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَمَا هُوَ مِنَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ وَيَقُولُونَ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۷۸)
There is a group of them who alter their voice while reading out a text [that they have themselves authored], so that you may suppose it to be from the Book, though it is not from the Book, and they say, ‘It is from Allah,’ though it is not from Allah, and they attribute lies to Allah, and they know [it]. (78)

Key Points

The sin of scholars and knowledgeable people lacking piety is multiplied (several times greater), because: A: They mislead people. «لتحسبوه من الكتاب» B: They attribute lies to Allah. «هو من عنداللّه» C: They carry out all these actions knowingly. «وهم یعلمون»

Messages

1- In judgments, we must be fair and not view everyone the same way. «وانّ منهم لفریقاً...»

2- The presence of treacherous scholars among the People of the Book is certain. «لفریقاً»

3- Demagoguery (deceiving the masses) and verbosity are among the sins of scholars. «یلوون السنتهم»

4- Eloquent speech, if not aligned with the path of truth, becomes a means of deviation. «یلوون السنتهم»

5- Misleaders use religious sanctities and heavenly books as their pretext. «بالكتاب»

6- Syncretism (mixing truth and falsehood) is one of the enemy's poisonous cultural weapons. «لتحسبوه من الكتاب»

7- If the enemy sees the potential for you to accept deviation, they will raise more dangerous claims. «لتحسبوه من الكتاب ...یقولون هو من عنداللّه»

8- The greatest betrayal of human culture and belief is the conscious and malicious distortion carried out by scholars and the elite. «یقولون على اللّه الكذب و هم یعلمون»

Ayah

مَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَن يُؤْتِيَهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحُكْمَ وَٱلنُّبُوَّةَ ثُمَّ يَقُولَ لِلنَّاسِ كُونُوا۟ عِبَادًا لِّى مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِن كُونُوا۟ رَبَّـٰنِيِّـۧنَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تُعَلِّمُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَبِمَا كُنتُمْ تَدْرُسُونَ (۷۹)
It does not behoove any human that Allah should give him the Book, judgement and prophethood, and then he should say to the people, ‘Be my servants instead of Allah.’ Rather [he would say], ‘Be a godly people, because of your teaching the Book and because of your studying it.’ (79)

Key Points

The distinction of the Prophets over others is that no matter how much their followers and power increase, they invite people more intensely to the servitude of Allah, they never change the path of Monotheism (Tawhid), and their emphasis and warnings become stronger. However, self-serving callers initially begin their work with a pious and compassionate facade, but after reaching the seat of power, they establish tyranny and self-centeredness.

It is narrated in traditions that you should not call your slaves ‘abdī (my slave), but rather call them fatā (young man), and slaves should not call their master Rabbī (my Lord), but rather call him Sayyidī (my master).[86]

If the Prophets should not be worshipped, the status of others is clear. If spiritual blessings do not become a cause for servitude (to Allah), the status of material glamour is obvious.

Rabbāniyyīn (plural of Rabbānī) refers to someone whose connection with Allah is firm and who is concerned with the reform and education of others.

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Two groups perished concerning me: 1. The extremist followers, who raised me above the human limit. 2. The unfair enemies, who do not accept my prophethood. And I am disgusted with both groups.

Jesus عليه السلام had also given this warning to His followers, and we see a similar meaning in Nahj al-Balaghah from Imam Ali عليه السلام.

He also said: “Lā tarfa‘ūnī fawqa ḥaqqī fa inna Allāha ta‘ālā ittakhadhanī ‘abdan qabla an yattakhidhanī nabiyyan” (Do not raise me above my due status, for indeed, Allah the Exalted took me as a servant before He took me as a Prophet). Then He recited this Ayah.[87]

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said regarding this Ayah: There is no Muslim man or woman, free or slave, except that a mandatory right from Allah is upon him, and that is to learn the Quran and reflect upon it.[88]

86) Tafsir Qurtubi. 87) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 88) Mustadrak, Vol. 4, p. 323.

Messages

  1. Misuse of position, popularity, and responsibility is forbidden. (mā kāna li-bashar)

  2. The Book, wisdom, and prophethood do not remove a person from being human. (mā kāna li-bashar)

  3. The goal of the Prophets is not merely to save humanity from polytheism (Shirk), but to elevate them to the stage of becoming Rabbāniyyīn. (kūnū rabbāniyyīn)

  4. The path to becoming Rabbānī is religious knowledge and studying the Book, not Sufism and forty-day seclusion (chillah-nishīnī).[89] (rabbāniyyīn bi-mā kuntum tu‘allimūn...)

  5. If teaching, learning, and studying do not make a person Rabbānī, then that knowledge was not truly knowledge.[90] (rabbāniyyīn bi-mā kuntum tu‘allimūn...)

  6. The Rabbānī scholars are the true interpreters of the Quran. (kuntum tu‘allimūna al-kitāb bi-mā kuntum tadrusūn)

  7. The teaching and studying of the heavenly Books must be continuous. (kuntum tu‘allimūn... tadrusūn)

  8. Teaching is the profession of the Prophets. (tu‘allimūna al-kitāb)

89) Tafsir Fi Zilal al-Quran.

90) Tafsir Safi.

Ayah

وَلَا يَأْمُرَكُمْ أَن تَتَّخِذُوا۟ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ وَٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ أَرْبَابًا ۗ أَيَأْمُرُكُم بِٱلْكُفْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ أَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۸٠)
And he would not command you to take the angels and the prophets for lords. Would he call you to unfaith after you have submitted [to Allah]? (80)

Messages

1- Any invitation to polytheism (shirk), from whosoever it may be, is forbidden. «ولایأمركم ان تتخذوا...»

2- Kufr (disbelief) is not merely the denial of Allah; rather, accepting any kind of lord (Rabb) and considering any creature independent, is kufr (disbelief). «تتّخذوا الملائكة و النبییّن ارباباً أیأمركم بالكفر»

Ayahs

وَإِذْ أَخَذَ ٱللَّهُ مِيثَـٰقَ ٱلنَّبِيِّـۧنَ لَمَآ ءَاتَيْتُكُم مِّن كِتَـٰبٍ وَحِكْمَةٍ ثُمَّ جَآءَكُمْ رَسُولٌ مُّصَدِّقٌ لِّمَا مَعَكُمْ لَتُؤْمِنُنَّ بِهِۦ وَلَتَنصُرُنَّهُۥ ۚ قَالَ ءَأَقْرَرْتُمْ وَأَخَذْتُمْ عَلَىٰ ذَٰلِكُمْ إِصْرِى ۖ قَالُوٓا۟ أَقْرَرْنَا ۚ قَالَ فَٱشْهَدُوا۟ وَأَنَا۠ مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلشَّـٰهِدِينَ (۸۱)
When Allah took a compact concerning the prophets, [He said,] ‘Inasmuch as I have given you [the knowledge] of the Book and wisdom, should an apostle come to you thereafter confirming what is with you, you shall believe in him and help him.’ He said, ‘Do you pledge and accept My covenant on this condition?’ They said, ‘We pledge.’ He said, ‘Then be witnesses, and I, too, am among the witnesses along with you.’ (81)
فَمَن تَوَلَّىٰ بَعْدَ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (۸۲)
Then whoever turns away after that—it is they who are the transgressors. (82)

Key Points

The difference between the schools (teachings) of the Prophets is like the difference in the programs of two teachers or two governors who share a single direction regarding the fundamentals of scientific and political issues, but present diverse programs concerning minor issues due to the individual differences of the students or regional conditions. [91] Imam Ali عليه السلام said: Allah took a covenant from the previous Prophets that they should inform and give glad tidings to the people about the advent of the Prophet of Islam and his characteristics, and command them to affirm him. [92] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "جائكم رسول مصدّق" (a confirming Messenger comes to you) is the Prophet of Islam. [93]

91) Tafsir Maraghi. 92) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 93) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- In Divine management, taking a covenant is a prerequisite for entrusting responsibilities. "اخذ اللّه میثاق النبیین"

2- Wherever the task is difficult, taking a covenant is necessary. Abandoning an existing religion and tradition, and believing in and supporting a newly emerging person, is not easy. Therefore, Allah takes a covenant. "اخذ اللّه میثاق..."

3- The advent of the Seal of the Prophets صلى الله عليه وآله is certain; therefore, the covenant was taken from all the Prophets, not just some of them. "میثاق النبیین... جائكم رسول"

4- The flow of prophethood is a source of unity, not a cause for division or schism. "رسول مصدّق لما معكم لتؤمننّ به"

5- The Prophets share a common goal. Therefore, the previous Prophets gave glad tidings of the coming of the subsequent Prophets and had a covenant of faith and support regarding them, and the subsequent Prophets affirmed the previous Prophets. "مصدّق لما معكم لتؤمننّ"

6- The predecessors should acknowledge the successors, introduce them to the people, and prepare the ground for the growth of those who follow. "لتؤمننّ... لتنصرنّه"

7- Faith alone is not sufficient; support is also necessary. "لتومنن به ولتنصرنه"

8- There is a hierarchy among the Prophets, and the Seal of the Prophets is the most noble among them, because all Prophets must be believers in him and supporters of him. "لتؤمننّ به و لتنصرنّه"

9- Since the previous Prophets were obligated to believe in and support the Prophet of Islam, their followers must also believe in him and obey him. "لتؤمننّ به و لتنصرنّه"

10- Support is valuable when it originates from faith. "لتؤمننّ به ولتنصرنّه"

11- What is important is possessing the spirit of submission and accepting the truth, even if the necessary conditions may not arise in practice. The previous Prophets did not live to see the time of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله, but they possessed this spirit. [94] "لتؤمننّ به و لتنصرنّه"

12- The covenant-breaker is wicked (fasiq). "فمن تولّى... هم الفاسقون"

94) For example, being martyred is one issue, and readiness for martyrdom is another issue. Just as Allah did not want the blood of Ishmael (Isma'il) to be shed, but He wanted Abraham (Ibrahim) to have complete readiness to sacrifice his son. We may not live to see the time of the appearance of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, but the anticipation of the appearance, and the love, affection, familiarity, and readiness for His presence, is an issue that has been emphasized in dozens of verses and narrations.

Ayah

أَفَغَيْرَ دِينِ ٱللَّهِ يَبْغُونَ وَلَهُۥٓ أَسْلَمَ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ طَوْعًا وَكَرْهًا وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ (۸۳)
Do they seek a religion other than that of Allah, while to Him submits whoever there is in the heavens and the earth, willingly or unwillingly, and to Him they will be brought back? (83)

Key Points

The creatures of the heavens and the earth are all submitted to Allah. Even if they conceal this submission in normal circumstances, when they feel danger, they inevitably surrender their hearts to Him. Believers submit to Him willingly and voluntarily, but everyone submits to Him at the time of feeling danger, or death, or being defeated in war, or being unable to provide an argument (in debate). All particles of the universe, from the smallest atom to the largest planets, are all subject to the same law that Allah has decreed upon them, and He can change it at any moment. (95) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: When Al-Qa'im عليه السلام rises, all lands will proclaim the testimony of "لااله الاّ اللّه و محمّد رسول اللّه". (96)

95) While writing these sentences, I suddenly felt intense pain in half of my head and face for no particular reason. I put down the pen, placed my hand on my head and the painful area, and after a few moments, I suddenly calmed down. I said to myself: Yes, the entire existence, at every moment and in every form, is in the hand of Allah. The movement of every leaf and the writing of every word is impossible without His will. 96) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 183.

Messages

1- Choosing a path other than Allah's is incompatible with the movement of existence. «أفغیر دین اللّه یبغون و له اسلم»

2- The reality of religion is submission. «أفغیر دین اللّه یبغون و له اسلم...»

3- Man is constantly searching for a way and a method, and the inclination toward a school of thought is inherent within him. (The phrase «یبغون» indicates constant desire and inclination.)

4- If existence is submitted to Him, why should we not submit? «له اسلم من فى السموات والارض»

5- There are conscious beings in the heavens. «من فى السموات» (97)

6- If our ultimate return is to Him, why should we not turn toward Him from the very beginning? «الیه یرجعون»

97) The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: The meaning of «من فى السموات» is the angels. Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 254.

Ayah

قُلْ ءَامَنَّا بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا وَمَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَىٰٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَإِسْمَـٰعِيلَ وَإِسْحَـٰقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَٱلْأَسْبَاطِ وَمَآ أُوتِىَ مُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰ وَٱلنَّبِيُّونَ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ لَا نُفَرِّقُ بَيْنَ أَحَدٍ مِّنْهُمْ وَنَحْنُ لَهُۥ مُسْلِمُونَ (۸٤)
Say, ‘We have faith in Allah and in what has been sent down to us, and what was sent down to Abraham, Ishmael, Isaac, Jacob and the Tribes, and that which Moses, Jesus and the prophets were given by their Lord. We make no distinction between any of them, and to Him do we submit.’ (84)

Key Points

This Ayah, except for one or two words, is completely similar to Ayah 136 of Surah Al-Baqarah. The form of the work is not the essence; rather, the essence is the ultimate and final goal. In every era, the form of the work and the invitation of the prophets may differ. For example, Prophet Moses (عليه السلام) went to Pharaoh, or Prophet Solomon (عليه السلام) invited a ruler like Bilqis, but the ultimate goal is one. Just like the various movements of the infallible Imams (علیهم السلام). If a covenant was taken from past prophets to introduce and assist the future Prophet, the future Prophet is also commanded, representing all his followers, to declare his faith in the past prophets.

Messages

1- Express your faith. «قل»
