2- Faith in the prophets is alongside faith in Allah. «آمنا باللّه و ما انزل... والنبیّون»

3- We should not ignore the services of those who came before. «وما انزل على ابراهیم... و ما اوتى موسى»

4- The coming of the prophets is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «والنّبیّون من ربّهم»

5- Divine religion and guidance have always accompanied humanity. The prophets are like teachers in a school who are sequentially commanded to guide the people. «ابراهیم واسماعیل و...»

6- All prophets share a single goal. «لانفرّق بین احد منهم»

7- The superiority of some prophets over others; «فضّلنا بعض النبییّن على بعض» (98) never prevents our faith in all of them. «لانفرّق بین احد منهم»

8- The Prophet of Islam, by declaring his faith in the other prophets, invites their followers to unity. «لانفرّق بین احد منهم»

9- The Prophet of Islam is absolutely submissive to the Truth. «نحن له مسلمون»

10- Whoever makes a distinction between the prophets is, in reality, not submissive to Allah. «لا نفرّق... نحن له مسلمون»

11- Believers, in harmony with all creation, are submissive to Allah. «له اسلم من فى السموات و الارض... و نحن له مسلمون»

98) Isra, 55.

Ayah

وَمَن يَبْتَغِ غَيْرَ ٱلْإِسْلَـٰمِ دِينًا فَلَن يُقْبَلَ مِنْهُ وَهُوَ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۸٥)
Should anyone follow a religion other than Islam, it shall never be accepted from him, and he will be among the losers in the Hereafter. (85)

Key Points

From the phrase «یَبتَغ» (seeks), it is understood that the intent of the Ayah is those people who have heard of Islam, but abandoned it and went towards other schools of thought. These individuals will be losers on the Day of Judgment, and their religion will not be accepted. Considering Ayah 81, the acceptance of the religion of Islam is Allah's covenant taken from all previous prophets, and therefore, with the advent of the Prophet of Islam, anything other than it is unacceptable. «فلن یقبل منه»

In the previous Ayahs, a portrait of the principles of the school of Islam was drawn, the general outlines of which are: 1. A covenant was taken from all previous prophets to believe in the subsequent prophet. 2. The entire existence is submitted to Allah, and it is impossible to accept anything other than the Divine religion. 3. Followers of Islam believe in all prophets and heavenly books.

And in this Ayah, He explicitly declares: Whoever accepts a religion other than this one is unacceptable. Yes, after invitation, argumentation, Mubahalah (solemn imprecation), and declaring alignment with other heavenly beliefs and books, it is time for clarity and speaking without reservation.

Messages

  1. Islam abrogates all previous religions. «ومَن یبتَغِ غیر الاسلام دیناً فلن یُقبَل منه»
  2. Abandoning a comprehensive school of thought leads to eternal loss. «مَن یبتغ... من الخاسرین»
  3. The Hereafter is the manifestation of true losses. «فى الاخرة من الخاسرین»
  4. Choosing Islam is the secret and sign of human foresight. «فى الاخرة من الخاسرین»

Ayah

كَيْفَ يَهْدِى ٱللَّهُ قَوْمًا كَفَرُوا۟ بَعْدَ إِيمَـٰنِهِمْ وَشَهِدُوٓا۟ أَنَّ ٱلرَّسُولَ حَقٌّ وَجَآءَهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۸٦)
How shall Allah guide a people who have disbelieved after their faith and [after] bearing witness that the Apostle is true, and [after] manifest proofs had come to them? Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (86)

Messages

1- The more numerous and clearer the proofs of guidance are, the more dangerous is disbelief and disregard for them. «كیف یهدى... لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

2- Initial faith is not sufficient; the continuation of faith and a good ultimate outcome are necessary, because the danger of apostasy constantly threatens man. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم»

3- A group of Muslims apostatized during the time of the Noble Prophet. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم»

4- Turning away from the truth, logic, reason, the Messenger, and clear proofs (Bayyinah) is oppression (zulm) and causes one to exit the sphere of Divine favors. «لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

5- It is the tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that He does not guide the oppressors. «واللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین» (The phrase «لایهدى» indicates continuity and the Divine tradition.)

6- Repentance and return are very difficult for the apostate. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم... واللّه لایهدى...»

7- Unaware disbelievers are closer to guidance than knowledgeable disbelievers. «جائهم البیّنات واللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

8- Benefiting from Divine guidance or being deprived of it is in our own hands. «لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayahs

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ جَزَآؤُهُمْ أَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ لَعْنَةَ ٱللَّهِ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ وَٱلنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ (۸۷)
Their requital is that there shall be upon them the curse of Allah, the angels, and all mankind. (87)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا لَا يُخَفَّفُ عَنْهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابُ وَلَا هُمْ يُنظَرُونَ (۸۸)
They will remain in it [forever], and their punishment will not be lightened, nor will they be granted any respite, (88)

Key Points

Perhaps the curse of all people, even the disbelievers (kuffār), upon the apostates (murtaddīn) is because conscious consciences and sound innate natures (fitrahs) condemn this conscious turning away and disbelief. Perhaps it is also because the radiation of conscious disbelief spreads to generations and eras, burning many people in its fire.

We read of a similar curse from Allah, the angels, and the people in Ayah 159 of Surah Al-Baqarah concerning scholars who conceal the truths. Therefore, the sin of a scholar who conceals the truth and an apostate who consciously abandons faith is the same.

Messages

1- The angels react to the evil thoughts and deeds of humans. «علیهم لعنة اللّه و الملائكة...»

2- People must show a reaction against apostates and deviants. «علیهم لعنة... الناس»

3- Knowledge and awareness are effective in determining the amount and quality of divine punishment. «شهدوا انّ الرّسول حقّ و وجائهم البیّنات... لایخفّف عنهم العذاب و لاهم ینظرون»

Ayah

إِلَّا ٱلَّذِينَ تَابُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ وَأَصْلَحُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۸۹)
except such as repent after that and make amends, for Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (89)

Key Points

If a disbeliever becomes Muslim, the rule of "Al-Islāmu yajubbu mā qablahu" (Islam wipes out what came before it) cleanses all past deeds. But if an apostate repents and becomes Muslim again, he must compensate for all neglected duties, reform his thoughts and actions, and perform the missed prayers and fasts as qaḍā (make-up).

Messages

1- The path of repentance is open to everyone, even those who have apostatized. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم... الاّ الّذین تابوا و اصلحوا»

2- The sign of true repentance is the rectification of corrupt deeds. «تابوا... و اصلحوا»

3- Allah not only accepts repentance and forgives sins, but He also loves the repentant person and grants him His mercy. «غفور رحیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ بَعْدَ إِيمَـٰنِهِمْ ثُمَّ ٱزْدَادُوا۟ كُفْرًا لَّن تُقْبَلَ تَوْبَتُهُمْ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلضَّآلُّونَ (۹٠)
Indeed those who turn faithless after their faith, and then advance in faithlessness, their repentance will never be accepted, and it is they who are the astray. (90)

Key Points

Apostates (al-Murtaddīn) and those who turn away from the religion are of two types: A: The National Apostate (Murtadd Millī), whose parents were disbelievers (Kāfir) at the time of his conception, who expresses disbelief after reaching maturity, then embraces Islam, and subsequently abandons Islam again. Since this person was not born of Muslim parents, his punishment is light and mild. B: The Innate Apostate (Murtadd Fiṭrī), whose father or mother was Muslim at the time of his conception, who expresses Islam after reaching maturity, and then abandons his religion and creed and turns his back on Islam. Such a person faces a severe punishment, such as execution. Although the path of repentance (Tawbah) is not closed to anyone, a group should not misuse this sacred repentance. Those who abandon their faith and constantly increase their disbelief and obstinacy, and are not willing to repent except at the moment of death or the victory of the Muslims, the repentance of such individuals will not be acceptable, because repentance has manners, conditions, and a method that does not apply to them.

Messages

1- Faith is valuable only if it continues until the end of life. Faith that ends in disbelief is ineffective. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم»

2- Man is free (has free will); he can believe or disbelieve. He can remain steadfast in his faith or persist in his disbelief. He can repent or insist on sin. «كفروا بعد ایمانهم»

3- Every perfection or deviation is subject to increase or decrease. Increase in knowledge, such as: «زدنى علما» (99) Increase in guidance: «زادهم هدى» (100) Increase in misguidance: «ازدادوا كفراً»

4- Allah is the Acceptor of repentance, but some groups have lost the necessary conditions (for acceptance). No matter how skilled a surgeon is, if the patient lacks the conditions for a transplant, the work will not succeed. «لن تُقبَل توبتهم»

5- Insistence and persistence in disbelief cause deprivation from the acceptance of repentance. «ازدادوا كفراً لن تُقبَل توبتهم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَمَاتُوا۟ وَهُمْ كُفَّارٌ فَلَن يُقْبَلَ مِنْ أَحَدِهِم مِّلْءُ ٱلْأَرْضِ ذَهَبًا وَلَوِ ٱفْتَدَىٰ بِهِۦٓ ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ وَمَا لَهُم مِّن نَّـٰصِرِينَ (۹۱)
Indeed those who turn faithless and die while they are faithless, a world of gold will not be accepted from any of them should he offer it for ransom. For such there will be a painful punishment, and they will have no helpers. (91)

Key Points

| The world and the Hereafter have differences, including: 1. Grief and sorrow in this world spread from one person to others, but this is not the case in the Hereafter. 2. In this world, a person can solve his problem through an excuse, or expiation (kaffarah), or a lie, or a trick, or repentance (tawbah), or lamentation, but this is not the case in the Hereafter. 3. The bitterness of this world is subject to mitigation, and if a person becomes accustomed to it, its bitterness is reduced, but this is not the case in the Hereafter.

Messages

  1. Dying as a disbeliever is the greatest danger. «ماتوا و هم كفّار» And dying as a Muslim is the wish of the Prophets. «توفّنى مسلماً»

  2. On the Day of Judgment, worldly wealth is worthless and has no efficacy whatsoever. Even if it were made of gold and its quantity equaled the capacity of the earth, it would still be worth nothing. «مل ء الارض ذهباً»

  3. Faith (Iman) has a value far exceeding all material things. On the Day of Judgment, only faith can save a person. «مل ء الارض ذهباً»

  4. Dying in a state of disbelief eliminates the possibility of intercession (shafa'ah). «مالهم من ناصرین»

Ayah

لَن تَنَالُوا۟ ٱلْبِرَّ حَتَّىٰ تُنفِقُوا۟ مِمَّا تُحِبُّونَ ۚ وَمَا تُنفِقُوا۟ مِن شَىْءٍ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِهِۦ عَلِيمٌ (۹۲)
You will never attain piety until you spend out of what you hold dear, and whatever you may spend of anything, Allah indeed knows it. (92)

Key Points

The word "Birr" (بِرّ) refers to widespread goodness and blessing, and to land that is prepared for cultivation, farming, or habitation. Also, wheat (بُرّ), which is the food of all humans and various animals, is called "Burr." Considering the linguistic derivation of "Birr," which means the expansion of goodness, the Qur'an identifies faith, righteous deeds, Jihad, prayer, and fulfilling covenants as examples of Birr.[101] Everyone is advised to help one another in performing Birr: «تعاونوا على البرّ» (Help one another in righteousness). And in this Ayah [102], He also states that you will never attain this precious jewel unless you spend (infaq) from that which is pleasing to you and which you love.

Examples of the believers' spending (Infaq):

  1. Abu Talhah al-Ansari had the largest number of date palms in Medina, and his garden was his most beloved possession. This garden, which was located opposite the Prophet's Mosque صلى الله عليه وآله, had clear water. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله would occasionally enter the garden and drink from its spring. This beautiful and excellent garden yielded a huge income that people spoke about. When the Ayah «لن تنالوا البرّ حتى...» was revealed, he came to the Prophet and said: "The most beloved thing to me is this garden; I wish to spend it in the way of Allah." The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: "That is a good trade, well done to you. But my suggestion is that you give this garden to the poor among your family and relatives." He accepted and divided the garden among them.

  2. A guest arrived at the house of Abu Dharr. Abu Dharr said: "Since I am busy, you yourselves slaughter one of my camels and prepare food." They chose a lean camel. Abu Dharr became upset and asked: "Why did you not bring the fat, plump camel?" They replied: "We saved that for your future need." Abu Dharr said: "The day of my need is the day of my grave."[104]

  3. When Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام was being taken to her husband's house on her wedding night, a poor person asked the حضرت (Her Holiness) for an old dress. Fatimah Zahra عليها السلام, remembering the Ayah «لن تنالوا البرّ...», spent that very new wedding dress (in the way of Allah).

  4. Abdullah ibn Ja'far saw a black slave working in a garden. At mealtime, a dog entered the garden and sat before the slave. The slave gave it a morsel. The dog looked again, requesting food. The slave gave his food to the dog, morsel by morsel, until it was all gone. Abdullah asked him: "What will you eat yourself?" He replied: "I have no share today." He asked: "Why did you do this?" He said: "This dog came from far away and was hungry." Abdullah was astonished by the chivalry (futuwwah) of this slave. He bought the garden and the slave, freed the slave, and gifted the garden to him.[105]

In the narrations concerning this Ayah, we read that the path to attaining Birr is helping one's parents before they ask, even if they are self-sufficient.[106]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said to Mufaddal ibn Umar: I heard my father saying: "Whoever passes a year without giving us (the Imams), whether little or much, from his wealth, Allah will not look upon him on the Day of Resurrection, unless He forgives him. O Mufaddal! This is a duty that Allah has made incumbent upon the Shi'a when He says in His Book: «لن تنالوا البرّ...» Thus, we are Birr, piety, and the path of guidance."[107]

101) «لیس البِرّ اَن تولّوا وُجوهكم قِبل المَشرق و المَغرب و لكنّ البِرّ مَن آمَنَ بِاللّه والیَومِ الآخَر و الملائِكة و الكتاب و النَبیّین و آتَى المالَ على حَبّه ذوِى القُربى و الیَتامى و المَساكین و ابن السبیل و السائلین و فِى الرّقاب و اقامَ الصّلوة و آتَى الزّكوة و المُوفون بعَهدِهم اذا عاهَدوا و الصّابِرین فِى البَأساء و الضّرّاء و حینَ البَأس اولئك الّذین صَدَقوا و اولئك هم المتّقون» (Al-Baqarah, 177). 102) Al-Ma'idah, 2. 103) Tafsir Kabir and Majma' al-Bayan; Sahih Bukhari, Vol. 2, p. 81. 104) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 105) Tafsir Al-Manar, Vol. 3, p. 376, quoted from Ihya' al-Ulum. 106) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 157. 107) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 182.

Messages

  1. The only way to reach the station of the righteous is sincere spending from beloved things, not merely remembrance (dhikr) and supplication. «لن تنالوا البرّ...»

  2. In the school of Islam, the goal of spending (infaq) is not only poverty alleviation, but also the growth of the spender. Detachment from imaginary beloved things and the blossoming of the spirit of generosity are among the most important effects of spending for the spender. «لن تنالوا البرّ»

  3. Attachment to the world causes deprivation from reaching the station of Birr. «لن تنالوا البرّ»

  4. Individual happiness lies in the shadow of one's social and generous outlook. «لن تنالواالبرّحتّى تنفقوا»

  5. The most beloved thing to a human being is their "soul" (life). Therefore, martyrs who give their lives in the way of Allah reach the highest station of Birr. «تنفقوا مما تحبّون»

  6. Spend what you love, not what the poor love. Because the poor, due to the severity of their poverty, might be satisfied with trivial things. «ممّا تحبّون» (what you love), not «ممّایحبّون» (what they love).

  7. A person who has received divine training is not a slave to wealth and possessions, but a ruler over them. «تنفقوا ممّا تحبّون»

  8. In spending, quality is the principle, not quantity. «ممّاتحبّون»

  9. Islam is the school of loving humanity, not loving wealth. «تنفقون مما تحبون»

  10. In spending, there should be neither excess nor deficiency; spend only a part of what you love. «ممّا تحبّون»

  11. Love of wealth is inherent in every human being: «ممّا تحبّون». What is dangerous is the intensity of that love which prevents spending. «و انّه لحبّ الخیر لشدید»[108]

  12. Spending is important, even if it is small. «و ما تنفقوا من شى ء» Yes, a single yellow leaf on the water can become a ship for hundreds of ants.

  13. Since Allah sees our spending, why should we be negligent in its quantity and quality? Let us spend the best things. «فان اللّه به علیم»

108) Al-Adiyat, 8.

Ayahs

۞ كُلُّ ٱلطَّعَامِ كَانَ حِلًّا لِّبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ إِلَّا مَا حَرَّمَ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلُ عَلَىٰ نَفْسِهِۦ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تُنَزَّلَ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةُ ۗ قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِٱلتَّوْرَىٰةِ فَٱتْلُوهَآ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۹۳)
All food was lawful to the Children of Israel except what Israel had forbidden himself before the Torah was sent down. Say, ‘Bring the Torah, and read it, if you are truthful.’ (93)
فَمَنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ ذَٰلِكَ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ (۹٤)
So whoever fabricates a lie against Allah after that—it is they who are the wrongdoers. (94)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir) that the Children of Israel (Bani Israel) objected to the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, asking that if his teachings were in accordance with the teachings of previous prophets like Abraham, Moses, and Jesus علیهم السلام, then why did he not consider the meat and milk of the camel forbidden (haram)? This Ayah was revealed in response to them, stating that all foods were lawful (halal) for the Children of Israel, and only Prophet Jacob (Ya'qub) had forbidden certain foods upon himself. And the reason for this prohibition, as mentioned in some commentaries, was that when Prophet Jacob consumed camel meat, it caused him physical discomfort, so he abstained from this food [109]. The Children of Israel, however, assumed that this was a permanent religious prohibition (tahrim shar'i). «طَعام» (Ta'am) refers to something that has taste for the human palate, and the word «حِلّ» (Hill), in contrast to «عَقد» ('Aqd) (binding/contract), means freedom and release.

109) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 184.

Messages

1- The lawfulness (halal) of foods is a fixed principle in the divine religions. «كلّ الطعام كان حلاً»

2- Do not consider lawful things (halal) forbidden (haram) without a religious reason or prohibition. «حَرّم اسرائیلُ على نفسه»

3- The best type of argumentation is argumentation based on the beliefs and the divine book of the opposing party. «فأتوا بالتوراة»

Ayah

قُلْ صَدَقَ ٱللَّهُ ۗ فَٱتَّبِعُوا۟ مِلَّةَ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ حَنِيفًا وَمَا كَانَ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۹٥)
Say, ‘Allah has spoken the truth; so follow the creed of Abraham, a Hanif, and he was not one of the polytheists. (95)

Key Points

The term hanīf (حَنیف) refers to someone who has distanced himself from error and turned towards guidance and the straight path.

Messages

1- The People of the Book, who consider themselves followers of Abraham, must be monotheists, because he was not of the polytheists. «فاتّبعوا ملّة ابراهیم... و ما كان من المشركین»

2- Abraham's adherence to truth made his religion stable and firm. «فاتّبعوا ملّةابراهیم حنیفاً»

Ayah

إِنَّ أَوَّلَ بَيْتٍ وُضِعَ لِلنَّاسِ لَلَّذِى بِبَكَّةَ مُبَارَكًا وَهُدًى لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹٦)
Indeed the first house to be set up for mankind is the one at Bakkah, blessed and a guidance for all nations. (96)

Key Points

"Bakkah" means the place of crowding and gathering of people. One of the objections raised by the Children of Israel was why Muslims, despite the antiquity of Bayt al-Maqdis (Jerusalem), which was built a thousand years before Christ by Prophet Solomon (Sulayman), abandoned it and made the Ka'bah their Qiblah (direction of prayer). This Ayah replies to them, stating: The Ka'bah, from the very first day, was the first house of worship, and its antiquity is greater than any other place. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, paying attention to the phrase "اوّل بیت" (the first House), said: Since the people of Mecca gathered around the Ka'bah after its establishment, the right of priority belongs to the Ka'bah. Therefore, for the expansion of the Masjid al-Haram, the houses surrounding the Ka'bah may be demolished for the benefit of the Sanctuary (Haram). [110] Tafsir al-Mizan states: The narrations regarding "Dahw al-Ard" [111] are numerous, and no scientific evidence contradicting them has been found, nor do they conflict with the Quran. Imam Ali عليه السلام states in Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192: Allah tests all people, from Adam عليه السلام until the last person, with these very stones of the Ka'bah. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: Prophets Adam, Solomon (Sulayman), and Noah (Nuh) علیهم السلام also performed Hajj around this very Ka'bah. [112] In the Quran, various terms are used for the Ka'bah, some of which we mention: 1- The Ka'bah is the first House. "اوّل بیت" 2- It is the center for the stability and uprising of the people. "قیاماً للناس" [113] 3- It is the free House without an owner. "البیت العتیق" [114] 4- It is the place of gathering and a secure sanctuary for the people. "مثابة النّاس وأمنا" [115]

110) Wasā’il, Vol. 13, p. 217. 111) "Dahw al-Ard" means the spreading of the earth from beneath the Ka'bah. Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 189. 112) Tafsir Burhān, Vol. 1, p. 300. 113) Al-Ma'idah, 97. 114) Al-Hajj, 29. 115) Al-Baqarah, 125.

Messages

1- The Ka'bah is the first House established for the worship and supplication of people. "اوّل بیت وضع للناس"

2- The historical precedence of places of worship is one of the criteria for their value. "انّ اوّل بیت وضع..."

3- The goodness and blessing of the Ka'bah are not only for believers or a specific race, but for everyone. "وضع للناس"

4- Being for the people (public nature) is a value for the Ka'bah. "وضع للناس"

5- The Ka'bah is a source of guidance for mankind. "هدى للعالمین"

Ayah

فِيهِ ءَايَـٰتٌۢ بَيِّنَـٰتٌ مَّقَامُ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ ۖ وَمَن دَخَلَهُۥ كَانَ ءَامِنًا ۗ وَلِلَّهِ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ حِجُّ ٱلْبَيْتِ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَاعَ إِلَيْهِ سَبِيلًا ۚ وَمَن كَفَرَ فَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَنِىٌّ عَنِ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۹۷)
In it are manifest signs [and] Abraham’s Station, and whoever enters it shall be secure. And it is the duty of mankind toward Allah to make pilgrimage to the House—for those who can afford the journey to it—and should anyone renege [on his obligation], Allah is indeed without need of the creatures. (97)

Key Points

Mecca and the Kaaba are an exhibition of divine power and signs, and their history is filled with memories and narratives that are instructive and cautionary. In its construction, Abraham (عليه السلام) was the builder, and Ishmael (عليه السلام) was the laborer. Abraha's elephant-riding army, which came intending to destroy it, was annihilated by divine power through the Ababil birds. Its wall split open for his mother during the birth of Ali (عليه السلام), so that a baby could be born inside it who, in the not-too-distant future, would demolish the idols within it. Bilal, the black Abyssinian slave, called the Adhan atop it before the astonished eyes of the Meccan aristocracy, and a day will also come when the last Divine Proof will lean against its wall and raise the call for the salvation of humanity, inviting the world to Islam. Mecca is the city of divine security; whoever enters it is safe. Even if a criminal enters the Sacred Mosque (Masjid al-Haram), he cannot be harmed; he can only be put under duress until he is forced to leave. [116]

Next to the Kaaba is the Station of Abraham (Maqam Ibrahim). According to narrations, the Station of Abraham is a stone that was under the feet of Abraham (عليه السلام) when he was building the Kaaba and raising its walls, and the imprint of His foot remains upon it. The survival of this stone and the footprint upon it, centuries before Jesus and Moses (عليهما السلام), despite all the changes and transformations around the Kaaba due to attacks, floods, and destruction, is itself a sign of divine power.

"Hajj" means intention accompanied by movement, and "Mahajjah" refers to the clear, straight path that leads a person to the destination. Hajj in Islam means intending (to visit) the House of God and performing the related rituals. The Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) said to Ali (عليه السلام): "تارك الحج و هو مستطیع كافر" (The one who abandons Hajj while being capable is an unbeliever). And whoever procrastinates until he dies, it is as if he died a Jew or a Christian. [117]

Financial capability (Istita'ah) for Hajj does not necessarily have to come from the person himself; rather, if someone hosts another or pays the cost of his Hajj, that person becomes capable (mustati') and Hajj becomes obligatory upon him. [118]

Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) said regarding the "آیات بیّنات" (Clear Signs): The intention is the Station of Abraham, the Black Stone (Hajar al-Aswad), and the Hijr of Ishmael. And they asked Him whether the intent of "مَن دخله" (whoever enters it) refers to the Kaaba or the Haram (Sanctuary)? He replied: The Haram. [119]

A person asked Imam Sadiq (عليه السلام) about "مَن دخله كان آمنا" (whoever enters it shall be safe). He replied: The person who intends (to visit) this House and knows that this is the House which Allah has commanded to be visited, and recognizes the Ahl al-Bayt (عليهم السلام) as they ought to be recognized, he will be safe in this world and the Hereafter. [120]

116) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 226. 117) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 4, p. 368; Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 265. 118) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 266. 119) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 223. 120) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 540.

Messages

  1. In the House of God, there are many clear signs. Signs of sanctity and spirituality, memories of the Prophets from Adam to the Seal (of the Prophets), being the circumambulation site for Prophets and the Qibla for all worshippers. «فیه آیات بیّنات»

  2. Abraham (عليه السلام) holds a status such that His footprint has given value to Mecca. «مقام ابراهیم»

  3. Contrary to Wahhabi thought, if an inanimate object is placed near the friends of God (Awliya Allah), it gains sanctity. «مقام ابراهیم»

  4. One of the privileges of Islam is that it has designated one area of the earth as a secure zone, so that the weak and the strong may gather together. «مَن دخله كان آمنا»

  5. Obligations are a type of divine commitment upon the neck of man. «وللّه على النّاس حِجّ البیت»

  6. Hajj must be sincere and solely for Allah. «للّه على النّاس حِجّ البیت»

  7. Duty and responsibility are commensurate with ability: the individual's financial and physical ability, and the availability of external conditions. «من استطاع الیه سبیلاً»

  8. Capability (Istita'ah) is a condition for Hajj. [121] «من استطاع الیه سبیلاً»

  9. Denying Hajj and abandoning it leads to disbelief (Kufr). «و من كفر»

  10. The benefit of performing divine commands returns to man himself; otherwise, Allah has no need for our actions. «فان اللّه غنىّ عن العالمین»

  11. Allah invites, but He does not beg. «وللّه على النّاس حِجّ البیت... و من كفر فانّ اللّه غنىّ عن العالمین»

121) Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 267.

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِمَ تَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۹۸)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! Why do you deny the signs of Allah, while Allah is witness to what you do?’ (98)

Messages

1- A question accompanied by reproach, but delivered in a polite tone, is the best method of warning. «یا اهل الكتاب لِمَ تكفرون»

2- If we consider Allah to be a witness over our deeds, we will cease opposing Him. «لِمَ تكفرون... واللّه شهید»

3- The People of the Book had secret activities against the Muslims. «واللّه شهیدعلى ماتعملون»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَهْلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لِمَ تَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ مَنْ ءَامَنَ تَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا وَأَنتُمْ شُهَدَآءُ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ بِغَـٰفِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (۹۹)
Say, ‘O People of the Book! why do you bar the faithful from the way of Allah, seeking to make it crooked, while you are witnesses [to its truthfulness]? And Allah is not oblivious of what you do.’ (99)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the question concerned the disbelief of the People of the Book: "لم تكفرون" (Why do you disbelieve?). In this Ayah, the criticism is directed at the fact that if you yourselves do not believe and disbelieve in the divine signs, why do you prevent others from following the path of Allah?! "لم تصدّون عن سبیل اللّه" (Why do you hinder from the way of Allah?).

Messages

  1. The People of the Book create obstacles and cause disruption to prevent the progress of Islam. "لِم تصُدّون"

  2. Presenting Islam as crooked (or distorted) is one of the ways to fight Islam. "تبغونها عوجا"

  3. The enemies strive constantly to lead you astray. "تبغونها عوجاً"

  4. The enemies are aware of and bear witness to the truthfulness of your religion. "و انتم شهداء"

  5. The source of discord (fitnah) is misguided scholars. "وانتم شهداء"

  6. If we know that He is not heedless of us and our actions for a single moment, we will cease wrongdoing. "ما اللّه بغافل عمّا تعملون"

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تُطِيعُوا۟ فَرِيقًا مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ يَرُدُّوكُم بَعْدَ إِيمَـٰنِكُمْ كَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٠٠)
O you who have faith, if you obey a part of those who were given the Book, they will turn you back, after your faith, into faithless ones. (100)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries (122): A Jewish man named Shas ibn Qays, when he saw the purity and intimacy of the Muslims of Medina, especially the two tribes of Aws and Khazraj, became very distressed and said to himself: These two tribes used to fight each other, but now, under the leadership and management of Muhammad صلى الله علیه وآله, they live together in peace and harmony. If this situation continues, the existence of the Jews is in danger. He planned, took a group with him, and assigned a young man among the Muslims to provoke them by recalling the memories of the conflicts of "Bu'ath" – the place where the war between the two tribes had occurred. This young man, by executing this plan, stirred their emotions, and a severe conflict and dispute was about to flare up. The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله warned them with His calming words and informed them of the enemy's plot. They put down their weapons, embraced each other, and regretted their action. According to the commentators, in this incident, this Ayah and the two preceding Ayahs were revealed, which contain a rebuke and censure of the People of the Book and a warning to the Muslims.

122) Commentaries Majma‘ al-Bayan, Ruh al-Bayan, Ruh al-Ma‘ani, Kabir, Qurtubi, Maraghi, and Namuneh.

Messages

1- Following deviated scholars leads to apostasy. «ان تطیعوا فریقا...یردّوكم»

2- Action affects belief. Obedience to the deviated results in deviation in belief. «ان تطیعوا فریقا...یردّوكم»

3- Since obedience to the disbelievers leads to disbelief (Kufr), dissociation (Tabarri) and disavowal from them is necessary. «ان تطیعوا فریقا...یردّوكم»

4- The result of following foreigners is disbelief (Kufr) and a bad end. «یردّوكم بعدایمانكم كافرین»

Ayah

وَكَيْفَ تَكْفُرُونَ وَأَنتُمْ تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْكُمْ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱللَّهِ وَفِيكُمْ رَسُولُهُۥ ۗ وَمَن يَعْتَصِم بِٱللَّهِ فَقَدْ هُدِىَ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۱٠۱)
And how would you be faithless while the signs of Allah are recited to you and His Apostle is in your midst? Whoever takes recourse in Allah is certainly guided to a straight path. (101)

Messages

1- The grounds for deviation and disbelief are either the lack of a path or the lack of a guide. But with the existence of the Book, the Sunnah, laws, and a divine leader, why deviation anymore? «كیف تكفرون و...»

2- The existence of law alone is not sufficient to prevent disbelief and deviation; a leader is also necessary. «وفیكم رسوله...»

3- Reliance and trust in Allah and seeking aid from Him is the secret to immunity from all temptations and deviations. «من یعتصم باللّه فقد هدى»

4- The path of holding fast (i'tisam) to Allah is open to everyone. «ومن یعتصم باللّه...»

5- Turning towards anyone or any authority other than Allah is deviation. «و من یعتصم باللّه فقد هدى الى صراط مستقیم»

6- What is more important than movement is finding the straight path and moving upon it. «هدى الى صراط مستقیم»

7- Guidance is the definite result of holding fast (i'tisam) to Allah. «فقد هدى»

8- Holding fast to Allah is a serious movement coupled with conscious choice, the prerequisite of which is journeying with the friends of God (Awliya).[123] «من یعتصم باللّه فقد هدى»

123) In Ziyarat Jami'a Kabira, Imam Hadi علیه السلام says: «مَن اِعتصَمَ بكم فقد اِعتَصَم باللّه» Seeking refuge and intercession (tawassul) through the divine Awliya is, in reality, placing oneself under the refuge and protection of Allah.

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ حَقَّ تُقَاتِهِۦ وَلَا تَمُوتُنَّ إِلَّا وَأَنتُم مُّسْلِمُونَ (۱٠۲)
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah with the wariness due to Him and do not die except as Muslims. (102)

Key Points

Every perfection, such as faith (Iman), knowledge ('Ilm), and piety (Taqwa), has stages: the initial stage, higher stages, up to the stage of absolute perfection. In the Quran, we read: «ربّ زدنى علماً» [124] (My Lord! Increase me in knowledge.) And in the supplication of Makarim al-Akhlaq, we read: «بلّغ بایمانى اكمل الایمان» (O Allah! Bring my faith to its perfection.) In this Ayah, too, we read: «اتّقوااللّه حقّ تُقاته» (Observe the highest degree of piety.)

124) Taha, 114.

Messages

1- The believer must strive for a higher stage every day. «آمنوا اتّقوا اللّه» 2- True piety is achieved in the light of faith in the Origin (Mabda') and the Return (Ma'ad). «یاایّها الّذین امنوا اتقّوا اللّه» 3- Piety is the secret to a good outcome (Husn al-'Aqibah). «اتّقوا اللّه... تموتنّ... مسلمون» 4- Merely believing is not enough; remaining faithful is necessary. The beginning of the work is not very important; the end of the work is more important. «لاتموتنّ الاّ و انتم مسلمون» 5- Islam teaches us not only how to live but also how to die. «لاتموتنّ الاّ و انتم مسلمون» 6- Man plays a role in his own destiny and outcome. «لاتموتنّ الاّ و انتم مسلمون» 7- Islam pays attention both to the quality of piety and to its continuation until the end of life. «حق تقاته و لاتموتنّ الاّ و انتم مسلمون»

Ayah

وَٱعْتَصِمُوا۟ بِحَبْلِ ٱللَّهِ جَمِيعًا وَلَا تَفَرَّقُوا۟ ۚ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ نِعْمَتَ ٱللَّهِ عَلَيْكُمْ إِذْ كُنتُمْ أَعْدَآءً فَأَلَّفَ بَيْنَ قُلُوبِكُمْ فَأَصْبَحْتُم بِنِعْمَتِهِۦٓ إِخْوَٰنًا وَكُنتُمْ عَلَىٰ شَفَا حُفْرَةٍ مِّنَ ٱلنَّارِ فَأَنقَذَكُم مِّنْهَا ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ يُبَيِّنُ ٱللَّهُ لَكُمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ (۱٠۳)
Hold fast, all together, to Allah’s cord, and do not be divided [into sects]. Remember Allah’s blessing upon you when you were enemies, then He brought your hearts together, so you became brothers with His blessing. And you were on the brink of a pit of Fire, whereat He saved you from it. Thus does Allah clarify His signs for you so that you may be guided. (103)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says: The Quran is the Rope of Allah (Habl Allah).[125] And it is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: "نحن حبل اللّه" (We are the Rope of Allah); we, the Ahl al-Bayt, are the Rope of Allah (Habl Allah).[126] And in another narration, it is stated: Ali ibn Abi Talib عليهما السلام is the Rope of Allah (Habl Allah).[127] The unification of hearts is solely in the hand of Allah. "فالّفَ بینَ قلوبكم" (And He united your hearts). The Quran addresses the Prophet, saying: "لو اَنفقتَ ما فِى الارضِ جَمیعاً ما اَلّفتَ بینَ قلوبِهم" (Had you spent all that is in the earth, you could not have united their hearts). If you spend all the wealth of the earth, you cannot create affection between hearts without His will.[128]

125) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 176. 126) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 127) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 128) Al-Anfal, 63.

Messages

1- Unity and avoidance of division are a divine duty. "واعتصموا"

2- The axis of unity must be the religion of Allah, not race, language, nationality, and so forth. "بحبل اللّه"

3- Do not be heedless of the blessings and services of Islam. "اذ كنتم اعداء فالّف بین قلوبكم"

4- Unity is the factor leading to brotherhood. "فأصبحتم بنعمته اخواناً"

5- Union is a great divine blessing.[129] "فاصبحتم بنعمته"

6- Division and enmity are a precipice and a pit of fire. "شفا حفرة من النار"

7- The blessings of Allah are His signs (Ayat). "واذكروا نعمت اللّه...یبین اللّه لكم آیاته"

8- Remembering divine blessings is a factor for love and a precursor to guidance. "واذكروا نعمت اللّه... لعلكم تهتدون"

129) Division is ranked alongside heavenly and earthly punishments and is a divine retribution. Furthermore, in Islam, lying to create unity is permissible, while telling the truth that causes division is forbidden (Haram).

Ayah

وَلْتَكُن مِّنكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى ٱلْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (۱٠٤)
There has to be a nation among you summoning to the good, bidding what is right, and forbidding what is wrong. It is they who are the felicitous. (104)

Key Points

The placement of the Ayah concerning Amr bil Ma'ruf (enjoining good) and Nahi anil Munkar (forbidding evil) between two Ayahs that command unity and cohesion is perhaps because, in a fragmented and disintegrated social system, either the power to invite to goodness and virtue does not exist, or such invitations are ineffective and unworkable. Amr bil Ma'ruf and Nahi anil Munkar are carried out in two ways: 1. As a general and public duty that everyone must undertake according to their ability. 2. As a duty undertaken by an organized and cohesive group, which pursues it with authority. Just as if a driver violates traffic laws on the street, other drivers protest to him with lights and horns, and the traffic police also intervene to deal decisively with the offender. Many narrations have been reported regarding Amr bil Ma'ruf and Nahi anil Munkar. We refrain from quoting them all and suffice with this much: Ali عليه السلام said: If Amr bil Ma'ruf and Nahi anil Munkar are abandoned, good and virtuous deeds will cease, and the wicked and evil people will gain dominance.[130]

130) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 47.

Messages

1- In an Islamic society, there must be a group of inspectors and observers, approved by the system, who supervise the social conduct of the people. «ولتكن منكم امّة»

2- Reforming society and preventing corruption is impossible without cohesive authority and clearly defined responsibility. «منكم امّة»

3- The inviter to goodness (khayr) and virtue (ma'ruf) must be knowledgeable of Islam, knowledgeable of people, and knowledgeable of methods. Therefore, some members of the Ummah bear this duty, not all of them. «منكم امّة»

4- Inviting to goodness, enjoining good, and forbidding evil must be permanent, not seasonal or temporary.[131] «یدعون، یأمرون، ینهون»

5- Enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) precedes forbidding evil (Nahi anil Munkar). If the path for good deeds is opened, the opportunity for evil decreases. «یأمرون بالمعروف و ینهون عن المنكر»

6- Those who care deeply for the growth and reform of society are the truly prosperous (Muflihun), and indifferent recluses have no share in this prosperity. «اولئك هم المفلحون»

7- Prosperity (Falah) and salvation are not limited merely to one's own rescue and liberation; rather, the salvation and growth of others are also conditions for prosperity. «یأمرون، ینهون، اولئك هم المفلحون»

131) The imperfect verb (present/future tense) is a sign of continuity.

Ayah

وَلَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ تَفَرَّقُوا۟ وَٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتُ ۚ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱٠٥)
Do not be like those who became divided [into sects] and differed after manifest signs had come to them. For such there will be a great punishment (105)

Messages

1- Let us learn a lesson from the bitter history of the disagreements among those who came before us. «و لاتكونوا كالّذین...»

2- The root of disagreements is not always ignorance; rather, desires (hopes/lusts) also provoke discord. «واختلفوا من بعد ما جائهم البینات»

3- Disagreement and division not only shatter your power in this world but also cause you to be afflicted by the torment of Hell in the Hereafter. «لهم عذاب عظیم»

Ayahs

يَوْمَ تَبْيَضُّ وُجُوهٌ وَتَسْوَدُّ وُجُوهٌ ۚ فَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْوَدَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ أَكَفَرْتُم بَعْدَ إِيمَـٰنِكُمْ فَذُوقُوا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْفُرُونَ (۱٠٦)
on the day when [some] faces will turn white and [some] faces will turn black. As for those whose faces turn black [it will be said to them], ‘Did you disbelieve after your faith? So taste the punishment because of what you used to disbelieve.’ (106)
وَأَمَّا ٱلَّذِينَ ٱبْيَضَّتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ فَفِى رَحْمَةِ ٱللَّهِ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱٠۷)
But as for those whose faces become white, they shall dwell in Allah’s mercy, and they will remain in it [forever]. (107)

Key Points

Radiance and darkness of face on the Day of Resurrection are, in reality, the embodiment of the very states and spirits of man in this world. Those who accepted the guardianship (Wilayah) of Allah in this world, Allah takes them from darknesses to light, and they will appear on that Day with a luminous face. But those who submitted to the guardianship (Wilayah) of the Taghut, the Taghuts lead them from light to the darknesses of lust, division, polytheism (shirk), and ignorance, and they will be resurrected in blackness and darkness on the Day of Resurrection. In the Quran, disbelief (kufr) after faith (iman) is mentioned sixteen times, disbelief after Islam twice, calf-worship after God-worship three times, and denial after knowledge and clear proof (bayyinah) twenty-seven times. All these instances indicate the danger of apostasy (irtidad) for all of us, and constitute a serious warning.

Ayahs

تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱللَّهِ نَتْلُوهَا عَلَيْكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ ۗ وَمَا ٱللَّهُ يُرِيدُ ظُلْمًا لِّلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠۸)
These are the signs of Allah, which We recite to you in truth, and Allah does not desire any wrong for the creatures. (108)
وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُرْجَعُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (۱٠۹)
To Allah belongs whatever there is in the heavens and the earth, and to Allah all matters are returned. (109)

Key Points

The revelation of divine verses upon the Prophet is without diminution or excess, and is based on truth and reality. Likewise, the actions and reactions, the rewards and punishments of the nations, are in accordance with a set of stable principles and traditions (Sunan). Allah neither burdens His servants with obligations beyond their capacity nor does He treat the conditions, talents, and abilities of individuals equally in punishment and reward, as doing so would lead to injustice (zulm).

Injustice is committed by someone who either suffers from a deficiency, or cannot achieve his goal through rightful means, or is unaware of the evil and ugliness of oppression. None of these apply to the Glorious Allah. The God from Whom everything originates and to Whom everything returns, what need does He have to commit injustice?!

Messages

1- The revelation of the Divine Message upon the Prophet is the truth, not a fabrication of his mind or imagination. «آیات اللّه نتلوها علیك بالحق»

2- Being honored (white-faced) or disgraced (black-faced) on the Day of Judgment is a reflection of the beliefs, thoughts, and deeds of the people themselves, not an injustice committed by Allah. «و ما اللّه یرید ظلماً للعالمین»

Ayah

كُنتُمْ خَيْرَ أُمَّةٍ أُخْرِجَتْ لِلنَّاسِ تَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَتَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ ۗ وَلَوْ ءَامَنَ أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَكَانَ خَيْرًا لَّهُم ۚ مِّنْهُمُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمُ ٱلْفَـٰسِقُونَ (۱۱٠)
You are the best nation [ever] brought forth for mankind: you bid what is right and forbid what is wrong, and have faith in Allah. And if the People of the Book had believed, it would have been better for them. Among them [some] are faithful, but most of them are transgressors. (110)

Key Points

In Ayah 104 of this same Surah, a specific stage of Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar (enjoining good and forbidding evil), which was carried out by a special and official group, was mentioned. This Ayah refers to the general stage, which is a universal duty.

Messages

1- Being the best nation is not achieved through slogans; it is achieved through faith and Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar. «كنتم خير امّة... تأمرون...»

2- There is no good in a silent and fearful nation. «خیر امّة... تأمرون... تنهون»

3- Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar is so important that its performance is the criterion for the distinction of nations. «كنتم خير امّة»

4- Amr bil Ma'ruf wa Nahy 'anil Munkar is realized only when Muslims exist as a single nation (Ummah), meaning they possess sovereignty. «كنتم خير امّة»

5- Muslims are responsible for the reform of all human societies. «اُخرجت للنّاس»

6- Enjoining good without combating corruption yields little result. «تأمرون بالمعروف و تنهون عن المنكر»

7- Every individual of the nation must enjoin good and forbid evil. (Even a 9-year-old girl has the right to enjoin good and forbid evil upon the president.) «تأمرون... تنهون»

8- In Amr bil Ma'ruf, age, region, race, literacy, and economic or social status play no role. «خیر امّة... تأمرون... تنهون»

9- A Muslim must enjoin and forbid from a position of strength, not out of pleading or begging. «تأمرون»

10- Enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) precedes forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar). «تأمرون... تنهون»

11- Enjoining and forbidding can only be effective if it stems from faith. «تأمرون، تنهون، تؤمنون»

12- Allah invites the People of the Book to Islam. «و لو آمن اهل الكتاب»

13- Islam abrogates all previous religions. «ولو آمن اهل الكتاب»

14- Man is free in choosing his path. «منهم المؤمنون و اكثرهم الفاسقون»

Ayah

لَن يَضُرُّوكُمْ إِلَّآ أَذًى ۖ وَإِن يُقَـٰتِلُوكُمْ يُوَلُّوكُمُ ٱلْأَدْبَارَ ثُمَّ لَا يُنصَرُونَ (۱۱۱)
They will never do you any harm, except for some hurt; and if they fight you, they will turn their backs [to flee], then they will not be helped. (111)

Key Points

This verse is glad tidings and consolation for Muslims, [assuring them] that they are safeguarded under the shadow of faith, unity, and enjoining the good. Do not fear the threats of the enemy, for victory is with you, and the enemy is humiliated and abject.

Messages

1- Morale must be strengthened. «لن یضرّوكم»

2- Muslims are safeguarded under the shadow of faith. «لن یضرّوكم»

3- Your opponents strive for your destruction, but they will only achieve a fraction of their harmful goals. «الاّ اذى»

4- Whoever lacks faith lacks the spirit of resistance. «یولّوكم الادبار»

5- The Quran prophesies the flight and weakness of the People of the Book against the Muslims. «ان یقاتلوكم یولّوكم»

6- Those who are steadfast in faith and enjoining the good are the ones who achieve victory and put the enemy to flight. «كنتم خیر امّة... و ان یقاتلوكم یولّوكم الادبار»

7- Transgression (Fisq) is the cause of weakness on the battlefronts. «اكثرهم الفاسقون... و ان یقاتلوكم یولّوكم الادبار»

8- Belittle the enemy's power. «و ان یقاتلوكم یولّوكم الادبار»

Ayah

ضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلذِّلَّةُ أَيْنَ مَا ثُقِفُوٓا۟ إِلَّا بِحَبْلٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَحَبْلٍ مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَبَآءُو بِغَضَبٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَضُرِبَتْ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْمَسْكَنَةُ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ وَيَقْتُلُونَ ٱلْأَنۢبِيَآءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا عَصَوا۟ وَّكَانُوا۟ يَعْتَدُونَ (۱۱۲)
Wherever they are found, abasement is stamped upon them, except for an asylum from Allah and an asylum from the people. They have earned the wrath of Allah, and poverty has been stamped upon them. That, because they would deny the signs of Allah and kill the prophets unjustly. That, because they would disobey and commit transgression. (112)

Key Points

As mentioned in the narrations (Riwāyāt), this Ayah concerns the Jewish people (Qawm-e Yahūd) who, due to disobeying the command of Allah and the Prophets, incurred Divine wrath, and the seal of humiliation (dhillah) and wretchedness (maskanah) was placed upon them. Among their sins was the killing of the Prophets, which, according to the narrations, does not mean killing by the sword, but rather the divulging of their news and secrets, which led to the dominance of oppressive powers and their subsequent murder. [132]

132) Kāfī, Vol. 2, p. 371.

Messages

1- The result of disbelief (kufr) and Prophet-killing is humiliation and wretchedness. «ضربت علیهم الذلّة»

2- The Jews are always humiliated. Although they sometimes hold the reins of propaganda, economy, and politics, they occupy the worst position in terms of dignity (karāmah), honor ('izzah), popularity, and security. (Like thieves and thugs who create fear and earn huge incomes, but never enjoy a high status.) «ضربت علیهم الذلّة»

3- Strengthen the morale of the believers by describing the miserable condition of the opponents. «ضربت علیهم الذلّة»

4- The secret to honor ('izzah) lies in two things: faith from within in Divine power, and good relations from without with nations and peoples. «حبل من اللّه و حبل من الناس»

5- The path to salvation for the Jews is either believing in Islam; «حبل من اللّه» or entering into a covenant with the Muslims. «حبل من الناس»

6- Belief affects action. «ذلك بانهم كانوا یكفرون»

7- Numerous Prophets came for the Jewish people. «النبیین»

8- Do not forget the standards even concerning the greatest personalities. Although Prophet-killing is the greatest crime, the word «بغیر حق» (without right) signifies that the standard is the falsehood (bātil) of the act, not the person of the Prophet. «یقتلون النبیین بغیر حق»

9- Sin and transgression pave the way for greater sins such as disbelief (kufr) and Prophet-killing. «ذلك بما عصوا...»

10- What is worse than transgression and disbelief is persistence in disbelief and transgression. «كانوا یعتدون»

Ayah

۞ لَيْسُوا۟ سَوَآءً ۗ مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أُمَّةٌ قَآئِمَةٌ يَتْلُونَ ءَايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ءَانَآءَ ٱلَّيْلِ وَهُمْ يَسْجُدُونَ (۱۱۳)
Yet they are not all alike. Among the People of the Book is an upright nation; they recite Allah’s signs in the watches of the night and prostrate. (113)

Key Points

This Ayah and the following Ayah describe the good qualities of a group among the People of the Book, which consist of rising up in obedience to Allah, reciting the heavenly Book, prostration (sajdah), enjoining good and forbidding evil, and hastening in good deeds. Perhaps these very actions are the manifestation of the "Habl Allah" (Rope of Allah) mentioned in the previous Ayah. Just as the recitation of the Quran accompanied by the establishment of prayer is praised by Allah for Muslims, (133) the recitation of the heavenly Books, accompanied by worship and prostration, is also praised for the People of the Book. "yatloona... yasjudoona" (they recite... they prostrate). In this Ayah, Allah has pointed to the value of knowledge, action, continuity, vitality, love, and having direction. He has pointed to knowledge with the word "yatloona" (they recite), to action with the word "yasjudoona" (they prostrate), to continuity with the phrase "anaa'al-layl" (hours of the night), to love and vitality with the word "qā'imah" (standing/upright), and to direction with the phrase "Āyāt Allāh" (Signs of Allah).

133) انّ الذین یتلون كتاب اللّه و اقامواالصلوة وانفقوا مما رزقناهم سرّاً و علانیة یرجون تجارة لن تبور» (Fatir, 29).

Messages

  1. We must accept goodness and perfection from whatever group or faction it may come, and acknowledge it. Alongside criticizing others, we should not overlook their good qualities. "min Ahl al-Kitāb ummatun qā'imah"
  2. One of the ways to attract and invite others is to acknowledge and confess their perfections. "min Ahl al-Kitāb ummatun qā'imah"
  3. Worship requires resolve, seriousness, love, and readiness. "ummatun qā'imah"
  4. The recitation of Allah's Signs (Āyāt) and nocturnal prostrations are causes for divine praise. "yatloona Āyāt Allāh ānā'al-layl"
  5. The recitation of divine Signs and prostration before Him constitute rising up in obedience to the divine command. "qā'imah yatloona"
  6. The night, especially the pre-dawn hours (sahar), is the best time for intimate supplication (munājāt). "ānā'al-layl"
  7. The highest state of worship is prostration (sajdah). "wa hum yasjudoona"

Ayahs

يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱللَّهِ وَٱلْيَوْمِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ وَيُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْخَيْرَٰتِ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مِنَ ٱلصَّـٰلِحِينَ (۱۱٤)
They have faith in Allah and the Last Day, and bid what is right and forbid what is wrong, and they are active in [performing] good deeds. Those are among the righteous. (114)
وَمَا يَفْعَلُوا۟ مِنْ خَيْرٍ فَلَن يُكْفَرُوهُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۱٥)
Whatever good they do, they will not go unappreciated for it, and Allah knows well the Godwary. (115)

Key Points

Just as in Surah Al-Baqarah, Ayah 3, prayer (Salat) is mentioned alongside faith, «الّذین یؤمنون بالغیب و یقیمون الصلوة» (Those who believe in the unseen and establish prayer), in this Ayah too, enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf) and forbidding evil (Nahy 'anil Munkar) are mentioned alongside faith. «یؤمنون... یأمرون» The method of the Quran is to mention enjoining good before forbidding evil. Indeed, if the doors of good are opened, the path of evil is closed. Easy marriage prevents immorality (Fahsha). Prayer restrains man from immorality and evil. Proper employment can prevent social corruption. Giving dignity to individuals prevents them from falling into degradation. The righteous (Salihun) are those whom everyone wishes to join: «اَلحِقنى بالصّالحین» (Join me with the righteous) [134], and every Muslim greets them five times every day: «السلام علینا و على عباد اللّه الصالحین» (Peace be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah). In the divine perspective, no righteous deed is wasted. «فلن یُكفروه» (They will never be denied [its reward]). Of course, as we read in the Quran: «انّما یتقبّل اللّه من المتّقین» (Allah only accepts from the pious) [135]. The condition for acceptance is faith and piety (Taqwa). Therefore, He says here: We Ourselves know who the pious (Muttaqin) are, whose deeds must be accepted.

134) Yusuf, 108; Shu'ara, 83. 135) Ma'idah, 27.

Messages

1- Enjoining good and forbidding evil was also practiced in other religions. «اهل الكتاب... یأمرون بالمعروف» (People of the Scripture... they enjoin good).

2- The prostrations (Sujud) of the dawn must be accompanied by enjoining and forbidding during the day. «یسجدون... یأمرون» (They prostrate... they enjoin).

3- Speed in good deeds increases their value. «یسارعون فى الخیرات» (They hasten to good deeds).

4- The precedence and speed of the righteous in good deeds are not seasonal; they are permanent. «یسارعون» (They hasten).

5- Righteous individuals are those who enthusiastically and quickly pursue every good deed, not just some deeds. «الخیرات» (The good deeds).

6- Faith, worship, recitation (of the Quran), enjoining good and forbidding evil, and speed in good deeds place a person among the righteous. «اولئك من الصالحین» (Those are among the righteous).

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَن تُغْنِىَ عَنْهُمْ أَمْوَٰلُهُمْ وَلَآ أَوْلَـٰدُهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا ۖ وَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۚ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۱۱٦)
As for the faithless, neither their wealth nor their children will avail them anything against Allah. They shall be the inmates of the Fire and remain in it [forever]. (116)
مَثَلُ مَا يُنفِقُونَ فِى هَـٰذِهِ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا كَمَثَلِ رِيحٍ فِيهَا صِرٌّ أَصَابَتْ حَرْثَ قَوْمٍ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ فَأَهْلَكَتْهُ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمَهُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَلَـٰكِنْ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (۱۱۷)
The parable of what they spend in the life of this world is that of a cold wind that strikes the tillage of a people who wronged themselves, destroying it. Allah does not wrong them, but they wrong themselves. (117)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly declared that neither wealth, nor children, nor relatives, nor spouse, nor friend, nor master, nor anything else, is of the slightest use against the wrath of Allah. The word «صِرّ» (ṣirr) means scorching heat and cold. [136] The budgets that the disbelievers spend on falsehood are like sowing seeds in a field that is destroyed by a scorching wind. From the beginning of Islam until now, every conspiracy, aggression, and propaganda against Islam has met with failure, and the Divine religion has grown and expanded daily, and ultimately, victory belongs to Islam.

136) التحقیق فى كلمات القرآن.

Messages

1- Belief is effective in action. Disbelief causes deprivation from the blessings of charity (infaq). «انّ الّذین كفروا... مَثَل ما ینفقون»

2- One of the causes of natural disasters and calamities is human sins. «ریح فیها صِرّ اصابت حَرثَ قوم ظلموا»

3- Sin destroys good deeds. «ظلموا انفسهم فاهلكته»

4- Divine wrath is not injustice, but rather the reflection of the actions of the people themselves. «وما ظلمهم اللّه ولكن انفسهم یظلمون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَتَّخِذُوا۟ بِطَانَةً مِّن دُونِكُمْ لَا يَأْلُونَكُمْ خَبَالًا وَدُّوا۟ مَا عَنِتُّمْ قَدْ بَدَتِ ٱلْبَغْضَآءُ مِنْ أَفْوَٰهِهِمْ وَمَا تُخْفِى صُدُورُهُمْ أَكْبَرُ ۚ قَدْ بَيَّنَّا لَكُمُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ ۖ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ (۱۱۸)
O you who have faith! Do not take your confidants from others than yourselves; they will spare nothing to ruin you. They are eager to see you in distress. Hatred has already shown itself from their mouths, and what their breasts hide [within] is yet worse. We have certainly made the signs clear for you, should you exercise your reason. (118)

Key Points

The word biṭānah refers to the undergarment that clings to the baṭn (stomach). This word is a metaphor for an intimate advisor or confidant. The word khabāl means the destruction of thought and intellect. The word ʿanat means hardship and suffering, and the word yaʾlūn means to fall short in performing a task.

What does the enemy wish for us? If we look at the word wadda in the Quran, we find the enemies' wishes concerning us: 1. He wishes that we be heedless of our weapons and assets. «ودّالّذین كفروا لو تغفلون عن اسلحتكم و امتعتكم» (137) 2. He wants compromise and softness from us. «ودوّا لو تُدهِن فیُدهِنون» (138) 3. He wishes hardship, pressure, and difficulty for us. «وَدوّا ما عَنِتّم» 4. He wishes apostasy and turning away from the religion for us. «وَدّ كَثیرٌ مِن اهل الكتاب لو یردّونكم من بعد ایمانكم كفّاراً» (139)

137) Nisa, 102. 138) Qalam, 9. 139) Baqarah, 109.

Messages

  1. We should not make strangers our intimate advisors. «لا تتخذوا بطانة من دونكم» The presence of foreign advisors in Islamic countries is forbidden.

  2. Secrecy is a definite duty. «لاتتخذوا بطانة من دونكم»

  3. Even any Muslim who falls under the category of dūnakum (other than you) should not be made a confidant, because there are many mischief-makers and spies even among Muslims. «لاتتخذوا بطانة من دونكم...»

  4. The criterion for friendship and intimate relations between Muslims and other societies must be faith. «لاتتخذوا بطانة من دونكم»

  5. The Islamic society is superior to the non-Islamic society. Dūnakum instead of ghayrukum (other than you) indicates that non-Muslims are dūn (inferior/lower). «من دونكم»

  6. The enemy's first movement and danger is cultural invasion. The enemy does not fall short in striking at thought and intellect. «لایألونكم خبالاً»

  7. Enemies deal with us using various methods: 1. Corruption. «لایألونكم خبالاً» 2. Pressure. «ودّوا ما عنتم» 3. Hypocrisy. «ماتخفى صدورهم اكبر»

  8. Know your enemies and be vigilant; they do not fall short even slightly in plotting and mischief against you. «لا یألونكم خبالا ودّوا ما عنتّم»

  9. Divine commands and prohibitions have reasons and philosophies. If He prohibits you from intimate relations with enemies, it is because they do not fall short in plotting against you. «لاتتخذوا... لا یألونكم»

  10. What is in the jar is what pours out of it. Nothing is expected from the enemy except malice and hatred. «قد بدت البغضاء من افواههم»

  11. Recognize your enemies through their propaganda. «قد بدت البغضاء من افواههم»

  12. Intimate relations with a plotting enemy are a sign of irrationality. Dealing sincerely with insincere people is foolishness. «ما تخفى صدورهم اكبر... ان كنتم تعقلون»

  13. Allah is aware of everything within and exposes the plots of the enemies of Islam. «وما تخفى صدورهم اكبر»

  14. Your enemies are hypocrites; their outward appearance and inward reality are not the same. «ما تخفى صدرهم»

  15. By introducing the enemy's characteristics, Allah has completed the argument (proof) against you. «قد بیّنا الایات»

  16. Being a believer is one matter, and being rational is another matter. Therefore, in this Ayah, Allah tells the believers: Do not make the disbelievers your confidants, if you are rational. «یا ایها الّذین آمنوا... ان كنتم تعقلون»

Ayah

هَـٰٓأَنتُمْ أُو۟لَآءِ تُحِبُّونَهُمْ وَلَا يُحِبُّونَكُمْ وَتُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْكِتَـٰبِ كُلِّهِۦ وَإِذَا لَقُوكُمْ قَالُوٓا۟ ءَامَنَّا وَإِذَا خَلَوْا۟ عَضُّوا۟ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلْأَنَامِلَ مِنَ ٱلْغَيْظِ ۚ قُلْ مُوتُوا۟ بِغَيْظِكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۱۱۹)
Ah! You are the ones who bear love towards them, while they do not love you, though you believe in all the Books. When they meet you, they say, ‘We believe,’ but when they are alone, they bite their fingertips at you out of rage. Say, ‘Die of your rage!’ Indeed Allah knows well what is in the breasts. (119)

Messages

1- Diagnosing the enemy's morale and one's own true motivations is extremely precise. For this reason, the word «ها» (Hā) appears at the beginning of the Ayah for warning and admonition.

2- Friendship must be mutual; otherwise, it leads to humiliation, self-abasement, and feelings of inferiority. «تحبّونهم و لا یحبّونكم»

3- A Muslim believes in all previous Divine Books, even if others do not believe in the Quran. «تؤمنون بالكتاب كلّه»

4- Do not trust every declaration of faith. «قالوا امنّا و اذاخلوا...»

5- Do not imagine that your affection will reassure spiteful enemies regarding you. «تحبّونهم... عضّواعلیكم الانامل من الغیظ»

6- Inner motivations have external reflections. In the previous Ayah: «قدبدت البغضاء من أفواههم» (Hatred has already appeared from their mouths); in this Ayah: «عضّواعلیكم الانامل من الغیظ» (They bite their fingertips at you out of rage.)

7- The enemy does not feel Ghadhab (anger) toward you, but Ghayz (rage/fury). Ghayz is used in cases where a person's vessel is full of anger. «من الغیظ»

8- One of the Divine aids is the exposure of the enemy's true nature to the Muslims, so that they are not deceived and remain vigilant. «وما تخفى صدورهم اكبر... و اذا خَلوا عضّوا علیكم الانامل...»

9- Humiliate your enemies. «موتوا بغیظكم»

10- Sometimes psychological issues like envy and spite can drive a person to the point of death. «موتوا بغیظكم»

11- Islam's confrontation with malicious hypocrites is more severe than with straightforward disbelievers. «اذا لقوكم قالوا آمنّا... موتوا بغیضكم»

12- Sometimes declaring hatred and cursing is necessary. Spiteful disbelievers and cunning hypocrites deserve destruction. «موتوا بغیظكم»

13- Do not show affection to the disbelievers under the pretext of attracting them, because sometimes the real motive for affection is greed, fear, or self-abasement. «انّ اللّه علیم بذات الصدور»

14- Allah is aware of the secrets of the hearts. «علیم بذات الصدور» If we believe that Allah knows everything, we will resort less to trickery and deceit. Conspiring hypocrites should also know that Allah knows their actions and will respond at the appropriate time.

Ayah

إِن تَمْسَسْكُمْ حَسَنَةٌ تَسُؤْهُمْ وَإِن تُصِبْكُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَفْرَحُوا۟ بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تَصْبِرُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ لَا يَضُرُّكُمْ كَيْدُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ (۱۲٠)
If some good should befall you, it upsets them, but if some ill befalls you, they rejoice at it. Yet if you are patient and Godwary, their guile will not harm you in any way. Indeed Allah encompasses what they do. (120)

Key Points

This Ayah explains the way to recognize friends and enemies, the best way being attention to their attitudes and reactions during times of success or failure for Muslims. In foreign policy, attention to condemnations, endorsements, congratulations, denials, and various types of aid and propaganda is necessary. In the previous Ayahs, Muslims were advised neither to take enemies as companions nor as friends. This Ayah states: This stance might have a heavy price, and they might conspire against you. Therefore, you must possess patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa), so that their schemes do not harm you.

Messages

1- The envy of the enemies is so intense that if the slightest good reaches you, they become distressed. «ان تمسسكم حسنة تسؤهم»

2- The path for the enemy's infiltration is either through our fear and greed, or through our recklessness and lack of piety (taqwa). Patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) are the way to neutralize them. «ان تصبروا و تتّقوا لایضرّكم»

3- Against the envious people who are upset by our growth, there is no remedy except patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa). «ان تصبروا و تتّقوا لایضرّكم»

4- By exposing the enemy's attitudes, Allah both boosts the morale of the Muslims and serves as a warning (or wake-up call). «ان تمسسكم حسنة تسؤهم... لا یضرّكم كیدهم»

Ayah

وَإِذْ غَدَوْتَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ تُبَوِّئُ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ مَقَـٰعِدَ لِلْقِتَالِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ (۱۲۱)
When you left your family at dawn to settle the faithful in their positions for battle —and Allah is all-hearing, all-knowing. (121)

Key Points

A Portrait of the Battle of Uhud (Second Year of the Hijra). The Quraysh disbelievers, having suffered seventy dead and seventy captives in the Battle of Badr, were defeated and returned to Mecca. Abu Sufyan said: Do not weep over the dead, so that the grudges remain and the resentments are not emptied. All of you must raise the slogan of revenge, and I, too, will not sleep with my wife until I have taken revenge. The following year, the disbelievers of Mecca, with three thousand cavalry, two thousand infantry, and complete equipment, moved toward Medina intending to fight the Muslims. Abbas, the uncle of the Prophet, who had not yet embraced Islam and lived in Mecca, due to his great affection and love for the Prophet, sent the news of the disbelievers' movement and attack in a confidential letter to the Prophet via a man from the Banu Ghiffar tribe. As soon as the noble Messenger of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله was informed of the matter, He sent a group from Medina toward Mecca to gather more information. Upon their return, the agents confirmed the movement of the disbeliever forces led by Abu Sufyan.

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله held a meeting on Friday and consulted with the Muslims regarding this matter. Two theories were proposed in this session: 1. We should remain in Medina and take shelter in the streets so that everyone can help us. 2. We should leave Medina and fight outside the city. The second theory, which was accompanied by heroism and a display of bravery, attracted the youth and gained more supporters, resulting in the decision to leave the city. Although the blessed opinion of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله Himself was to remain in the city, He waived His own opinion out of respect for the feelings of the youth.

Then the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, accompanied by one person, left the city of Medina to prepare the camp and chose a location on the slopes of Mount Uhud that had good military conditions. Then, in the Friday prayer sermons, the Prophet informed the people of the situation, and after the prayer, He set off for the war camp with one thousand Muhajirin (Emigrants) and Ansar (Helpers).

The commander of this war was the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله Himself. He raised several banners, some of which were entrusted to the Muhajirin and some to the Ansar. The journey from Medina to the Uhud camp was on foot, and the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله reviewed His companions during the march and organized the ranks. During the inspection of the ranks, the Prophet saw new individuals and asked: Who are you? They said: We are from the Jews of Medina who have come to help you. After a moment of reflection, He said: We do not seek help from a polytheist to fight a polytheist. For this reason, three hundred people were subtracted from the thousand-man army of Islam. Of course, some have said that not all of these three hundred people were Jews, but there were also Muslims like Abdullah ibn Ubayy who separated along the way because the movement of the Muslims did not align with their opinion of taking shelter in the city. [140]

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله performed the Fajr (Dawn) prayer with seven hundred people in Uhud, and He appointed Abdullah ibn Jubayr, along with fifty skilled archers, to guard the sensitive mountain pass, strictly ordering them never to abandon this area. Abu Sufyan also assigned Khalid ibn Walid, along with two hundred soldiers, to attack the army of Islam from the rear whenever the guards neglected the mountain pass.

Finally, the two armies lined up against each other. Abu Sufyan encouraged his army in the name of idols and beautiful women, while the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله encouraged His army in the name of Almighty Allah. From the Muslim army, the cry of Allahu Akbar (Allah is Greater) was heard, and from the army of disbelief, the sound of drums and flutes rose.

When the battle began, the Muslims quickly crushed the Quraysh army, and the army of disbelief fled, with the Muslims pursuing them. Some Muslims, thinking the disbelievers were definitively defeated, became busy collecting spoils, and the guards of the mountain pass, contrary to the strict orders of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, abandoned their protected area out of greed for collecting spoils. At this moment, Khalid ibn Walid, with his two hundred soldiers who were in ambush, seized the opportunity and attacked the army of Islam from the rear. Suddenly, the Muslims found themselves surrounded by the disbelievers.

Hamza, the noble uncle of the Prophet, was martyred, and except for a few individuals who circled the blessed presence of the Messenger of Allah like moths, the rest of the Muslims fled. In this battle, the role of Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام was greater than that of all others, to the extent that his sword broke, and the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله gave him His own sword, named Dhul-Fiqar, and Ali علیه السلام protected Him. In this battle, sixty blows and wounds were inflicted upon the blessed body of Ali علیه السلام. Imam Sadiq علیه السلام said: The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله saw Gabriel between the earth and the sky saying: «لا فتى الاّ علىّ لاسَیف الاّ ذوالفقار» (There is no youth/hero but Ali, and no sword but Dhul-Fiqar).

One of the disbelievers of Mecca named Ibn Qami'ah martyred Mus'ab, a devoted soldier of Islam, thinking he was the Prophet, and shouted: By Lat and Uzza, Muhammad has been killed! The disbelievers, convinced of the martyrdom of the Messenger of Allah, took the road back to Mecca and abandoned the battle, and this slogan practically ended up benefiting the Muslims. Meanwhile, the Muslims, upon hearing the rumor of the Prophet's martyrdom, fled in fear and panic, but after learning that the Prophet was alive, they returned and apologized to Him. In this battle, seventy Muslims were martyred and a large number were wounded. [141]

140) Differences regarding this matter are seen in the book Furoogh-e Abadiyyat (Eternal Radiance), authored by His Eminence Ayatollah Subhani, Volume 2, p. 38, and Tarikh-e Payambar-e Islam (History of the Prophet of Islam), authored by Dr. Ayati, p. 285. 141) Tafsir Namuneh.

Messages

1- We should not forget the sensitive days of the past, but recall them as a document of experience. «و اذ غدوت...»

2- On the eve of war, one must detach oneself from family. «واذ غدوت من اهلك...»

3- Technical, geographical, and natural planning for military operations must take place before the enemy attacks. «واذ غدوت... تبوّى ء»

4- The command of the war and the selection of defensive bases and strategic areas are the responsibility of the Prophet and the leader of the Muslims Himself. «تبوّى ء المؤمنین مقاعد للقتال»

Ayah

إِذْ هَمَّت طَّآئِفَتَانِ مِنكُمْ أَن تَفْشَلَا وَٱللَّهُ وَلِيُّهُمَا ۗ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱۲۲)
When two groups among you were about to lose courage—though Allah is their guardian, and in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (122)

Key Points

Two groups of Muslims, named "Banu Salama" from the tribe of Aws and "Banu Harithah" from the tribe of Khazraj, decided to refrain from participating in the Battle of Uhud. Several factors have been mentioned as the reason for the weakness of these two groups: 1- Fear of the enemy, who outnumbered the Muslims. 2- Displeasure that their opinion was not considered during consultation, and that they had not taken shelter within the city instead of on the slopes of Uhud. 3- Objecting to why the Prophet did not allow their allied Jews to assist them. But Allah, through His grace, protected these two groups from falling into the trap of sin and fleeing the battlefield, and preserved them under His guardianship (Wilayah).[142]

142) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan, concerning the Ayah.

Messages

1- Allah is aware of our decisions and informs His Prophet of people's thoughts. «اذ همّت»

2- Do not forget the difficult days and the lessons learned from them. «اذ همّت»

3- Not everyone who goes to the battlefield is the same. «طائفتان... ان تفشلا»

4- The presence of weak-willed elements on the fronts is a factor leading to defeat and danger. «تفشلا»

5- If a person is not under the guardianship (Wilayah) of Allah, he becomes weak. «ان تفشلا واللّه ولیّهما»

6- Allah does not abandon the believers to their own devices and takes hold of their hands during critical moments. «واللّه ولیّهما»

7- The thought of sin, if it does not reach the stage of action, does not remove a person from under Divine guardianship (Wilayah). «همّت... واللّه ولیّهما»

8- The sole remedy for weakness is reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah. This remedy is only available to the believers. «وعلى اللّه فلیتوكّل المؤمنون»

9- Reliance upon Allah is a factor leading to victory. «همّت... ان تفشلا واللّه ولیّهما و على اللّه فلیتوكّل»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ نَصَرَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ بِبَدْرٍ وَأَنتُمْ أَذِلَّةٌ ۖ فَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَشْكُرُونَ (۱۲۳)
Certainly Allah helped you at Badr, when you were weak [in the enemy’s eyes]. So be wary of Allah so that you may give thanks. (123)

Key Points

The word 'adhillah (اذلّة) either means the scarcity of believing manpower in Badr, or it means the lack of military equipment and logistics. Recalling the deficiencies in Badr points to their capability (or strength) compared to the Battle of Uhud. In the three preceding verses, we read that patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) safeguard a person, and in this verse, the practical example of this is introduced as the mujahidin (fighters) of Badr. In the previous verse, there was also talk of reliance (tawakkul), and the mujahidin of Badr are the practical example of reliance. This verse addresses the Muslims, saying: You who witnessed Allah's help (nusrah) in Badr, why did you contemplate weakness in Uhud?!

Messages

1- Do not forget the unseen aids in the fields of battle. «نصركم اللّه ببدر»

2- Divine help (nusrah) is a sign of Allah's guardianship (wilayah) over the believers. «واللّه وليّهما... و لقد نصركم اللّه ببدر»

3- Let us rely upon Allah, for we have seen His help on the battlefields. «وعلى اللّه فلیتوكّل... نصركم اللّه ببدر»

4- Divine aids determine the outcome of the war, not natural factors. «نصركم اللّه ببدر و انتم اذلّة»

5- Whoever seeks Divine help must adopt piety (taqwa). «نصركم اللّه... فاتقوا اللّه»

6- The gratitude for Divine aids is that we adopt Divine piety. «فاتّقوا اللّه... تشكرون»

Ayah

إِذْ تَقُولُ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ أَلَن يَكْفِيَكُمْ أَن يُمِدَّكُمْ رَبُّكُم بِثَلَـٰثَةِ ءَالَـٰفٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ مُنزَلِينَ (۱۲٤)
When you were saying to the faithful, ‘Is it not enough for you that your Lord should aid you with three thousand angels sent down?’ (124)

Messages

1- One of the duties of leadership is to instill hope in the people and draw their attention to divine, unseen assistance. «اذ تقول للمؤمنین»

2- Hope in Allah's grace is essential for a combatant. «ألَن یَكفیكم»

3- The angels, by Allah's command, are placed in the service of the believers. «یُمِدّكم ربّكم بثلاثة آلاف من الملائكة»

4- In the divine worldview, human life is connected with the world of angels. «یمدّكم...»

5- Divine assistance is based on His Lordship (Rububiyyah). «یُمِدّكم ربّكم»

6- One of the means of divine assistance on the battlefronts is the descent of angels. «یُمِدّكم ربّكم بثلاثة آلاف من الملائكة»

Ayah

بَلَىٰٓ ۚ إِن تَصْبِرُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ وَيَأْتُوكُم مِّن فَوْرِهِمْ هَـٰذَا يُمْدِدْكُمْ رَبُّكُم بِخَمْسَةِ ءَالَـٰفٍ مِّنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةِ مُسَوِّمِينَ (۱۲٥)
Yes, if you are steadfast and Godwary, and should they come at you suddenly, your Lord will aid you with five thousand angels sent in [to the scene of battle]. (125)

Key Points

| In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the descent of three thousand assisting angels, and in this Ayah, the discussion is about five thousand special angels, and this is conditional upon the patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) of the combatants. Mentioning the number of angels indicates that their power is limited. Just as it is understood from the word «مُنزَلین» (Munzalīn) that the angels are under the command of Allah and do not act on their own accord. And from the word «مُسَوّمِین» (Musawwamīn), it is inferred that the assisting angels are special forces. In Sahifah Sajjadiyyah, Imam Zayn al-'Abidin عليه السلام, in his salutation to the angels, describes a specific mission for each group of angels. (143)

143) Sahifah Sajjadiyyah, the third supplication.

Messages

  1. Steadfastness and piety are factors for the descent of angels and unseen aid. «ان تصبروا و تتّقوا... یمددكم ربّكم»
  2. Divine aid is not exclusive to the time of the Prophet. «ان تصبرواوتتّقوا... یمددكم ربّكم»
  3. Resistance coupled with piety is valuable; otherwise, it is mere obstinacy and stubbornness. «ان تصبروا و تتّقوا»
  4. Do not be heedless of the enemy, for his attack is furious and surging. «یاتوكم من فورهم»
  5. Whenever the enemy's attack is severe against the people of piety, Allah also increases His aid. «یاتوكم من فورهم... بخمسة آلاف»

Ayah

وَمَا جَعَلَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بُشْرَىٰ لَكُمْ وَلِتَطْمَئِنَّ قُلُوبُكُم بِهِۦ ۗ وَمَا ٱلنَّصْرُ إِلَّا مِنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۱۲٦)
