Allah did not appoint it but as a good news for you and to reassure with it your hearts; and victory comes only from Allah, the All-mighty, the All-wise, (126)

Messages

1- Tranquility and hope are the needs of combatants on the fronts. «بُشرى لكم و لتطمئنّ»

2- All material, scientific, psychological, and human preparations accomplish nothing without the will of Allah. «و ما النّصر الاّ من عنداللّه»

3- The power of Allah is accompanied by His wisdom. Therefore, Muslims might sometimes suffer defeat for specific reasons. «اللّه العزیز الحكیم»

Ayah

لِيَقْطَعَ طَرَفًا مِّنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَوْ يَكْبِتَهُمْ فَيَنقَلِبُوا۟ خَآئِبِينَ (۱۲۷)
that He may cut down a section of the faithless, or subdue them, so that they retreat disappointed. (127)

Key Points

The word "طَرف" (ṭaraf) means the end of a thing, not its corner. Therefore, the Ayah states: Our unseen aid is intended to cut off the root of some of the disbelievers. However, some commentators have taken "طَرف" to mean the elite (ashrāf). That is, the goal of the Battle of Badr was the destruction of the elite among the disbelievers, similar to the Ayah: «فقاتلوا ائمّة الكفر» (144). Despair (na-omīdī) is of two types: If someone is despairing from the beginning, they are said to have "یأس" (ya's). But if they become despairing after having hope, they are said to be "خائب" (khā'ib) (145). War in Islam is sometimes defensive, intended to neutralize the enemy's aggression, and sometimes initiatory (ibtidā'ī) and offensive.

144) Tawbah, 12. 145) Tafsir Atyab al-Bayan and Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran.

Messages

1- The leaders of disbelief must either be completely uprooted, or they must become humiliated, disgraced, and despairing. Do not be content with weak, seasonal, or localized confrontations that do not harm the root of disbelief. «لیقطع طرفاً من الّذین كفروا»

2- The unity, power, politics, and management of Muslims must be such that the enemy is driven to despair. «فینقلبوا خائبین»

Ayah

لَيْسَ لَكَ مِنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ شَىْءٌ أَوْ يَتُوبَ عَلَيْهِمْ أَوْ يُعَذِّبَهُمْ فَإِنَّهُمْ ظَـٰلِمُونَ (۱۲۸)
You have no hand in the matter, whether He accepts their repentance or punishes them, for they are indeed wrongdoers. (128)

Key Points

When the leader of the Ummah (community) possesses complete sincerity, he acts in such a way that he recites, with utmost honesty and courage, even those verses that absolve him of responsibility, such as when Allah said: "You have no say in the matter." We read in both Shi'a and Sunni commentaries (Tafasir) that during the Battle of Uhud, when the Prophet's tooth was broken and blood flowed, the Prophet said: "How will these people ever attain salvation?" The Ayah was revealed (stating) that you are not responsible for the people's salvation; they might be forgiven in the future, or they might be left to their own devices and punished.

Messages

1- Forgiveness or punishment is in the hands of Allah. The intercession (Shafa'at) of the saints (Awliya) does not occur without His permission, and the Prophets never possess independence before Allah on their own accord. «لیس لك من الامر شى ء»

2- The path of repentance (Tawbah) is not closed, even for those who fled the front lines of truth or for the disbelievers (Kuffar) who inflicted the greatest harm upon the Muslims. «او یتوب علیهم»

3- The punishment of the people is due to their own oppression and injustice. «یعذّبهم فانّهم ظالمون»

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ يَغْفِرُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيُعَذِّبُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (۱۲۹)
To Allah belongs whatever there is in the heavens and the earth: He forgives whomever He wishes and punishes whomever He wishes, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (129)

Key Points

This Ayah is an emphasis on the previous Ayah, stating that punishment or forgiveness is solely in the hand of Allah. This is because the creation and sovereignty of the entire existence are in His hand. It is interesting that in the Ayah, the individuals subject to wrath or favor are not specified, so that no one develops arrogance or despair, and everyone remains in a state of fear and hope. It goes without saying that forgiveness or punishment is based on Divine wisdom and depends on the conditions that man creates within himself or in society.

Messages

1- The right to forgive or punish belongs only to the One for whom the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth belongs. «للّه ما فى السموات... یغفر... ویعذّب»

2- In the Divine system, the principle is based on pardon and forgiveness. «یغفر» is placed before «یعذّب».

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَأْكُلُوا۟ ٱلرِّبَوٰٓا۟ أَضْعَـٰفًا مُّضَـٰعَفَةً ۖ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۱۳٠)
O you who have faith! Do not exact usury, twofold and severalfold, and be wary of Allah so that you may be felicitous. (130)

Key Points

This Ayah, along with the eight succeeding Ayahs, is situated among the verses concerning the Battle of Uhud. Perhaps this is because moral and economic issues are effective within a defensive system. A society whose members possess sincerity (ikhlas), self-sacrifice (ithar), precedence in good deeds, and obedience to the leader, will also be successful in war. However, a society that is money-worshipping, stingy, disobedient, and insistent upon committing sin, will certainly be defeated. The verses prohibiting usury (riba) were revealed in several stages and gradually. The first step was criticizing the usury practiced by the Jews: «و اخذهم الرّبا وقد نهوا عنه»[۱۴۶] The second step is this Ayah, which prohibits usury that is multiplied many times over (severalfold). And in the third step, Islam prohibited even a single dirham of usury and introduced it as tantamount to waging war against Allah.

146) Nisa, 161.

Messages

1- Faith is the foundation for acting upon divine commands. «یا ایّها الّذین آمنوا لاتاكلوا الرّبا»

2- Before the general prohibition of usury, its ugly and prominent examples were prohibited. «اضعافاً مضاعفة»

3- Observing piety (taqwa) is strictly necessary in economic matters. «لا تأكلوا الربا... اتقوا اللّه»

4- Salvation (felicity) is not achieved through usury, but rather through piety (taqwa). «لا تأكلوا الربا... واتّقوا اللّه لعلكم تفلحون»

5- The usurer is not successful (felicitous) in this world (due to the intensification of class differences, which leads to division, resentment among the deprived, and their eventual explosion), nor in the Hereafter (due to incurring the wrath of Allah). «لا تأكلوا الربا... لعلّكم تفلحون»

Ayah

وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱلنَّارَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱۳۱)
Beware of the Fire, which has been prepared for the faithless, (131)

Messages

1- Usury (Riba) is a type of disbelief (Kufr). «لا تأكلوا الربا... اعدّت للكافرین»

2- A Muslim who practices usury will face the same punishment that has been prepared for the disbeliever. «اعدّت للكافرین»

3- Hellfire has been prepared for the disbelievers, and if Muslims become afflicted by it, it is due to the similarity of their actions to those of the disbelievers. «اعدّت للكافرین»

4- Hellfire currently exists. «اعدّت للكافرین» Just as Paradise is prepared for the righteous. «و أزلفت الجنّة للمتّقین»[147]

147) Shu'ara, 90.

Ayah

وَأَطِيعُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَٱلرَّسُولَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ (۱۳۲)
and obey Allah and the Apostle so that you may be granted [His] mercy. (132)

Key Points

The reason for the Muslims' defeat in the Battle of Uhud was disobedience to the command of the Noble Messenger صلى الله علیه وآله. This was because the Prophet had commanded them not to abandon the defensive position located in the valleys of Mount Uhud, but the guard unit, due to greed and the desire to collect war spoils, abandoned the area contrary to the command, and the enemy struck the Muslims from that very location.

Messages

1- The governmental instructions and commands of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are obligatory to obey, just like the command of Allah. «واطیعوا اللّه و الرسول»

2- Usury (interest-taking) is rebellion and transgression against Allah and the Messenger. «لاتاكلوا الربا... واطیعوا اللّه و الرسول»

3- Whoever abandons usury and shows mercy to people out of obedience to the command of Allah and the Messenger will be granted mercy. «اطیعوا... تُرحمون»

4- Following Allah and the Messenger is the key to salvation from the Fire (Hell). «اتّقوا النار... واطیعوا اللّه و الرسول»

5- The result of following Allah and the Messenger returns to the person himself, not to Allah or the Messenger. «لعلّكم تُرحمون»

6- Obedience to Allah is not without reward; rather, it is the means and context for receiving His mercy. «اطیعوا... لعلكم تُرحمون»

Ayah

۞ وَسَارِعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ مَغْفِرَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَجَنَّةٍ عَرْضُهَا ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ وَٱلْأَرْضُ أُعِدَّتْ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۳۳)
Hasten towards your Lord’s forgiveness and a paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for the Godwary (133)

Key Points

A group of Islamic scholars believe that Paradise and Hell currently exist, and one of their proofs is these verses: «اُعدّت للمتّقین» (prepared for the righteous) or «اُعدّت للكافرین» (prepared for the disbelievers). Forgiveness of sins is a Divine act, and the intent behind speeding towards forgiveness is speeding towards an action that results in forgiveness. In this verse, the word «عَرض» (width/breadth) means vastness, not a word opposite to length. Imam Ali عليه السلام said in the interpretation of this verse: «سارِعُوا الى اداء الفَرائِض» (Hasten to perform the obligatory duties). Hasten to perform the Divine obligations. [148]

148) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Speed in good deeds increases their value. «سارعوا» (Hasten).

2- First being forgiven, then entering Paradise. «مغفرة... جنّة» (Forgiveness... Paradise).

3- The forgiveness of people's sins is among the affairs of Divine Lordship. «مغفرة من ربّكم» (Forgiveness from your Lord).

4- Speed in repentance is necessary. «سارعوا الى مغفرة» (Hasten towards forgiveness).

5- The prerequisite for hastening towards the Paradise of the righteous is to be counted among the righteous. «جنّة... اُعدّت للمتقین» (Paradise... prepared for the righteous).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُنفِقُونَ فِى ٱلسَّرَّآءِ وَٱلضَّرَّآءِ وَٱلْكَـٰظِمِينَ ٱلْغَيْظَ وَٱلْعَافِينَ عَنِ ٱلنَّاسِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱۳٤)
—those who spend in ease and adversity, and suppress their anger, and excuse [the faults of] the people, and Allah loves the virtuous; (134)

Key Points

Following the condemnation of usury (riba) in the previous verses, this verse praises charity (infaq), pardon, forgiveness, and cooperation. A historical incident has been narrated: One day, while a servant of Imam Sajjad عليه السلام was washing the Imam's head and face, the water container fell from his hand and wounded the Imam's head. The Imam looked at him. He realized that the Imam was displeased. He immediately said: "والكاظمین الغیظ" (and those who restrain anger). The Imam said: I have restrained My anger. He said again: "والعافین عن الناس" (and those who pardon people). The Imam said: I have pardoned you. He said: "واللّه یحبّ المحسنین" (and Allah loves the doers of good). The Imam said: You are free for the sake of Allah.[149]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: There is no servant who restrains his anger except that Allah increases his honor in this world and the Hereafter. Allah said: "و الكاظمین الغیظ... و اللّه یحبّ المحسنین" This is the reward for restraining that anger.[150]

149) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Ruh al-Bayan. 150) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 110.

Messages

  1. Piety (Taqwa) is inseparable from charity (Infaq). "اُعدّت للمتّقین الّذین ینفقون..."

  2. Charity requires generosity, not wealth. "فى السرّاء والضرّاء"

  3. We should neither neglect the deprived during times of ease, nor should we say we are afflicted ourselves during times of hardship. "فى السرّاء والضرّاء"

  4. The pious are not slaves to their instincts; they are masters and owners of themselves. "الكاظمین الغیظ"

  5. Piety is inseparable from broad-mindedness (magnanimity). "والعافین عن الناس"

  6. The pious person is not isolated; rather, he interacts with people through his wealth and his good character. "ینفقون, كاظمین, عافین"

  7. In pardoning the wrongdoer, his faith is not a condition. "والعافین عن الناس"

  8. Whoever wishes to become beloved by Allah must sacrifice wealth and restrain anger and rage. "واللّه یحبّ المحسنین"

  9. Giving charity to the deprived and overlooking people's mistakes are instances of Ihsan (goodness) and benevolence. "ینفقون, كاظمین, عافین, محسنین"

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا فَعَلُوا۟ فَـٰحِشَةً أَوْ ظَلَمُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ ذَكَرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ فَٱسْتَغْفَرُوا۟ لِذُنُوبِهِمْ وَمَن يَغْفِرُ ٱلذُّنُوبَ إِلَّا ٱللَّهُ وَلَمْ يُصِرُّوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا فَعَلُوا۟ وَهُمْ يَعْلَمُونَ (۱۳٥)
and those who, when they commit an indecent act or wrong themselves, remember Allah, and plead [to Allah seeking His] forgiveness for their sins—and who forgives sins except Allah?—and who knowingly do not persist in what [sins] they have committed. (135)

Key Points

Some commentators have considered the meaning of al-fāḥishah (الفاحشة) to be adultery (zina) and the meaning of al-ẓulm (الظلم) to be other sins. Others have considered al-fāḥishah to mean major sins and al-ẓulm to mean minor sins. We read in narrations that inattention to past sins and abandoning repentance for them is considered persistence in sin (iṣrār).[151] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: When this verse was revealed, Iblis gathered all his helpers and sought a solution. The devils offered suggestions that were not accepted. Waswās al-Khannās (the whispering tempter) said: I will find a solution! They asked: How? He said: I will give them promises and entangle them in hopes (and desires) until they commit sins, and when they fall into sin, I will make them forget seeking forgiveness (istighfār).[152]

151) هو ان یذنب الذنب فلایستغفر اللّه ولایحدث نفسه بتوبة فذلك الاصرار». كافى، ج ۲، ص ۲۸۷. 152) وسائل، ج ۱۱، ص ۶۶.

Messages

1- Sometimes even pious individuals (the muttaqīn) commit sins. «للمتّقین... الّذین اذا فعلوا فاحشة»

2- More dangerous than committing the sin is heedlessness regarding the sin and inattention to its ugliness. If the pious commit a sin, they immediately seek forgiveness (istighfār). «اذا فعلوا فاحشة... ذكروا اللّه فاستغفروا»

3- The sign of piety (taqwā) is immediate repentance from sin. «اذا فعلوا فاحشة... ذكروا اللّه فاستغفروا»

4- The remembrance of Allah is the key to repentance. «ذكروا اللّه فاستغفروا»

5- As long as the remembrance of Allah is alive in the heart of the sinner, he can enter the circle of the pious. «اذا فعلوا فاحشة... ذكروا اللّه»

6- Sin is oppression against oneself. «ظلموا انفسهم»

7- Only Allah is the One Who forgives sins. «و من یغفر الذنوب الا اللّه»

8- The pious person (muttaqī) is one who does not persist in sin, because persistence in sin is a sign of belittling it and being heedless of the remembrance of Allah. «ولم یصرّوا على ما فعلوا»

9- Conscious persistence in sin leads to deprivation from Divine forgiveness. «و من یغفر الذنوب الا اللّه و لم یصرّوا»

Ayah

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ جَزَآؤُهُم مَّغْفِرَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَجَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ وَنِعْمَ أَجْرُ ٱلْعَـٰمِلِينَ (۱۳٦)
Their reward is forgiveness from their Lord, and gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever]. How excellent is the reward of the workers! (136)

Key Points

The appearance of the words Muttaqin (the pious), Muhsinin (the good-doers), and ‘Amilin (the workers/doers) at the end of three consecutive verses indicates that Taqwa (piety) is not a state of isolation and seclusion, nor is it merely a spiritual quality, but rather it is presence in the arena (of life), accompanied by action and goodness (Ihsan).

Messages

1- Unless a person is purified from sin, he does not deserve entry into Paradise. (Maghfirah... Jannat)

2- Allah's pardon and forgiveness are for the nurturing of man. (Maghfirah min Rabbihim)

3- One cannot attain Allah's favors merely through wishes; rather, work and action are necessary. (Ni‘ma ajr al-‘amilin)

Ayah

قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ سُنَنٌ فَسِيرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ فَٱنظُرُوا۟ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُكَذِّبِينَ (۱۳۷)
Certain [Divine] precedents have passed before you. So travel through the land and observe how was the fate of the deniers. (137)

Key Points

This Ayah points to the importance of sociology (understanding societies) and the discovery of the principles governing the honor and degradation of communities. The divine traditions (Sunan) that existed among previous nations include the following: A: Their acceptance of the truth and their salvation. B: Their denial of the truth and their destruction. C: Divine tests for them. D: Unseen (Ghaybi) assistance for the people of truth. E: Opportunity and respite granted to the oppressors. F: The resistance of the men of God and their achievement of goals. G: The conspiracy of the disbelievers and its neutralization by Allah.

Messages

1- Fixed laws and traditions govern human history, and understanding them is beneficial for humanity today. «قد خلت من قبلكم سنن»

2- The history of those who passed away is a guiding light for the lives of future generations. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

3- Purposeful travel (tourism) and visitation accompanied by reflection is the best class for educating human beings. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

4- You are no different from other nations. The factors leading to honor or downfall are the same for everyone. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

5- Knowing the conduct and the ultimate end of previous generations is useful in choosing one's path. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

6- By being aware of past history, you can predict the future course of your path. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

7- Preserving ancient monuments is necessary for the admonition (lesson) of future generations. «فسیروا فى الارض»

8- Examination and research into historical transformations are necessary. «فسیروا... فانظروا»

9- When studying history, appearances are not important; the ultimate end is important. «فسیروا... فانظروا كیف كان عاقبة المكذّبین»

10- Divine punishments are not limited only to the Day of Judgment; sometimes, Allah's wrath overtakes the arrogant in this very world. «عاقبة المكذّبین»

Ayah

هَـٰذَا بَيَانٌ لِّلنَّاسِ وَهُدًى وَمَوْعِظَةٌ لِّلْمُتَّقِينَ (۱۳۸)
This is an explanation for mankind, and a guidance and advice for the Godwary. (138)

Key Points

The word "هذا" (this) either refers to Ayahs 130 to 137, or it refers to the historical traditions and lessons mentioned in the previous Ayah.

Messages

1- The Quran is understandable for all people in every era. «بیان للناس» 2- The Quran is for all people, in all times and places. «للناس» 3- Purposeful travel (tourism) is among the factors leading to guidance. «سیروا فى الارض...هدى» 4- Although the Quran is for the general public, only the pious and the God-fearing accept advice. «موعظة للمتقین» 5- The spiritual disposition of individuals is effective in their utilization and benefit from the Divine Ayahs. «للمتقین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَهِنُوا۟ وَلَا تَحْزَنُوا۟ وَأَنتُمُ ٱلْأَعْلَوْنَ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۳۹)
Do not weaken or grieve: you shall have the upper hand, should you be faithful. (139)

Key Points

After the Battle of Uhud, which resulted in a defeat for the forces of Islam due to disobedience to the command, the Muslims lost their morale. This verse was revealed [to tell them] not to lose heart because of the defeat, but rather to strengthen themselves with faith, for superiority is theirs.

Messages

1- A localized defeat in one battle is not a sign of the ultimate defeat of the Muslims. «ولا تهنوا ولا تحزنوا و انتم الاعلون»

2- The leader must strengthen morale. «وانتم الاعلون...»

3- If you return to faith and obedience, you will achieve victory in battles. «وانتم الاعلون ان كنتم مؤمنین»

4- In the light of faith, it is possible to overcome the entire world. «انتم الاعلون ان كنتم مؤمنین»

5- Apparent victory and defeat are not the criteria; superiority based on correct beliefs and sound thinking is the fundamental principle. «وانتم الاعلون ان كنتم مؤمنین»

6- In the materialistic worldview, the factor of victory is weapons and equipment, but in the Divine perspective, the factor of victory is faith. «ان كنتم مؤمنین»

Ayah

إِن يَمْسَسْكُمْ قَرْحٌ فَقَدْ مَسَّ ٱلْقَوْمَ قَرْحٌ مِّثْلُهُۥ ۚ وَتِلْكَ ٱلْأَيَّامُ نُدَاوِلُهَا بَيْنَ ٱلنَّاسِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَيَتَّخِذَ مِنكُمْ شُهَدَآءَ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٤٠)
If wounds afflict you, like wounds have already afflicted those people; and We make such vicissitudes rotate among mankind, so that Allah may ascertain those who have faith, and that He may take martyrs from among you, and Allah does not like the wrongdoers. (140)

Key Points

This Ayah, while consoling the Muslims, states a reality: that if you have suffered casualties for the sake of truth and for a divine goal, your enemies have also given killed and wounded. If you were not victorious today at Uhud, your enemies were defeated yesterday at Badr, so be patient in hardships.

Although the words shuhada', shahid, and shahid usually mean "witness" in the Qur'an, due to the occasion of revelation (sha'n nuzul), the issue of war, and the topic of wounds and injuries on the battlefield, if someone interprets the word shuhada' in this Ayah as "those killed in the way of Allah," they have not strayed far.

Muslims should maintain their morale by considering the following points: A: «انتم الاعلون» You are the exalted ones. B: «قد مسّ القوم قرح» Your enemies have also been wounded. C: «تلك الایام نداولها» These bitter days will pass. D: «و لیعلم اللّه الّذین امنوا» Allah distinguishes the true believers from the hypocrites. E: «و یتخذ منكم شهدا» Allah takes witnesses from among you for the future of history. F: «واللّه لایحب الظالمین» Allah does not love your opponents.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Since the day Allah created Adam, divine and satanic power and governance have been in conflict with one another, but the complete divine state (Dawlah) will be realized with the advent of Hadrat Qa'im عليه السلام.[153]

153) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 199.

Messages

1- Muslims should not be less patient and forbearing than the disbelievers. «فقد مسّ القوم قرح مثله»

2- Bitter and sweet events are not permanent. «تلك الایام نداولها بین الناس»

3- In wars and the ups and downs of life, the people of faith are distinguished from those who merely claim faith. «لیعلم اللّه الّذین آمنوا»

4- Allah took martyrs from among you, those killed in the way of truth, so that you would not easily abandon the religion of Allah. «ویتّخذ منكم شهداء»

5- Allah took witnesses from among yourselves (to show) how disobedience to leadership leads to defeat. «ویتّخذ منكم شهداء»

6- The temporary victory of the disbelievers is not a sign of Allah's love for them. «اللّه لایحبّ الظّالمین»

7- Historical movements and currents are by the will of Allah. «نداولها»

Ayah

وَلِيُمَحِّصَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَيَمْحَقَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٤۱)
And so that Allah may purge [the hearts of] those who have faith and that He may wipe out the faithless. (141)

Key Points

The word "تَمحِیص" (Tamhīṣ) means purifying from every defect, and "مَحق" (Maḥq) means gradual reduction. It is as if Allah, in [the Battle of] Uhud, intended to show the Muslims their weaknesses so that they would consider reforming and compensating for their deficiencies, and be adequately prepared for subsequent movements. Sometimes constructive and awakening defeats are more desirable than sleep-inducing victories.

Messages

1- War is a measure for distinguishing the pure individuals from the hollow ones. «لیمحّص اللّه»

2- For the people of faith, defeat or victory is felicity. Because they attain either martyrdom, experience, or triumph over the enemy. «لیمحّص اللّه الّذین آمنوا»

3- The growth and flourishing of faith, and the obliteration and downfall of disbelief (Kufr), are achieved gradually and throughout history. (154) «لیمحّص... یمحق»

4- The future of history is towards the obliteration and destruction of the disbelievers. «یمحق الكافرین»

5- Today's defeat is not important; the end result is the fundamental matter. «و یمحق الكافرین»

(154) «یُمَحّص» (Yumaḥḥiṣ) indicates continuity, and «یَمحَق» (Yamḥaq) carries the meaning of gradualness.

Ayah

أَمْ حَسِبْتُمْ أَن تَدْخُلُوا۟ ٱلْجَنَّةَ وَلَمَّا يَعْلَمِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ جَـٰهَدُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَيَعْلَمَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱٤۲)
Do you suppose that you would enter paradise, while Allah has not yet ascertained those of you who have waged jihad and He has not ascertained the steadfast? (142)

Key Points

This Ayah refers to the Jihad (struggle), patience, and steadfastness of the believers, because the path to Paradise is through patience and Jihad. The Quran says: «سلام علیكم بما صبرتم»(155) Peace be upon you, O people of Paradise, because of the patience and steadfastness you maintained. There is a subtlety in this expression; it does not say peace be upon you because of Hajj, fasting, or Khums and Zakat, because the performance of every action requires patience and perseverance.

155) Ra'd, 24.

Messages

1- Let us abandon vain hopes and false aspirations. «ام حسبتم»

2- Inner faith is not enough; effort and action are also necessary. Paradise is granted for a price, not an excuse. «ام حسبتم ان تدخلوا الجنة و لما یعلم...»

3- The key to Paradise is patience (sabr) and Jihad. Patience in the face of calamity, sin, and in performing acts of worship, and presence on the front lines of the Greater Jihad and the Lesser Jihad. «و یعلم الصابرین»

4- Patience and steadfastness are necessary alongside Jihad. Because the start of war, its continuation, and the consequences and effects after the war, all require patience and forbearance. «جاهدوا منكم و یعلم الصابرین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ كُنتُمْ تَمَنَّوْنَ ٱلْمَوْتَ مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَلْقَوْهُ فَقَدْ رَأَيْتُمُوهُ وَأَنتُمْ تَنظُرُونَ (۱٤۳)
Certainly you were longing for death before you had encountered it. Then certainly, you saw it, as you were looking on. (143)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is narrated: After the Battle of Badr, when the Muslims were victorious and a number of people were martyred, some individuals used to say: "If only we had also been martyred in the Battle of Badr in the way of Allah." However, those very people fled the battlefield the following year in the Battle of Uhud, and they are criticized and reproached in this Ayah. In the Ziyarah (visitation) of the martyrs of Karbala, we say: "If only we had been with you and attained martyrdom." Have we ever considered what we actually do in the realm of action?

It would be good if the touchstone of experience were brought forth, So that whoever has deceit within him may be disgraced.

Messages

1- Do not be deceived by your own wishes, and do not trust every slogan or slogan-raiser. «ولقد كنتم... فقد رایتموه...»

2- Men are tested in the field of action. «رایتموه... و انتم تنظرون»

Ayah

وَمَا مُحَمَّدٌ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِهِ ٱلرُّسُلُ ۚ أَفَإِي۟ن مَّاتَ أَوْ قُتِلَ ٱنقَلَبْتُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَـٰبِكُمْ ۚ وَمَن يَنقَلِبْ عَلَىٰ عَقِبَيْهِ فَلَن يَضُرَّ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًٔا ۗ وَسَيَجْزِى ٱللَّهُ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (۱٤٤)
Muhammad is but an apostle; [other] apostles have passed before him. If he dies or is slain, will you turn back on your heels? Anyone who turns back on his heels, will not harm Allah in the least, and soon Allah will reward the grateful. (144)

Key Points

It is mentioned in the commentaries (Tafasir) that when a stone struck the Prophet's forehead during the Battle of Uhud, causing blood to flow, a cry went up that Muhammad had been killed. Some also mistakenly thought the martyrdom of Mus'ab was the martyrdom of the Prophet. This rumor caused joy and boosted the morale of the disbelievers, and a group of wavering Muslims fled. Others even considered seeking amnesty from Abu Sufyan, the commander of the disbelievers. In contrast to this group, Muslims loudly shouted: Even if Muhammad is gone, the path of Muhammad and the God of Muhammad remains; do not flee. [156] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The reckoning of being killed (qatl) is separate from death (mawt), and every martyr will return in the future of history and die again. This is because the Quran states: [157] «كلّ نفس ذائقة الموت» [158]

156) I reached the writing of this Ayah at the time when the Founder of the Islamic Republic, His Eminence Imam Khomeini قدس سره, passed away to the highest realm, and approximately ten million people participated in his funeral procession in Tehran. Based on this very Ayah, the Muslims of Iran remained firm upon their covenant and renewed their allegiance to the Imam and his lofty ideals by electing a just and jurisprudent leader: "Ayatollah Khamenei." 157) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 2, p. 113. 158) Al-Anbiya, 35.

Messages

1- The duty of the Prophet is to convey the message of Allah. It is we who must continue His path. «و ما محمّد الاّ رسول»

2- Did the followers of previous Prophets abandon the religion upon their death?! «قد خلت من قبله الرسل...»

3- The Islamic society must possess such organization and cohesion that even the departure of the leader does not harm it. «أفاِن مات او قتل انقلبتم على اعقابكم»

4- The Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله is also subject to Divine traditions and natural laws, such as death and life. «أفاِن مات او قتل»

5- Spreading rumors is one of the enemy's tactics. «مات او قتل» (Considering the occasion of revelation)

6- The Prophet's lifespan is limited, but His path is not. «افاِن مات او قتل انقلبتم»

7- Personality worship is forbidden. Once the path of Allah is clear, the coming and going of individuals should not impede the pursuit of the Divine line. «أفاِن مات او قتل»

8- Make your faith so firm and steadfast that historical events do not shake it. «أفاِن مات او قتل»

9- Abandoning the path of the Prophets is a fall and a retreat. «انقلبتم على اعقابكم»

10- The disbelief of people does not harm Allah. «فلن یضرّاللّه»

11- Resistance in the way of truth is the best form of practical gratitude, the reward for which is with Allah. «سیجزى اللّه الشاكرین»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَن تَمُوتَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ كِتَـٰبًا مُّؤَجَّلًا ۗ وَمَن يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ ٱلدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِۦ مِنْهَا وَمَن يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِۦ مِنْهَا ۚ وَسَنَجْزِى ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (۱٤٥)
No soul may die except by Allah’s leave, at an appointed time. Whoever desires the reward of this world, We will give him of it; and whoever desires the reward of the Hereafter, We will give him of it; and soon We will reward the grateful. (145)

Messages

1- By fleeing from war, you cannot escape death. «انقلبتم على اعقابكم... ما كان لنفس»

2- Death is not in our hands, «كتاباً مؤجّلاً», but the will and the goal are in our hands. «ومن یرد...»

3- Now that this world and the Hereafter are before us, let us continue on the path of eternity and the pleasure of the Creator. «من یرد... نؤته منها»

4- Every type of motivation and action has a specific reflection and reaction. Whatever path we tread, we will reach a specific destination. «من یرد ثواب الدنیا... و من یرد...»

Ayah

وَكَأَيِّن مِّن نَّبِىٍّ قَـٰتَلَ مَعَهُۥ رِبِّيُّونَ كَثِيرٌ فَمَا وَهَنُوا۟ لِمَآ أَصَابَهُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا ضَعُفُوا۟ وَمَا ٱسْتَكَانُوا۟ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ (۱٤٦)
How many a prophet there has been with whom a multitude of godly men fought. They did not falter for what befell them in the way of Allah, neither did they weaken, nor did they abase themselves; and Allah loves the steadfast. (146)

Key Points

«رِبّیّون» جمع «رِبّى» بر وزن «ملّى» به كسى گفته مى شود كه ارتباط و پیوند او با خدا محكم و در دین، دانشمند و مقاوم و با اخلاص باشد.[۱۵۹]

159) Tafsir Namūneh (Tafsir al-Mizan).

Messages

1- The history of the prophets is accompanied by struggle. «وكایّن...»

2- When feeling weak, learn lessons from the lives of the valiant men of history and their steadfastness. «و كایّن من نبىّ»

3- A war (Jihad) is righteous only if it is conducted under the supervision of a divine leader. «قاتل معه» [160]

4- The number of knowledgeable and Gnostic (wise) Mujahidin throughout history is vast. «كایّن...ربیّون كثیر»

5 - Although all Mujahidin are honored, the Gnostic and knowledgeable fighters have a special status. «ربّیّون»

6- Faith in Allah is the source of steadfastness. «ربّیّون... فما وهنوا»

7- Difficulties should not lead to weakness and feebleness; rather, they should become a factor for new movement and effort. «فما وهنوا لما اصابهم»

8 - What gives a person morale and makes enduring difficulties easy is the fact that they are endured in the way of Allah. «فى سبیل اللّه»

9- Pressures do not force the men of Allah to surrender. «وما استكانوا»

10- Although you were victorious at Badr, the future path for you Muslims is the path of Jihad and struggle, so you must be patient and steadfast. (Considering the preceding and succeeding verses)

11- Insightful fighters neither lose their morale internally; «فما وهنوا» nor lose their fighting capability; «وما ضعفوا» nor surrender due to pressures. «و ما استكانوا»

12- Fulfilling duty and remaining steadfast upon the truth is important, whether we achieve victory or not. The Ayah states: «واللّه یحبّ الصابرین» (And Allah loves the patient ones), and it did not state: «یحبّ الفاتحین» (He loves the victorious ones).

160) In the supplication of the month of Ramadan, we read: «اللّهم ارزقنا قتلاً فى سبیلك تحت رایة نبیّك مع اولیائك»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ قَوْلَهُمْ إِلَّآ أَن قَالُوا۟ رَبَّنَا ٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَإِسْرَافَنَا فِىٓ أَمْرِنَا وَثَبِّتْ أَقْدَامَنَا وَٱنصُرْنَا عَلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۱٤۷)
All that they said was, ‘Our Lord, forgive us our sins and our excesses in our affairs, make our feet steady, and help us against the faithless lot.’ (147)

Messages

1- One of the factors leading to defeat in war is sin and extravagance. Sincere fighters eliminate these obstacles to victory through seeking forgiveness. «اغفر لنا ذنوبنا و اسرافنا»

2- Do not attribute your defeat to fate and destiny or to others; pay attention to your own shortcomings and faults as well. «ذنوبنا و اسرافنا»

3- First Istighfar (seeking forgiveness), then Istinsar (seeking help). First ask for forgiveness, then ask Allah for victory. «اغفر لنا... وانصرنا»

4- Instead of surrendering to the enemy, express humility before Allah. «و ما استكانوا...ربنّا اغفرلنا...»

5- The arenas of Jihad and war draw man toward prayer (Du'a) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar). «و ما كان قولهم الا ان قالوا ربّنا اغفر لنا»

6- The men of God seek Allah's help and assistance for the destruction of disbelief (Kufr), not for gaining personal honor. «وانصرنا على القوم الكافرین»

7- Rely on the grace of Allah, not on your own numbers. «ربّیون كثیر... ربّنا... انصرنا»

Ayah

فَـَٔاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ثَوَابَ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَحُسْنَ ثَوَابِ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُحْسِنِينَ (۱٤۸)
So Allah gave them the reward of this world and the fair reward of the Hereafter; and Allah loves the virtuous. (148)

Key Points

It is possible that the worldly reward for the combatants refers to steadfastness and victory, and their reward in the Hereafter refers to the forgiveness of sins.

Messages

1- There is a great difference between worldly and otherworldly rewards. «و حسن ثواب الاخرة» (and the excellence of the reward of the Hereafter). Worldly benefits also contain bitterness, but the reward of the Hereafter is entirely goodness and virtue.

2- Supplication (Du'a), striving (Jihad), patience (Sabr), seeking forgiveness (Istighfar), faith (Iman), and reliance (Tawakkul) upon Allah, which were mentioned in the previous verses, are factors leading to belovedness in the sight of Allah. «واللّه یحب المحسنین» (And Allah loves the doers of good).

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِن تُطِيعُوا۟ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ يَرُدُّوكُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَعْقَـٰبِكُمْ فَتَنقَلِبُوا۟ خَـٰسِرِينَ (۱٤۹)
O you who have faith! If you obey the faithless, they will turn you back on your heels, and you will become losers. (149)

Key Points

We read in the context of the revelation (Shān Nuzūl) of this Ayah that during the Battle of Uhud, when the Muslims were defeated, the leader of the hypocrites, Abdullah ibn Ubayy, shouted: Join your polytheist brothers and continue the same idol worship. [161]

161) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The danger of apostasy (irtidād) and deviation lies in wait for the believers. «الّذین آمنوا...یردّوكم على اعقابكم»

2- During ups and downs, be mindful of the enemy's propaganda and temptations, lest you gradually incline towards them. «ان تطیعوا الّذین كفروا»

3- Disbelief (kufr) after faith is regression and downfall. «على اعقابكم... خاسرین»

4- Real loss is wasting spiritual, intellectual, and psychological capital; it is losing Paradise and gaining Hellfire. «فتنقلبوا خاسرین»

5- Defeat on the battlefield is not a loss; ideological defeat and apostasy are a very great loss. «فتنقلبوا خاسرین»

Ayah

بَلِ ٱللَّهُ مَوْلَىٰكُمْ ۖ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ ٱلنَّـٰصِرِينَ (۱٥٠)
Indeed, Allah is your Master, and He is the best of helpers. (150)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about obeying the disbelievers (كفّار), not taking them as masters (مولى). This Ayah states: Allah is your Master (مولاى شماست). Therefore, it becomes clear that whoever obeys the disbelievers has, in reality, taken them as masters. The motivation for apostasy and obedience to the disbelievers is the acquisition of honor and power. The Quran refutes this vain notion in this Ayah and similar Ayahs, stating: «انّ القوّة للّه جمیعاً» (162) and «فانّ العزّة للّه جمیعاً» (163)

162) Baqarah, 165. 163) Yunus, 65.

Messages

1- Guardianship (Wilayah) and obedience are exclusive to Allah. «بل اللّه مولیكم»

2- Taking Allah as your Guardian (Walī) is the cause of your victory. «مولیكم و هو خیر الناصرین»

Ayah

سَنُلْقِى فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱلرُّعْبَ بِمَآ أَشْرَكُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِۦ سُلْطَـٰنًا ۖ وَمَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ مَثْوَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٥۱)
We shall cast terror into the hearts of the faithless because of their ascribing partners to Allah, for which He has not sent down any authority, and their refuge shall be the Fire, and evil is the [final] abode of the wrongdoers. (151)

Key Points

After the defeat of the Muslims at Uhud, Abu Sufyan and his army, who were still around Medina, said: The Muslims have been destroyed and the remainder of them have fled; it is good that we return and eradicate them. But Allah cast such a terror into their hearts that they returned to Mecca like the defeated, because they were worried about an attack by the Muslims. (164)

(164) Manaqib, Vol. 1, p. 126.

Messages

  1. Allah aids the Muslims by casting fear into the hearts of the enemy. «سنلقى فى قلوب الّذین كفروا الرعب»

  2. Reliance on other than Allah is polytheism (shirk) and a cause of fear, just as faith and the remembrance of Allah are causes of assurance. «الرعب بما اشركوا»

  3. The polytheist has no proof for his claim of polytheism. «مالم ینزّل به سلطاناً»

  4. The fundamentals of belief must be based on logic and reasoning. «لم ینزّل به سلطاناً»

  5. Proof (Burhan) is a Divine light that descends upon hearts, and the polytheists do not possess this light. «لم ینزّل به سلطاناً»

  6. Polytheism is oppression (zulm). Therefore, the end of the Ayah states: «بئس مثوى الظالمین»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَدَقَكُمُ ٱللَّهُ وَعْدَهُۥٓ إِذْ تَحُسُّونَهُم بِإِذْنِهِۦ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا فَشِلْتُمْ وَتَنَـٰزَعْتُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ وَعَصَيْتُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَآ أَرَىٰكُم مَّا تُحِبُّونَ ۚ مِنكُم مَّن يُرِيدُ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمِنكُم مَّن يُرِيدُ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةَ ۚ ثُمَّ صَرَفَكُمْ عَنْهُمْ لِيَبْتَلِيَكُمْ ۖ وَلَقَدْ عَفَا عَنكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٥۲)
Allah certainly fulfilled His promise to you when you were slaying them with His leave, until you lost courage, disputed about the matter, and disobeyed after He showed you what you loved. Some among you desire this world, and some among you desire the Hereafter. Then He turned you away from them so that He might test you. Certainly He has excused you, for Allah is gracious to the faithful. (152)

Key Points

Muslims were victorious in the Battle of Badr in the second year of the Hijra, and Allah had promised that they would also be victorious in future battles. The following year, when the Battle of Uhud took place, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله appointed fifty armed men to guard the valleys and mountains. When the battle began, the Muslims initially attacked, overwhelmed the enemy, and defeated them. Unfortunately, disagreement arose among those fifty men responsible for guarding the mountain. Some said: Our victory is certain, so let us go and collect the spoils, while a small number continued to hold their positions. The defeated enemy attacked from the very area that had been left unguarded, and this time the Muslims suffered a heavy blow and sustained many martyrs, to the extent that the life of the Prophet was endangered and many Muslims fled. After the battle ended, the Muslims criticized the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, asking: Had Allah not promised us victory? Why then were we defeated? This Ayah answers that Allah's promise was true, but weakness, conflict, and disobedience became the cause of your defeat.

Messages

1- The fulfillment of Allah's promises does not mean ignoring the Divine laws (Sunan). Allah's help lasts only as long as you fulfill your duty. «لقد صدقكم اللّه وعده...اذا فشلتم و تنازعتم»

2- Among the factors leading to defeat are weakness, conflict, and disobedience to the command structure. «فشلتم و تنازعتم... وعصیتم»

3- People fight for different goals. Some fight for this world (Dunya) and some fight for the Hereafter (Akhirah). «منكم من یریدالدنیا و منكم من یرید الاخرة»

4- Steadfastness in battle, maintaining unity, and obedience are signs that the fighters seek the Hereafter. «منكم من یرید الآخرة»

5- The recompense for one who fuels disagreement, disobedience, and weakness instead of showing gratitude for the blessing of victory is defeat. «صرفكم عنهم»

6- The cause of external defeats is internal. You became weak and disobedient internally, thus you were defeated externally. «فشلتم... صرفكم عنهم»

7- Defeats are a means of Divine testing. «لیبتلیكم»

8- Do not despair of Allah's grace even during transgression and defeat. «عفا عنكم»

9- Faith is the foundation and context for receiving Allah's special favor. «عفا عنكم و اللّه ذو فضل على المؤمنین»

10- A believer should not be considered outside the ranks of the faithful due to a single transgression; rather, they should be warned on the one hand, and encouraged and reassured on the other. «عفا عنكم و اللّه ذو فضل على المؤمنین»

Ayah

۞ إِذْ تُصْعِدُونَ وَلَا تَلْوُۥنَ عَلَىٰٓ أَحَدٍ وَٱلرَّسُولُ يَدْعُوكُمْ فِىٓ أُخْرَىٰكُمْ فَأَثَـٰبَكُمْ غَمًّۢا بِغَمٍّ لِّكَيْلَا تَحْزَنُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا فَاتَكُمْ وَلَا مَآ أَصَـٰبَكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ خَبِيرٌۢ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٥۳)
When you were fleeing without paying any attention to anyone, while the Apostle was calling you from your rear, He requited you with grief upon grief, so that you may not grieve for what you lose nor for what befalls you, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (153)

Key Points

Regarding the phrase «فاثابكم غمّاً بغمّ», various interpretations have been presented, including: 1- Allah, by His grace, replaced the undesirable grief of losing the spoils of war with the acceptable grief over the victory of the disbelievers (kuffar) over the Muslims. 2- Allah afflicted you with abundant sorrow and grief as a recompense for the grief you imposed upon the heart of the Prophet. 3- Allah inflicted numerous sorrows upon you, one after another, as a recompense for fleeing the battle and disobeying the Prophet. Based on narrations (revāyāt), the dominance of Khalid ibn al-Walid over the Muslims increased their sorrow resulting from the defeat in the Battle of Uhud.[165]

165) Tafsir Burhan; Rāhnamā.

Messages

1- The result of weakness, discord, and disobedience to command is flight and panic on the battlefield. «حتّى اذا فشلتم و تنازعتم... اذتصعدون»

2- Remembering weaknesses is a means of benefiting from experiences. «اذ تصعدون»

3- In times of danger, the ordinary person thinks of no one but himself. «لاتلوون على أحد»

4- The cry of the leader has no effect on fearful and weak-in-faith individuals. «والرسول یدعوكم»

5- Expressing affection during times of ease is not important; sympathy and companionship during difficult days are signs of true friendship. «والرسول یدعوكم...»

6- Where everyone flees, the leader must remain on the scene and call back the fugitives. «والرسول یدعوكم...»

7- A single moment of negligence and failure to perform duty leads to the onslaught of successive misfortunes. «غمّاً بغمّ»

8- Learn lessons from past misfortunes and no longer grieve over the loss of worldly possessions or the suffering of calamities. «لكیلا تحزنوا على ما فاتكم و لا ما اصابكم»

Ayah

ثُمَّ أَنزَلَ عَلَيْكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ٱلْغَمِّ أَمَنَةً نُّعَاسًا يَغْشَىٰ طَآئِفَةً مِّنكُمْ ۖ وَطَآئِفَةٌ قَدْ أَهَمَّتْهُمْ أَنفُسُهُمْ يَظُنُّونَ بِٱللَّهِ غَيْرَ ٱلْحَقِّ ظَنَّ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِيَّةِ ۖ يَقُولُونَ هَل لَّنَا مِنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ مِن شَىْءٍ ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْأَمْرَ كُلَّهُۥ لِلَّهِ ۗ يُخْفُونَ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِم مَّا لَا يُبْدُونَ لَكَ ۖ يَقُولُونَ لَوْ كَانَ لَنَا مِنَ ٱلْأَمْرِ شَىْءٌ مَّا قُتِلْنَا هَـٰهُنَا ۗ قُل لَّوْ كُنتُمْ فِى بُيُوتِكُمْ لَبَرَزَ ٱلَّذِينَ كُتِبَ عَلَيْهِمُ ٱلْقَتْلُ إِلَىٰ مَضَاجِعِهِمْ ۖ وَلِيَبْتَلِىَ ٱللَّهُ مَا فِى صُدُورِكُمْ وَلِيُمَحِّصَ مَا فِى قُلُوبِكُمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۱٥٤)
Then He sent down to you safety after grief—a drowsiness that came over a group of you—while another group, anxious only about themselves, entertained false notions about Allah, notions of [pagan] ignorance. They say, ‘Do we have any role in the matter?’ Say, ‘Indeed the matter belongs totally to Allah.’ They hide in their hearts what they do not disclose to you. They say, ‘Had we any role in the matter, we would not have been slain here.’ Say, ‘Even if you had remained in your houses, those destined to be slain would have set out toward the places where they were laid to rest, so that Allah may test what is in your breasts, and that He may purge what is in your hearts, and Allah knows well what is in the breasts.’ (154)

Messages

1- Allah punished you due to the violations and disagreements during the Battle of Uhud, but He did not abandon you. «ثم انزل علیكم...امنة»

2- Allah grants tranquility to the hearts of the believers even amidst the peak of bitter events. «بعدالغمّ أمنة»

3- Sleep is a source of tranquility and a Divine gift. «انزل علیكم... نعاسا»

4- The morale, beliefs, convictions, and analyses of all combatants are not uniform. «طائفة... و طائفة...»

5- Being preoccupied with oneself leads to suspicion regarding Divine promises. «اهمتهم انفسهم یظنّون باللّه غیر الحق ظنّ الجاهلیة»

6- Do not harbor ill-suspicion toward Allah and His promises, even during crises and defeats. «وطائفة... یظنّون باللّه غیر الحق»

7- Ill-suspicion toward Allah's promises stems from the beliefs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) and lacks truth. «یظنّون باللّه غیر الحقّ ظنّ الجاهلیّة»

8- Self-centeredness and seeking comfort remove a person from the path of Allah's pleasure. «قد اهمّتهم انفسهم یظنّون باللّه غیر الحق»

9- The notion that Muslims should not be defeated, even in cases of weakness, disagreement, and disobedience, is an ignorant notion. «ظنّ الجاهلیة»

10- Individuals weak in faith attribute their defeat to the lack of Divine help. «یقولون لو كان لنا من الامر شى ما قتلنا ههنا»

11- Raising questions that weaken the morale of the combatants is forbidden. «هل لنا من الامر من شى ء»

12- Decisiveness is necessary in the face of temptations and doubts. «قل ان الامر كلّه للّه»

13- Comfort-seeking and self-centered Muslims are constantly worried internally. «یخفون فى انفسهم ما لایبدون لك»

14- Those who think only of themselves expect their opinions to be accepted in decision-making. «اهمّتهم انفسهم...یقولون لو كان لنا من الامر...»

15- There is no possibility of escaping inevitable Divine decrees. «لو كنتم فى بیوتكم لبرز الّذین...»

16- Belief in the inevitable decrees of the Lord grants tranquility to a person. «لبرز الّذین كتب علیهم القتل الى مضاجعهم»

17- Bitter events and defeat on the battlefronts are among the ways to identify the morale and degrees of faith in individuals. «لیبتلى اللّه ما فى صدوركم»

18- Bitter and sweet events are a stage for the manifestation of your spirit and thought; otherwise, Allah already knows all morale and thoughts. «و اللّه علیم بذات الصدور»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ تَوَلَّوْا۟ مِنكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْتَقَى ٱلْجَمْعَانِ إِنَّمَا ٱسْتَزَلَّهُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ بِبَعْضِ مَا كَسَبُوا۟ ۖ وَلَقَدْ عَفَا ٱللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ حَلِيمٌ (۱٥٥)
Those of you who fled on the day when the two hosts met, only Satan had made them stumble because of some of their deeds. Certainly, Allah has excused them, for Allah is all-forgiving, all-forbearing. (155)

Key Points

The Ayah concerns the flight of some Muslims during the Battle of Uhud. As mentioned in the commentaries (tafasir), in the Battle of Uhud, everyone fled except thirteen people, five of whom were from the Muhajirin (Emigrants) and eight from the Ansar (Helpers). Regarding the names of these thirteen people, there is disagreement about who they were, except for Ali عليه السلام. In the Battle of Uhud, the Muslims were divided into four groups: 1- The martyrs (shuhada) 2- The steadfast (sabiran) 3- The fugitives who were pardoned 4- The hypocrites (munafiqan).

Messages

1- One of the causes of fleeing from battle is sin. «تولّوا... ببعض ما كسبوا»

2- Sin opens the field for the whispers (temptations) of Satan. «استزلّهم... ببعض ماكسبوا»

3- The wrongdoer should not be permanently rejected, nor should one hasten in punishing him. «عفااللّه عنهم»

4- Allah forgives the sinners, so you should not reproach them. «عفَااللّه عنهم»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ لَا تَكُونُوا۟ كَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ وَقَالُوا۟ لِإِخْوَٰنِهِمْ إِذَا ضَرَبُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَوْ كَانُوا۟ غُزًّى لَّوْ كَانُوا۟ عِندَنَا مَا مَاتُوا۟ وَمَا قُتِلُوا۟ لِيَجْعَلَ ٱللَّهُ ذَٰلِكَ حَسْرَةً فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ (۱٥٦)
O you who have faith! Do not be like the faithless who say of their brethren, when they travel in the land or go into battle, ‘Had they stayed with us they would not have died or been killed,’ so that Allah may make it a regret in their hearts. But Allah gives life and brings death, and Allah watches what you do. (156)

Messages

1- After the war ends, the enemy spreads discouraging rumors. «لو كانوا عندنا ما ماتوا و ما قتلوا» (If the fighters had remained with us, they would not have died or been killed.)

2- The enemy spreads poison under the guise of sympathy and regret. «لوكانوا عندنا»

3- Whoever makes material life his goal considers death and martyrdom a loss. «لو كانوا عندنا ما ماتوا و ما قتلوا»

4- The hypocrite is a disbeliever, because the speakers of these words were hypocrites, yet the Quran refers to them as «الّذین كفروا» (those who disbelieved).

5- Death and life are among the Divine decrees (Qadar), and are not dependent on war or travel. «واللّه یحیى و یمیت» (And Allah gives life and causes death.)

6- Belief in the Divine decrees removes human fear. Beware of shirking the burden of Jihad in the hope of staying alive. How many individuals have been in wars yet survived, and conversely, how many have not gone to the front lines but died young. «واللّه یحیى و یمیت»

7- Allah is All-Seeing and Aware; be mindful of your states, thoughts, and deeds. «واللّه بما تعملون بصیر» (And Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.)

Ayah

وَلَئِن قُتِلْتُمْ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَوْ مُتُّمْ لَمَغْفِرَةٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَرَحْمَةٌ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ (۱٥۷)
If you are slain in the way of Allah, or die, forgiveness and mercy from Allah are surely better than what they amass. (157)

Key Points

Allah responds to the discouraging rumors spread by the hypocrites (which were mentioned in the previous Ayah) by stating two points: A: Death and life are in His hands. Not going to the front lines does not decrease one's lifespan. B: Whoever steps forward in the way of Allah, whether he dies or is martyred, is victorious. This is because he gave up his life (or lifespan) but received forgiveness and mercy; thus, he has lost nothing. Those who pass away while traveling to the front lines, or while seeking knowledge, or traveling for Hajj and pilgrimage (Ziyarah), propagation (Tabligh) and guidance (Irshad), and other sacred goals, will be covered by divine mercy and forgiveness.

Messages

1- In the divine worldview, death and martyrdom in the way of Allah are better than the entire world and all that is gathered therein. «ولئن قتلتم فى سبیل اللّه او متّم...»

2- What is important is being in the way of Allah, whether it leads to martyrdom or death. «قتلتم فى سبیل اللّه او متّم...»

3- One must first be forgiven, and then receive divine mercy. The word «لمغفرة» (forgiveness) precedes the word «رحمة» (mercy).

4- Forgiveness and mercy have an eternal result, while wealth and possessions have temporary effects. «لمغفرة من اللّه و رحمة خیر مما یجمعون»

Ayah

وَلَئِن مُّتُّمْ أَوْ قُتِلْتُمْ لَإِلَى ٱللَّهِ تُحْشَرُونَ (۱٥۸)
If you die or are slain, you will surely be mustered toward Allah. (158)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the phrase "فى سبیل اللّه" (in the way of Allah) is not mentioned, indicating that the gathering (resurrection) of all those who die or are killed, regardless of the path they were on, is towards Allah. Now that death is inevitable for everyone, and the gathering of everyone in the presence of Allah is also inevitable, why should we not willingly accept the best type of departure? If death and martyrdom are both a return to Allah, then why worry about martyrdom?

Imam Husayn عليه السلام says:

فان تكن الابدان للموت انشئت فقتل امرء فى اللّه بالسّیف افضل

If bodies have been created for death, then the martyrdom of a man in the way of Allah by the sword is the best death. [166]

166) Bihar, Vol. 44, p. 374.

Ayah

فَبِمَا رَحْمَةٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ لِنتَ لَهُمْ ۖ وَلَوْ كُنتَ فَظًّا غَلِيظَ ٱلْقَلْبِ لَٱنفَضُّوا۟ مِنْ حَوْلِكَ ۖ فَٱعْفُ عَنْهُمْ وَٱسْتَغْفِرْ لَهُمْ وَشَاوِرْهُمْ فِى ٱلْأَمْرِ ۖ فَإِذَا عَزَمْتَ فَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يُحِبُّ ٱلْمُتَوَكِّلِينَ (۱٥۹)
It is by Allah’s mercy that you are gentle to them; had you been harsh and hardhearted, they would have surely scattered from around you. So excuse them and plead for forgiveness for them, and consult them in the affairs, and once you are resolved, put your trust in Allah. Indeed Allah loves those who trust in Him. (159)

Key Points

Although the content of the Ayah consists of general instructions, its revelation concerns the Battle of Uhud. This is because the Muslims who had fled and been defeated in the Battle of Uhud were burning in the fire of regret, remorse, and shame. They gathered around the Prophet, offering apologies, and Allah, through this Ayah, issued the command for their general pardon. The word "شُوْر" (Shūr) originally means the sucking of nectar from flowers by a bee. In consultation (Shūrā), a person likewise absorbs the best theories. Mawlawi (Rumi) says: "For these intellects are like bright lamps; twenty lamps are brighter than one."

Messages

1- Gentleness toward people is a Divine gift. «فبما رحمة من اللّه لنت لهم»

2- Harsh and strict individuals cannot manage people. «لانفضّوا من حولك»

3- The governmental system of Islam is based on love and connection with the people. «حولك»

4- Correct leadership and management are accompanied by pardon and compassion. «فاعف عنهم»

5- Accept and attract remorseful wrongdoers and ashamed sinners. «فاعف عنهم و استغفرلهم و شاورهم»

6- Do not overlook the value of consultation due to seasonal failures.[167] «وشاورهم»

7- The Prophet's consultation involves inquiring about the forces, blossoming talents, identifying friends from enemies, selecting the best opinion, creating love and affection, and providing a practical lesson for others. «وشاورهم»

8- Pardon the wrong they committed against you; «فاعف عنهم» seek forgiveness for the sin they committed against Allah; «واستغفرلهم» and in socio-political matters, make them your consultants. «وشاورهم»

9- The Prophet's seeking forgiveness for his community is by Allah's command. Therefore, it is also accepted. «واستغفر لهم»

10- Consultation does not contradict unified sovereignty and decisiveness. «وشاورهم... و اذا عزمتَ»

11- Alongside thought and consultation, reliance upon Allah should not be forgotten. «فتوكّل»

12- First consultation, and then reliance (Tawakkul), is the solution for affairs, whether we achieve the desired result or not. «شاورهم... فتوكّل... انّ اللّه یحبّ المتوكّلین»

167) Although the result of the consultation in the Battle of Uhud, which was based on fighting outside the city, led to defeat, such instances should not deter us from the principle of consultation and its benefits.

Ayah

إِن يَنصُرْكُمُ ٱللَّهُ فَلَا غَالِبَ لَكُمْ ۖ وَإِن يَخْذُلْكُمْ فَمَن ذَا ٱلَّذِى يَنصُرُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۗ وَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ فَلْيَتَوَكَّلِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٦٠)
If Allah helps you, no one can overcome you, but if He forsakes you, who will help you after Him? So in Allah let all the faithful put their trust. (160)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, reliance (Tawakkul) was commanded, and in this Ayah, the reason for relying upon Allah is presented thus: that honor (Izzah) and disgrace (Dhullah) are solely in His hands. It is narrated in a Hadith that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله asked Gabriel: What is reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul)? He replied: It is that you know that creation can bring you neither benefit nor harm, and that you become completely despairing of anyone other than Allah. If a person reaches this degree, he works for none but Allah, fears none but Allah, and hopes in none but Allah, and this is the reality of Tawakkul. [168] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whenever a barrier does not intervene between a person and sin, and the person falls into disobedience (Ma'siyah), this is divine abandonment (Khidhlan Ilahi). [169]

168) Bihar, Vol. 71, p. 138. 169) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 323.

Messages

1- Natural victories are overshadowed by other factors, but divine help and assistance are not overshadowed. «ان ینصركم اللّه فلا غالب لكم»

2- Both victory and defeat are by the will of Allah. «ینصركم... یخذلكم»

3- Faith is inseparable from reliance upon Allah. «فلیتوكّل المتوكّلون»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لِنَبِىٍّ أَن يَغُلَّ ۚ وَمَن يَغْلُلْ يَأْتِ بِمَا غَلَّ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۚ ثُمَّ تُوَفَّىٰ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّا كَسَبَتْ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (۱٦۱)
A prophet may not breach his trust, and whoever breaches his trust will bring his breaches on the Day of Resurrection; then every soul shall be recompensed fully for what it has earned, and they will not be wronged. (161)

Key Points

Perhaps this Ayah is a response to those who abandoned the defensive position at Uhud in order to collect spoils (of war). They ignored the warnings of their commander—who had said: "Your share is preserved and will not be overlooked by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله"—thinking that they might be deprived of their share of the spoils. This Ayah states: The Prophet will not commit treachery regarding your share of the spoils, so be assured and fulfill your duty. Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The stolen item will be materialized in Hellfire before the eyes of the traitor, and he will be told to enter the Fire and bring it out. (170) Deceit (ghishsh), fraud, usurpation (ghasb), and treachery are instances of ghill (treachery/embezzlement). A person who was responsible for collecting Zakat came to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and said: This amount of wealth I have collected is Zakat and belongs to you, and this amount is the gifts people gave to me personally. The Prophet ascended the pulpit and said: If this person had stayed in his house, would anyone have given him a gift?! By Allah, he will be resurrected on the Day of Judgment carrying this very unlawful wealth. (171) When people harbor suspicion against the Prophets, what expectation do you have for yourselves?! It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that he said: People's satisfaction cannot be attained, and their tongues cannot be controlled. (172)

170) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn. 171) Tafsir Furqan. 172) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Not only the Prophet of Islam, but all Divine Prophets are trustworthy. "و ما كان لنبىّ..." (It is not for a Prophet...). Prophethood is incompatible with treachery. One who wishes to raise trustworthy individuals must himself not be treacherous. Although, among non-Divine leaders, those who commit treachery are not few.

  2. No one, not even the Prophets, is safe from people's suspicion. "ما كان لنبىّ ان یَغُلّ" (It is not for a Prophet to commit treachery). (Weakness of faith on the one hand, and love of the world on the other, compels a person to harbor suspicion against the Prophets.)

  3. Defend the sanctity of pure individuals and the friends of Allah. "و ما كان لنبىّ ان یغل..."

  4. Bringing unlawful wealth on the Day of Judgment is a form of punishment. And how difficult it is that on the Day of Judgment, before the Prophets, the martyrs, and all people, a person is brought accompanied by the wealth concerning which he committed treachery. "یأت بما غلّ" (He shall bring what he embezzled).

  5. Remembering the Day of Judgment is a deterrent against treachery. "یأت بما غلّ"

  6. Treachery is treachery, and its amount is not important. The word mā (ما) in the phrase "یأت بما غلّ" includes both small and large amounts.

  7. The Day of Judgment is a scene where the righteous bring their good deeds, "جاء بالحسنة" (He comes with a good deed) (173), and the treacherous carry their acts of treachery. "یأت بما غلّ"

  8. Allah is Just. He gives the reward in full and does not commit the slightest excess. "وهم لایظلمون" (And they shall not be wronged).

173) Al-An'am, 160.

Ayahs

أَفَمَنِ ٱتَّبَعَ رِضْوَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ كَمَنۢ بَآءَ بِسَخَطٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَمَأْوَىٰهُ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱٦۲)
Is he who follows [the course of] Allah’s pleasure like him who earns Allah’s displeasure and whose refuge is hell, an evil destination? (162)
هُمْ دَرَجَـٰتٌ عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بَصِيرٌۢ بِمَا يَعْمَلُونَ (۱٦۳)
They have ranks with Allah, and Allah watches what they do. (163)

Key Points

Regarding the occasion of revelation (Sha'n Nuzul) of this Ayah, it is narrated that when the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله issued the command to move towards Uhud, the hypocrites remained in Medina under various pretexts, and a group of weak-in-faith Muslims followed them and did not attend the front. In Ayah 155, we read that Allah forgives those who fled the battlefield and regretted it, but this Ayah states that He does not forgive the affluent and the hypocrites who make excuses. In some verses of the Quran, the expression is used that there are ranks (degrees) for the believers: «لهم درجات عند ربّهم» [174], «لهم الدّرجات العُلى» [175]. However, in this Ayah, He states that the believers themselves become the ranks (degrees). Just as pure individuals, such as Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام, initially act according to the standard (Mizan), and then they themselves become the standard; they initially revolve around the axis, and then they themselves become the axis of truth.

174) Al-Anfal, 4. 175) Taha, 75.

Messages

1- The goal of true Mujahidin (striving ones) is to attain the pleasure of Allah, not victory, spoils, or showing off. «افمن اتّبع رضوان اللّه...»

2- In an Islamic society, the strivers (Mujahidin) and the affluent should not be viewed equally. (Considering the occasion of revelation) «افمن اتّبع رضوان اللّه... كمن باء...»

3- Turning away from the front and war is returning to the wrath of Allah. «كمن باء بسخط»

4- Divine pleasure or wrath must be the sole criterion for a Muslim's action. «رضوان اللّه... بسخط من اللّه»

Ayah

لَقَدْ مَنَّ ٱللَّهُ عَلَى ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ إِذْ بَعَثَ فِيهِمْ رَسُولًا مِّنْ أَنفُسِهِمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ لَفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۱٦٤)
Allah certainly favoured the faithful when He raised up among them an apostle from among themselves to recite to them His signs and to purify them and teach them the Book and wisdom, and earlier they had indeed been in manifest error. (164)

Key Points

Mann (مَنّ), means a stone used for weighing objects. The granting of any heavy and precious blessing is called mann (favor/obligation). However, making small blessings appear heavy and great is disliked. Therefore, granting a great blessing is good, but making small blessings appear great is disliked. (176)

The appointment of Prophets from among the people has several blessings: 1. People know his background and trust him. 2. They are models for the people in implementing divine commands and decrees. 3. They are familiar with suffering and share in the joys and sorrows of the people. 4. They are accessible to the people.

As is understood from the statements of Imam Ali عليه السلام in Nahj al-Balagha, during the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah), people neither possessed a sound culture nor benefited from hygiene. His brother, Ja'far al-Tayyar, also described the situation of the Age of Ignorance to the Negus during the migration to Abyssinia, saying: «كنّا اهل الجاهلیّة نعبد الاصنام و نأكل المیتة و نأتى الفواحش و نقطع الارحام و نُسى ء الجوار، یأكل القوى منا الضعیف حتّى بعث اللّه رسولا...» We were idolaters who, in those days, ate carrion and engaged in immorality and corruption, cut ties with relatives, and treated neighbors badly. The powerful among us trampled the rights of the weak until Allah sent His Messenger... . (177)

176) Tafsir Namuneh. 177) Kamil al-Tarikh, Vol. 2, p. 80.

Messages

1- The appointment of Prophets is the greatest heavenly gift and divine blessing. «لقد من اللّه»

2- Although the appointment (of the Prophet) is for all people, only the believers are grateful for this blessing and benefit from the light of guidance. «لقد منّ اللّه على المؤمنین»

3- The Prophets were from among the people. «من انفسهم»

4- The recitation of the verses of the Quran is a means of purification (tazkiyah) and teaching (ta'lim). «یتلوا... یزكّیهم و یعلمهم»

5- Purification precedes teaching. «یزكیهم و یعلمهم»

6- Purification and teaching are at the forefront of the Prophets' programs. «یزكیهم و یعلمهم»

7- Growth and self-building must occur under the guidance of the school of the Prophets and divine verses. (Ascetic practices and monasticism whose source is not divine verses and whose teacher is not the Prophets constitute a deviation in themselves). «یتلوا علیهم آیاته و یزكیهم»

8- To better understand the blessing of the appointment of the Prophets, one must refer to the history of the people who preceded them. «ان كانوا من قبل لفى ضلال مبین»

9- Work can be done even in environments steeped in sin and misguidance. «یزكیهم و یعلمهم... وان كانوا من قبل لفى ضلال مبین»

10- In the school of the Prophets, spiritual nurturing and intellectual training are presented alongside insight and wisdom. «یزكیهم و یعلمهم الكتاب و الحكمة»

Ayah

أَوَلَمَّآ أَصَـٰبَتْكُم مُّصِيبَةٌ قَدْ أَصَبْتُم مِّثْلَيْهَا قُلْتُمْ أَنَّىٰ هَـٰذَا ۖ قُلْ هُوَ مِنْ عِندِ أَنفُسِكُمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱٦٥)
What, when an affliction visits you—while you have inflicted twice as much—do you say, ‘How is this?’! Say, ‘This is from your own souls.’ Indeed Allah has power over all things. (165)

Key Points

The Muslims, who suffered seventy casualties and were defeated at Uhud, asked the Prophet: Why were we defeated? Allah addresses His Prophet, saying: Tell them: You inflicted twice this number upon the enemy last year, in the Battle of Badr. You took seventy dead and seventy captives from them, and furthermore, this year's defeat was due to your own disunity, weakness, and disobedience to the command.

Messages

1- When judging, consider both the bitter and the sweet aspects together. Do not only see the defeat of Uhud; remember the victory of Badr as well. «قد اصبتم مثلَیها»

2- When searching for the causes of defeat, first look into your own internal, spiritual, and intellectual factors, and then pursue other factors. «قل هو من عند انفسكم»

3- Allah is capable of all things, but we must acquire the necessary conditions and the merit for benefit. «هو من عند انفسكم... ان اللَّه على كل شى قدیر»

4- Do not assume that merely being a Muslim is sufficient for victory, leading you to use the words "why and how" in every defeat. Rather, in addition to faith, adherence to military principles and laws, and divine traditions is necessary. «قلتم اَنّى هذا قل هو من عند انفسكم»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَصَـٰبَكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْتَقَى ٱلْجَمْعَانِ فَبِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٦٦)
What befell you on the day when the two hosts met, was by Allah’s permission, so that He may ascertain the faithful, (166)

Key Points

Allah has decreed a cause for every matter, and every defeat and victory has a reason. The cause of your defeat at Uhud was that you became weak, lacked coordination, and were greedy for collecting spoils, which the previous Ayah stated: «هو من عند انفسكم» (It is from yourselves). This Ayah states: every choice you make—between unity or dispersion, seriousness or weakness—is within the scope of the Divine Will. It is He Who gives you the power of choice, and whichever path you choose, you will reach its corresponding result. «ما اصابكم... فباذن اللَّه» (Whatever afflicted you... is by the permission of Allah).

Messages

1- Defeats and victories are realized by the Will of Allah, which are His established traditions (Sunan). «فباذن اللّه»

2- Bitter and sweet events are the field for testing and identifying people. «لیعلم المؤمنین»

Ayah

وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱلَّذِينَ نَافَقُوا۟ ۚ وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ تَعَالَوْا۟ قَـٰتِلُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَوِ ٱدْفَعُوا۟ ۖ قَالُوا۟ لَوْ نَعْلَمُ قِتَالًا لَّٱتَّبَعْنَـٰكُمْ ۗ هُمْ لِلْكُفْرِ يَوْمَئِذٍ أَقْرَبُ مِنْهُمْ لِلْإِيمَـٰنِ ۚ يَقُولُونَ بِأَفْوَٰهِهِم مَّا لَيْسَ فِى قُلُوبِهِمْ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَكْتُمُونَ (۱٦۷)
and ascertain the hypocrites. [When] they were told: ‘Come, fight in the way of Allah, or defend [yourselves], they said, ‘If we knew any fighting, we would have surely followed you.’ That day they were nearer to unfaith than to faith. They say with their mouths what is not in their hearts, and Allah knows well whatever they conceal. (167)

Key Points

Three meanings have been given for the phrase «لو نعلم قتالا» (If we knew there would be fighting): A: Your movement towards Uhud outside the city is not an equal war, but rather a type of suicide, and therefore we do not consider this war and will not participate. B: If we knew military techniques, we would participate. C: We are certain that no fighting will occur, and thus participation in the front is unnecessary.

Messages

1- The battlefield exposes the faces of the hypocrites. «و لیعلم الّذین نافقوا»

2- The leader must mobilize the people to confront the enemy. «تعالوا قاتلوا»

3- Jihad is sometimes offensive (initial) for removing tyrants (Taghuts), and sometimes defensive for protecting the lives and property of Muslims. «قاتلوا فى سبیل اللَّه او ادفعوا»

4- Motivations, degrees, and their value are different. «قاتلوا فى سبیل اللّه او ادفعوا»

5- Defending one's homeland and life is a value. «او ادفعوا»

6- Hypocrites justify their stances. «لو نعلم قتالاً لاتبعناكم»

7- Refusing to join the front is a sign of disbelief (Kufr). «هم للكفر یومئذ اقرب منهم للایمان»

8- A person's faith differs in various conditions and times. «هم للكفر یومئذ اقرب منهم للایمان»

9- Not all companions of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله were just (Adil). «هم للكفر یومئذ أقرب منهم للایمان»

10- Allah both threatens and exposes the hypocrites. «هم للكفر یومئذ اقرب... یقولون بافواههم ما لیس فى قلوبهم»

11- What was revealed to everyone at Uhud was only a corner of the hypocrisy; Allah knows the greater part of it. «واللَّه اعلم بما یكتمون»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوا۟ لِإِخْوَٰنِهِمْ وَقَعَدُوا۟ لَوْ أَطَاعُونَا مَا قُتِلُوا۟ ۗ قُلْ فَٱدْرَءُوا۟ عَنْ أَنفُسِكُمُ ٱلْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱٦۸)
Those who said of their brethren, while they themselves sat back: ‘Had they obeyed us, they would not have been killed.’ Say, ‘Then keep death off from yourselves, if you are truthful.’ (168)

Messages

1- When people move towards the battlefronts, the hypocrites stay home. «و قعدوا»

2- The hypocrites weaken the morale of the martyrs' families. «لو اطاعونا ما قتلوا»

3- The hypocrites consider themselves the intellectual leaders of others. «لو اطاعونا»

4- In the worldview of the hypocrites, the priority lies with material life and welfare. «و قعدوا لو اطاعونا ما قتلوا»

5- Belief in the predestination of death leads to courage and acceptance of going to the battlefront, whereas in the view of the hypocrites, martyrdom and eternal bliss have no place. «لو اطاعونا ما قتلوا»

6- One must confront the malicious propaganda of the hypocrites. «قل فادرؤا عن انفسكم الموت»

7- Man cannot ward off death from himself. «فادرؤا عن انفسكم الموت»

8- Death is in the hands of Allah; the battlefront or the home do not play the primary role in death. «فادرؤا عن انفسكم الموت و ان كنتم صادقین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ قُتِلُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِ ٱللَّهِ أَمْوَٰتًۢا ۚ بَلْ أَحْيَآءٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ يُرْزَقُونَ (۱٦۹)
Do not suppose those who were slain in the way of Allah to be dead; no, they are living and provided for near their Lord, (169)

Key Points

Based on the Quran, which considers martyrs alive, we greet the martyrs of Islam, especially the martyrs of Karbala, speak to them, and seek intercession through them. At the end of the Battle of Uhud, Abu Sufyan shouted loudly: These seventy slain Muslims in Uhud are in exchange for our seventy slain in the Battle of Badr. But the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: Our slain reside in Paradise, but your slain are in Hellfire. [178]

Points regarding the Martyr (Shahid) and Martyrdom (Shahadat)

  1. It is narrated in traditions that the martyr possesses seven characteristics bestowed upon him by Allah: The first drop of his blood results in the forgiveness of his sins. He rests his head in the lap of the Houris. He is adorned in heavenly garments. He is perfumed with the best scents. He observes his station in Paradise. He is granted permission to roam and tour all of Paradise. The veils are lifted and he gazes upon the Face of God. [179]
  2. The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله heard a person praying, saying: «اسئلك خیر ما تسئل» (I ask You for the best of what is asked of You). He said: If this prayer is answered, he will be martyred in the way of Allah. [180]
  3. It is narrated: Above every goodness, there is a greater goodness, except martyrdom, for when a person is martyred, no greater good than that is conceivable. [181]
  4. On the Day of Judgment, the martyr holds the station of intercession (shafa'at). [182]
  5. It is narrated from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام that He said: On the Day of Judgment, the martyr's slips will not even be shown to him. [183]
  6. The martyrs of the first line of attack and those who break the enemy lines have a superior rank. [184]
  7. The Mujahidin (strugglers/fighters) enter Paradise through a special gate [185], they go to Paradise before everyone else [186], and they have a special station in Paradise. [187]
  8. Only the martyr wishes to return to the world and be martyred again. [188]
  9. The best and most excellent of deaths is martyrdom. [189]
  10. No drop is more beloved to Allah than a drop of blood spilled in the way of Allah. [190]
  11. On the Day of Judgment, the martyr appears on the scene armed, in battle dress, and fragrant, and the angels send greetings upon him. [191]
  12. Our Imams were martyred. Many prophets and their followers and adherents were also martyred. «وكایّن من نبىّ قاتل معه ربّیّون كثیرا» [192], «یقتلون النبیین بغیر حق» [193]
  13. Despite possessing dozens of exclusive virtues, it was only when Imam Ali عليه السلام was on the verge of martyrdom that He said: «فزت و ربّ الكعبة» (I have succeeded, by the Lord of the Ka'bah). He was the first person to believe, He slept in the Prophet's صلى الله علیه وآله place, He became the Prophet's brother, His house was the only one with a door opening into the Prophet's Mosque, He was the father of the Imams and the husband of Zahra عليها السلام. He was the idol-breaker, and His strike on the Day of the Trench was superior to the worship of the two worlds. Yet, in none of the aforementioned instances did He say: «فُزت» (I have succeeded).
  14. Ali عليه السلام used to say: By the God in Whose Hand is the soul of the son of Abu Talib, enduring a thousand sword strikes in the way of Allah is easier than dying in bed. [194]
  15. During the Battle of Uhud, Ali عليه السلام was distressed that He had not achieved martyrdom, until He received the glad tidings of future martyrdom from the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله.
  16. Shahid Mutahhari writes in the book Hamaseh-ye Husayni (The Husayni Epic): The generous person, the artist, and the scholar immortalize their wealth, art, and knowledge, which are parts of their existence, but the martyr immortalizes himself. [195]
  17. Even in the animal kingdom, a dead sheep is worthless, but a sheep slaughtered facing the Qiblah and in the Name of Allah has value.
  18. Just as the blind person does not comprehend the concept of sight, the living people of this world do not comprehend the life of the martyrs.
  19. If spending wealth in the way of Allah has the potential to grow up to seven hundred times or more, what about giving blood and life in the way of Allah?

178) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayan. 179) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, p. 10. 180) Mustadrak, Vol. 2, p. 243. 181) Bihar, Vol. 74, p. 61. 182) Bihar, Vol. 2, p. 15. 183) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, p. 9. 184) Mizan al-Hikmah. 185) Bihar, Vol. 97, p. 8. 186) Bihar, Vol. 97, p. 11. 187) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 2, p. 241. 188) Kanz al-‘Ummal, Vol. 4, p. 290. 189) Bihar, Vol. 100, p. 8. 190) Wasā’il, Vol. 11, p. 6. 191) Bihar, Vol. 97, p. 13. 192) Al ‘Imran, 146. 193) Al-Baqarah, 61. 194) Nahj al-Balaghah. 195) Hamaseh-ye Husayni, Vol. 3, p. 40.

Messages

  1. Martyrdom is not the end of life; it is the beginning of life. Many living people are dead, but those slain in the way of Allah are alive. «بل أحیاءٌ»
  2. Martyrdom is not losing and giving up; rather, it is finding and gaining. «بل أحیاء... یرزقون»
  3. Being slain is valuable only when it is in the way of Allah. «قتلوا فى سبیل اللَّه»
  4. The notion of destruction or loss for the martyr is a misguided thought that must be corrected. «لا تحسبنّ»

Ayah

فَرِحِينَ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ وَيَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِٱلَّذِينَ لَمْ يَلْحَقُوا۟ بِهِم مِّنْ خَلْفِهِمْ أَلَّا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (۱۷٠)
exulting in what Allah has given them out of His grace, and rejoicing for those who have not yet joined them from [those left] behind them, that they will have no fear, nor will they grieve. (170)

Key Points
