It is mentioned in narrations (Riwāyāt) that the true believers who do not exchange the Qur'an and the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet (علیهم السلام) for any other book or leader are included in the glad tidings of this Ayah. It is also understood that the life in Barzakh (the intermediate realm) is a real life possessing sustenance (Rizq), life (Hayāt), joy, and glad tidings. And the intent (of this life) is not merely the preservation of a good name in history.

Messages

1- The joy of the martyrs is due to the divine favors (Alṭāf Ilāhī), not their own performance. «فرحین بما اتاهم اللّه»

2- The martyrs consider the divine favors to be a grace (tafaḍḍul) from Allah, not a reward for their blood. «من فضله»

3- The martyrs do not forget their comrades and give them glad tidings of a good future. «یستبشرون بالّذین لم یلحقوا بهم»

4- Martyrs have a communal life; they join one another and await the joining of others. «یستبشرون بالّذین لم یلحقوا بهم»

5- The desire for the happiness of others and their joining the caravan of honor and dignity is a value. «و یستبشرون بالّذین لم یلحقوا»

6- The success (kāmyābī) of the martyrs is eternal, and they never grieve the loss of any blessing. «لاخوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»

Ayah

۞ يَسْتَبْشِرُونَ بِنِعْمَةٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱۷۱)
They rejoice in Allah’s blessing and grace, and that Allah does not waste the reward of the faithful. (171)

Messages

1- In the intermediate world (Barzakh), there are good tidings and joy. «یستبشرون»

2- The martyrs attain a blessing and grace that are unknown to us. The words «نعمة» and «فضل» are used in the indefinite form (nukirah).

3- The reward for the deeds of the believers is guaranteed. «لایضیع اجر المحسنین»

4- The promise guaranteeing the reward is a motivation for action. «لایضیع»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لِلَّهِ وَٱلرَّسُولِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَآ أَصَابَهُمُ ٱلْقَرْحُ ۚ لِلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ وَٱتَّقَوْا۟ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱۷۲)
Those who responded to Allah and the Apostle [even] after they had been wounded—for those of them who have been virtuous and Godwary there shall be a great reward. (172)

Key Points

After their victory at Uhud, the disbelievers of Quraysh returned toward Mecca. On the way, they thought, "How much better it would be if we returned to Medina and eliminated the remaining Muslims, thus finishing the matter of Islam entirely." This news reached the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. He commanded mobilization and said: "Let those who participated in Uhud set out." The Muslims prepared themselves. When Abu Sufyan learned of the general mobilization of the Muslims, he assumed that a fresh army had been prepared to turn the sweetness of the victory of Uhud bitter for them. Therefore, he abandoned the idea of a renewed attack and quickly moved toward Mecca. We read in history that during the Battle of Uhud, Hadrat Ali عليه السلام sustained more than sixty wounds and injuries, yet he did not cease supporting the Prophet for a single moment. It is mentioned in the Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq that this Ayah was revealed concerning Hadrat Ali عليه السلام and nine other individuals.

Messages

1- True believers do not cease supporting Islam even in the most difficult circumstances. «استجابوا... من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

2- The participation of the wounded in the battlefield encourages and strengthens the morale of the healthy individuals. «استجابوا... من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

3- Obedience to the Messenger of Allah is like obedience to Allah. «استجابوا للَّه و الرّسول»

4- The participation of the wounded in the battlefield is a sign of their complete loyalty, love, and understanding of the school of thought, the leader, and the goal. «من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

5- Sometimes the conditions of war are such that even the wounded must be present. «استجابوا... من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

6- The more difficult the action, the more worthy of praise it is. «استجابوا... من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

7- Presence on the battlefield and being wounded is worthless if it is not accompanied by piety (taqwa). «للذین احسنوا منهم واتّقوا»

8- Some companions of the Prophet, even those who participated in the battlefield, strayed from sound thinking and piety. «للذین احسنوا منهم واتّقوا»

9- A fighter is worthy of praise who:

A: Does not tire of fighting. «استجابوا... من بعد ما اصابهم القرح»

B: Is a doer of good deeds. «للذین احسنوا منهم»

C: Does not violate the command of Allah and the Messenger. «واتّقوا»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا۟ لَكُمْ فَٱخْشَوْهُمْ فَزَادَهُمْ إِيمَـٰنًا وَقَالُوا۟ حَسْبُنَا ٱللَّهُ وَنِعْمَ ٱلْوَكِيلُ (۱۷۳)
—Those to whom the people said, ‘All the people have gathered against you, so fear them.’ That only increased them in faith, and they said, ‘Allah is sufficient for us, and He is an excellent trustee.’ (173)

Key Points

The enemy's propaganda agents and some simple-minded and fearful people suggest and advise the fighters and mujahidin that the enemy is strong and no one can stand against them, so it is better not to engage in war. But true Muslims, without any fear or dread, respond to them with peace of mind and reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: I am surprised at someone who is afraid, why does he not seek refuge in the saying of Allah: «حسبنا الّه و نعم الوكیل» (Allah is sufficient for us, and [He is] the best Disposer of affairs).[196]

196) Man La Yahduruhu al-Faqih, Vol. 4, p. 392.

Messages

1- Do not be afraid in the face of the enemy's hollow propaganda. «فاخشوهم فزادهم ایمانا»

2- Be watchful of the enemy's infiltrators on the fronts. «فاخشوهم»

3- The strongest leverage against the enemy's threats is faith (Iman) and reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul). «حسبنا اللّه و نعم الوكیل»

4- When a believer is caught in the whirlpool of calamity, he increases his reliance and connection with Allah. «زادهم ایماناً»

5- Both action and effort are necessary, as are faith and reliance (Tawakkul). «استجابوا... حسبنا اللَّه ونعم الوكیل»

Ayah

فَٱنقَلَبُوا۟ بِنِعْمَةٍ مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ وَفَضْلٍ لَّمْ يَمْسَسْهُمْ سُوٓءٌ وَٱتَّبَعُوا۟ رِضْوَٰنَ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو فَضْلٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱۷٤)
So they returned with Allah’s blessing and grace, untouched by any harm. They pursued the pleasure of Allah, and Allah is dispenser of a great grace. (174)

Key Points

After the wounded of the Battle of Uhud, by the command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, mobilized once again to defend Islam and pursued the enemy as far as the station of Hamra al-Asad, the enemies became fearful of the Muslims' readiness and morale, abandoned the idea of a renewed attack, and retreated. This Ayah is in appreciation of the wounded and sincere fighters of the Battle of Uhud.

Messages

1- Moving forward in the path of fulfilling duty, coupled with reliance upon Allah (Tawakkul), causes difficulties to turn into ease. «فانقلبوا بنعمة....»

2- How many are those who went to meet danger in the way of Allah and returned safe. «لم یمسسهم سوء»

3- For the men of Allah, the principle is Allah's pleasure, not martyrdom. «لم یمسّهم سوء واتّبعوا رضوان اللّه»

4- The great favor of Allah is the share of those who participate on the front line. «واللّه ذوفضل عظیم»

Ayah

إِنَّمَا ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ يُخَوِّفُ أَوْلِيَآءَهُۥ فَلَا تَخَافُوهُمْ وَخَافُونِ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ (۱۷٥)
That is only Satan frightening [you] of his followers! So fear them not, and fear Me, should you be faithful. (175)

Messages

1- Any rumor that causes fear and despair among Muslims is satanic. «انما ذلكم الشیطان يخوّف اوليائه»

2- The method of intimidation and threat is the permanent policy of satanic powers. «يخوّف»

3- Cowardly individuals in the fields of battle are allies of Satan and under the influence of Satan. «يخوّف اوليائه»

4- Brave believers are far from the guardianship (wilayah) of Satan. «الشیطان يخوّف اوليائه»

5- Fear of Allah is the basis for obedience, and fear of others besides Allah is the basis for fleeing commands. «فلا تخافون و خافون» (with attention to the preceding verses)

6- A true believer does not fear anyone besides Allah and knows that faith is inseparable from courage. «و خافون ان كنتم مومنين»

Ayah

وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُسَـٰرِعُونَ فِى ٱلْكُفْرِ ۚ إِنَّهُمْ لَن يَضُرُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًٔا ۗ يُرِيدُ ٱللَّهُ أَلَّا يَجْعَلَ لَهُمْ حَظًّا فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱۷٦)
Do not grieve for those who are active in unfaith; they will not hurt Allah in the least: Allah desires to give them no share in the Hereafter, and there is a great punishment for them. (176)

Key Points

It seems the participants in the Battle of Uhud were asking one another: Now that we have been defeated and the disbelievers have returned victoriously to Mecca, what will happen? The Ayah replies to them: Do not be worried; this is a divine respite so that their measure of disbelief may be completed, and they will no longer have any share in the Hereafter.

Messages

1- Maintain your composure, for the efforts of the disbelievers to erase Islam are ineffective. «لایحزنك... انهم لن یضرّوا اللّه»

2- The disbelief of humans does not cause the slightest harm to the sanctity of Allah. «لن یضرّوا اللّه شیئاً»

3- Persistence in the field of disbelief deprives a person of the capacity for repentance and the reception of divine mercy. «الاّ یجعل لهم حظّاً فى الاخرة»

4- Granting respite to the disbelievers is a divine tradition, not a sign of Allah’s unawareness or inability. «یریداللّه الاّ یجعل لهم حظّاً فى الاخرة»

5- A person's deprivation in the Hereafter stems from his actions in this world. «یسارعون فى الكفر... الا یجعل لهم حظّاً فى الاخرة»

6- Both Allah’s grace and His wrath are great. (In the two previous Ayahs, He gave glad tidings of great divine grace regarding the wounded who went to the battlefield by the command of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, and in this Ayah, He reminds the stubborn disbelievers of a great punishment). «لهم عذاب عظیم»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱشْتَرَوُا۟ ٱلْكُفْرَ بِٱلْإِيمَـٰنِ لَن يَضُرُّوا۟ ٱللَّهَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۷۷)
Those who have bought unfaith for faith will not hurt Allah in the least, and there is a painful punishment for them. (177)

Key Points

In the Quran, the issue of buying and selling, and the profit and loss concerning the capital of life (one's lifespan), has been repeated many times. The Quran considers the world a market, people as sellers, and human beliefs and deeds throughout their lives as the commodities of this market. In this market, selling is compulsory, but the choice of the customer rests with the human being. That is, we cannot abandon what we possess—power, action, and belief—but we can make the path of our beliefs and deeds profitable or detrimental. In the Quran, some are praised who trade their souls with Allah and receive Paradise and His pleasure in return, while others are criticized because, due to deviation and poor choice, they either gain no profit; «فما ربحت تجارتهم»[۱۹۷] or they suffer loss; «انّ الانسان لفى خُسر». And in some verses, like the one above, those who trade their faith for disbelief are belittled, and conversely, the believers are reassured that their apostasy does not harm Allah or the path of Allah in any way.

197) Baqarah, 16.

Messages

1- The primary capital of man is faith, or the innate foundations of faith. «اشتروا الكفر بالایمان»

2- In an ideological and cultural revolution, do not worry about minor losses (defections). «اشتروا الكفر... لن یضرّوا اللَّه»

Ayah

وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ أَنَّمَا نُمْلِى لَهُمْ خَيْرٌ لِّأَنفُسِهِمْ ۚ إِنَّمَا نُمْلِى لَهُمْ لِيَزْدَادُوٓا۟ إِثْمًا ۚ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ مُّهِينٌ (۱۷۸)
Let the faithless not suppose that the respite that We grant them is good for their souls: We give them respite only that they may increase in sin, and there is a humiliating punishment for them. (178)

Key Points

The Quran has repeatedly used the phrase "do not think" (or "do not assume") addressing the disbelievers (kuffar), the hypocrites (munafiqin), and those weak in faith. This is because these individuals are deprived of realism, correct analysis, and the necessary insight. They assume creation is futile, martyrdom is annihilation, the world is permanent, honor depends on relying on disbelievers, and their long life is a source of goodness and blessing. The Quran has invalidated all these assumptions.

The people of disbelief consider possessing resources, achieving victories, and living in comfort as signs of their own merit, while Allah grants them respite due to their contamination with disbelief and corruption, so that they may drown in their own depravity.

We read in history that when Yazid martyred Imam Husayn عليه السلام, he took the Imam's family, along with Zaynab al-Kubra عليها السلام, as captives to Sham (Syria). In his assembly, Yazid, full of arrogance, addressed Lady Zaynab, saying: "Did you see that God is with us?" In response, the Lady recited this Ayah and said: "I consider you base, insignificant, and deserving of every kind of humiliation. Do whatever you wish, but by God, you cannot extinguish the Light of God." Yes, a humiliating punishment has been prepared for such pleasure-seekers, so that their imaginary worldly honor may be accompanied by disgrace and humiliation in the Hereafter.

Sinners are of two types: a group that is reformable, whom Allah warns and awakens through admonition and bitter and sweet events, and a group that is not amenable to guidance; Allah leaves them to their own devices so that all their potential (for evil) may manifest. For this reason, Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Death is a blessing for the disbelievers, because the longer they live, the more sins they commit. [198]

Imam Ali عليه السلام said concerning "انّما نملى لهم لیزدادوا اثماً" (We only give them respite that they may increase in sin): How many are those for whom kindness shown to them (by Allah) is istidraj (gradual enticement) and their gradual destruction, and how many are those who are arrogant because their sins and faults are concealed, and how many are those who are deceived by the good things said about them. Allah has afflicted no servant with anything like imla' (granting long respite). [199]

198) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

199) Tuhaf al-'Uqul, p. 203.

Messages

  1. Disbelief prevents the recognition of truth. «لا یحسبن الّذین كفروا...»
  2. The length of life and the duration of comfort are in the hand of Allah. «نملى لهم... نملى لهم»
  3. All human beings seek goodness, but some err in identifying its true instance. «لایحسبن... خیر لانفسهم»
  4. Divine respites are not a sign of favor. «لایحسبنّ... خیر لانفسهم»
  5. Blessings are beneficial only if they are used for growth and goodness, not for evil and sin. «انما نملى لهم لیزدادوا اثماً»
  6. Length of life is not important; benefiting from life is important. «انّما نملى لهم» Imam Sajjad عليه السلام says in the Supplication of Noble Moralities (Du'a Makarim al-Akhlaq): O God! If my life is to become a pasture for Satan, then shorten it.
  7. Do not judge hastily; take the outcome and the Hereafter into account as well. «انّما نملى لهم خیر... و لهم عذاب مهین»
  8. The comfort and rule of oppressors are not signs of Allah's satisfaction with them, nor are they a justification for our silence in the face of them. «نملى لهم... لهم عذاب مهین»
  9. The punishments of the Hereafter have multiple types and stages. 'Adhabun Alim (painful punishment) in the previous Ayah, and 'Adhabun Muhin (humiliating punishment) in this Ayah.

Ayah

مَّا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيَذَرَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَىٰ مَآ أَنتُمْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّىٰ يَمِيزَ ٱلْخَبِيثَ مِنَ ٱلطَّيِّبِ ۗ وَمَا كَانَ ٱللَّهُ لِيُطْلِعَكُمْ عَلَى ٱلْغَيْبِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱللَّهَ يَجْتَبِى مِن رُّسُلِهِۦ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرُسُلِهِۦ ۚ وَإِن تُؤْمِنُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ فَلَكُمْ أَجْرٌ عَظِيمٌ (۱۷۹)
Allah will not leave the faithful in your present state, until He has separated the bad ones from the good. Allah will not acquaint you with the Unseen, but Allah chooses whomever He wishes from His apostles. So have faith in Allah and His apostles; and if you are faithful and Godwary, there shall be a great reward for you. (179)

Key Points

This Ayah is the last Ayah concerning the Battle of Uhud. It states that the world is a great laboratory, and it is not the case that whoever claims faith is simply left alone to live an ordinary life in society. Rather, defeats and victories are meant for the hidden aspects of human beings to become known. Some believers desired knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb) and the hidden aspects of people, wanting to recognize the hypocrites through the unseen, not through trial. This Ayah states that the path to recognition is trial, not the unseen, and the identification of good and bad occurs through gradual tests. Because if good and bad individuals were known through the knowledge of the unseen, the flame of hope would be extinguished, social ties would be severed, and life would descend into chaos.

Messages

1- Allah leaves the disbelievers to their own devices: «نملى لهم لیزدادوا اثماً» (We grant them respite so that they may increase in sin), but He does not leave the believers. «ما كان اللّه لیذر المؤمنین»

2- Separating the pure from the wicked is one of the Divine traditions (Sunan). «حتى یمیز الخبیث من الطیّب»

3- The faith and disbelief of individuals are internal and unseen matters that must be revealed through trial, not through the knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb). «حتّى یمیز الخبیث من الطیّب و ما كان اللَّه لیطلعكم على الغیب»

4- Life with people must be based on their outward appearance; awareness of people's secrets paralyzes life. «و ما كان اللّه لیطلعكم على الغیب»

5- The knowledge of the unseen (Ghayb) belongs exclusively to Allah, and He has granted a share of it only to some of His chosen Prophets—and that is limited to informing them of the unseen, not all its dimensions. «لیطلعكم»

6- Although life must proceed normally, Allah grants the knowledge of the unseen to certain individuals. «ولكن اللّه یجتبى من رسله»

7- The ranks of the Prophets are not equal. «یجتبى من رسله من یشاء»

8- Allah grants the knowledge of the unseen to those who hold a divine mission (Risalah) from Him. «ولكن اللّه یجتبى من رسله»

9- Inner faith must be accompanied by practical piety (Taqwa). «تؤمنوا و تتقوا»

10- Goodness (Tayyib) and purity are inherent and enduring, while wickedness (Khabith) is incidental and transient. «یمیزالخبیث من الطیّب» Separating the impure from the pure is a sign that impurity is incidental.

Ayah

وَلَا يَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَبْخَلُونَ بِمَآ ءَاتَىٰهُمُ ٱللَّهُ مِن فَضْلِهِۦ هُوَ خَيْرًا لَّهُم ۖ بَلْ هُوَ شَرٌّ لَّهُمْ ۖ سَيُطَوَّقُونَ مَا بَخِلُوا۟ بِهِۦ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ وَلِلَّهِ مِيرَٰثُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ (۱۸٠)
Let the stingy not suppose that [their grudging] what Allah has given them out of His bounty is good for them; no, it is bad for them. They will be collared with what they grudge on the Day of Resurrection. To Allah belongs the heritage of the heavens and the earth, and Allah is well aware of what you do. (180)

Messages

1- Be realistic and avoid deviant imagination and conjecture. «لایحسبنّ»

2- The wealth is not yours, so why the stinginess? «اتاهم اللّه من فضله»

3- The source of stinginess is ignorance and a wrong analysis of good and evil. «لایحسبنّ الّذین یبخلون... خیرا لهم بل هو شرّ لهم»

4- One of the duties of the prophets is reforming the insight and thought of human beings. «لایحسبنّ...هو خیرا لهم بل هو شرّ لهم»

5- The love of the world makes evils appear as good to man. «لا یحسبنّ... هو خیر»

6- On the Day of Judgment, even some inanimate objects will be resurrected. «سیطوقون ما بخلوا»

7- The Resurrection is physical. «سیطوقون ما بخلوا»

8- Qiyamah is the scene where real good and evil become manifest. «هو شرّ لهم سیطوّقون ما بخلوا»

9- Man is not absolutely free in disposing of his wealth. «سیطوقون ما بخلوا»

10- Qiyamah is the scene of the embodiment of deeds. «سیطوقون ما بخلوا» It is stated in a Hadith: Whoever does not pay the Zakat (obligatory charity) on his wealth, on the Day of Judgment, his wealth will be placed around his neck in the form of a snake and a collar.(200)

11- The time for the establishment of Qiyamah is not far. «سیطوقوّن»

12- Becoming a captive of wealth in this world causes captivity in the Hereafter. «سیطوّقون»

13- The worst kind of torture is being lashed by the hands of one's beloved possessions. That which we were stingy with in this world will become the source of our torment. «سیطوّقون ما بخلوا»

14- Allah is both the Owner of the lasting inheritance of the world and He is not in need of our spending (in charity). Therefore, the command to spend is for our own felicity. «و للَّه میراث السموات والارض»

15- Allah is the true Inheritor of everything and everyone. We came empty-handed and we leave empty-handed, so why the stinginess? «و للَّه میراث السموات والارض»

Ayahs

لَّقَدْ سَمِعَ ٱللَّهُ قَوْلَ ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ فَقِيرٌ وَنَحْنُ أَغْنِيَآءُ ۘ سَنَكْتُبُ مَا قَالُوا۟ وَقَتْلَهُمُ ٱلْأَنۢبِيَآءَ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ وَنَقُولُ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْحَرِيقِ (۱۸۱)
Allah has certainly heard the remark of those who said, ‘Allah is poor and we are rich.’ We will record what they have said, and their killing of the prophets unjustly, and We shall say, ‘Taste the punishment of the burning. (181)
ذَٰلِكَ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَأَنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَيْسَ بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ (۱۸۲)
That is because of what your hands have sent ahead, and because Allah is not tyrannical to His servants.’ (182)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله wrote a letter to the Jews of Banu Qaynuqa' inviting them to faith, prayer (Salat), charity (Zakat), and spending (Infaq). When this letter reached the hands of the Jewish scholar named "Fathas" at his place of teaching, he turned to his students and those present and mockingly said: According to this invitation, God is poor and we are rich. He asks us for a loan and promises us usury (Riba) and increase! Since the Jews during the time of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله were pleased with the actions of their ancestors regarding the killing of the prophets, Allah attributes the killing to them as well. "و قتلهم الانبیاء" (and their killing of the prophets). Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Those who consider the Imam poor concerning what they give Him are like those who say: "انّ اللَّه فقیر" (Indeed, Allah is poor).[201]

201) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn; Bihar, Vol. 24, p. 278.

Messages

1- Allah hears all sayings, so we should not utter every word. "لقد سمع اللَّه" (Allah has certainly heard).

2- Ignorance and arrogance have no limit, reaching the point where weak, needy humans say: Allah is poor and we are rich. "ان اللَّه فقیر و نحن اغنیاء" (Indeed, Allah is poor and we are rich).

3- The feeling of richness and self-sufficiency is the basis for disobeying divine commands and mocking them. "نحن اغنیاء" (We are rich).

4- Some narrow-minded people imagine that the root of divine commands is need. "انّ اللَّه فقیر" (Indeed, Allah is poor).

5- All sayings and deeds are recorded. "سنكتب ما قالوا و قتلهم" (We will write down what they said and their killing).

6- The sin of uttering nonsense about Allah is equivalent to the sin of killing the prophets. "قالوا ان اللَّه فقیر... و قتلهم الانبیاء" (They said, 'Indeed, Allah is poor'... and their killing of the prophets).

7- The criterion for evaluating speech and action, regardless of who performs it, is truth (Haqq). Even the killing of prophets is subject to punishment because it is unjust. "قتلهم الانبیاء بغیر حق" (their killing of the prophets without right).

8- Man is free in performing actions; therefore, deeds are attributed to him. "قدّمت ایدیكم" (what your hands have put forth).

9- Divine reward and punishment are based on justice and the free and conscious actions of man himself. "ذلك بما قدّمت ایدیكم" (That is because of what your hands have put forth).

10- Divine punishments are not Allah's oppression of mankind, but rather man's oppression of his own soul. "انّ اللّه لیس بظلام للعبید" (And Allah is not an oppressor of His servants).

11- If Allah does not punish the stingy people and the killers of the prophets, He would have oppressed the poor and the prophets. "انّ اللّه لیس بظلام للعبید" (And Allah is not an oppressor of His servants).

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَهِدَ إِلَيْنَآ أَلَّا نُؤْمِنَ لِرَسُولٍ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِيَنَا بِقُرْبَانٍ تَأْكُلُهُ ٱلنَّارُ ۗ قُلْ قَدْ جَآءَكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِى بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَبِٱلَّذِى قُلْتُمْ فَلِمَ قَتَلْتُمُوهُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۱۸۳)
Tell those who say, ‘Allah has pledged us not to believe in any apostle unless he brings us an offering consumed by fire,’ ‘Apostles before me certainly did bring you manifest signs and what you speak of. Then why did you kill them, if you are truthful?’ (183)

Key Points

Some, to evade accepting Islam, made excuses and claimed: Allah took a covenant from us that we should only believe in a prophet who sacrifices an animal and a heavenly lightning bolt burns that sacrifice in front of the people, so that we may be certain of his prophethood. This Ayah was revealed so that the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله could tell those stubborn excuse-makers: If you are truthful, why did you not believe in the prophets before me who, in addition to various types of miracles, presented to you exactly what you are requesting? Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, after reciting this Ayah, said: There was a gap of five hundred years between the excuse-making Jews of the time of the Prophet of Islam and the Jews who killed the prophets, but because of their satisfaction with the killing committed by their ancestors, Allah attributed the act of killing to them as well.[۲۰۲] In the Torah, the Book of First Kings, Chapter 18, verses 3 and 4, the story of the martyrdom of the prophets of the Children of Israel is mentioned. Also, in the Book of Leviticus, Chapter 9, the second verse, the subject of the ram sacrifice is mentioned.

۲۰۲) تفسیر برهان، ج ۱، ص ۳۲۸ ؛ كافى، ج ۲، ص ۴۰۹. (به نقل از تفسیر راهنما)

Messages

1- Do not justify evading the acceptance of truth with religious titles. «انّ اللّه عهدالینااَلاّ نؤمن»

2- The Jews did not consider Moses to be the last prophet; rather, they claimed: Allah’s covenant with us was that the next prophet should be such and such. «عهد الینا... یأتینا بقربان»

3- A person who develops an arrogant spirit both slanders Allah; «انّ اللّه عهد الینا» and refuses to submit to any prophet; «ألاّ نؤمن لرسول» and expects miracles to conform to his desires and demands. «حتى یأتینا بقربان»

4- Animal sacrifice has a very long history. «...بقربان»

5- The history and background of every group and nation is the best witness to the truth or falsehood of their claims. «جاءكم... بالبیّنات و بالّذى قلتم»

6- The excuse-making of opponents throughout history is similar. «و بالّذى قلتم»

7- The Prophet’s argumentation and debate with the Jews was by the instruction of Allah. «قل قد جائكم»

8- Sometimes, to prove the truth or the righteousness of a matter or a more important interest, wealth must be sacrificed. Indeed, if wealth is burned but an eye is awakened or a heart is enlightened, it will not be extravagance. «قد جائكم رسلى... بالّذى قلتم...»

9- Satisfaction with the sin of the ancestors causes one to share in their crime. «فلم قتلتموهم»

Ayah

فَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقَدْ كُذِّبَ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ جَآءُو بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَٱلزُّبُرِ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْمُنِيرِ (۱۸٤)
But if they deny you, [other] apostles have been denied before you, who came with manifest signs, holy writs, and an illuminating scripture. (184)

Messages

1- Familiarity with the history of predecessors raises the spirit of patience and resistance in man. «فان كذّبوك فقد كُذّب رسل من قبلك»

2- All prophets had opponents. «كُذِّب رسل»

3- The movement of the prophets throughout history has been a cultural, intellectual, and ideological movement. «بالبیّنات والزبر والكتاب»

4- The miracles of the prophets were diverse, but the fundamentals of all religions were one. The word «البینات» is plural, but «الكتاب» is singular.

Ayah

كُلُّ نَفْسٍ ذَآئِقَةُ ٱلْمَوْتِ ۗ وَإِنَّمَا تُوَفَّوْنَ أُجُورَكُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۖ فَمَن زُحْزِحَ عَنِ ٱلنَّارِ وَأُدْخِلَ ٱلْجَنَّةَ فَقَدْ فَازَ ۗ وَمَا ٱلْحَيَوٰةُ ٱلدُّنْيَآ إِلَّا مَتَـٰعُ ٱلْغُرُورِ (۱۸٥)
Every soul shall taste death, and you will indeed be paid your full rewards on the Day of Resurrection. Whoever is delivered from the Fire and admitted to paradise has certainly succeeded. The life of this world is nothing but the wares of delusion. (185)

Key Points

This Ayah grants the spirit of patience and resistance to the Prophet and other reformers who are subjected to persecution, annoyance, and denial by the disbelievers, because it states: everyone dies, and obstinacy is temporary, and Allah grants the reward for enduring these difficulties completely and without reduction. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding "كلّ نفس ذائقة الموت": All the inhabitants of the earth and the heavens shall die, and only the Angel of Death (Malak al-Mawt), the bearers of the Throne, Gabriel, and Michael shall remain. Then the Angel of Death is commanded to take their souls as well, and he alone remains. Then Allah addresses him, saying, "Die," and he too dies. [203] And that Imam also said: Whoever has been killed without having tasted death will be returned to the world so that he may taste death. [204]

203) Kafi, Vol. 3, p. 256. 204) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 2, p. 202.

Messages

1- Death is coming, so why all this obstinacy and arrogance against the truth? «كذّبوك... كلّ نفس ذائقة الموت»

2- Death is not non-existence; it is an existential matter that can be tasted, and it is the path of transition from this world to the Hereafter. «ذائقة الموت»

3- Sins and factors leading to Hellfire possess attractions from which man must free himself through the power of faith and righteous deeds. «زُحزِح عن النّار»

4- The full recompense is in the Hereafter, and worldly rewards are worthless. «توفون اُجوركم یوم القیامة... ما الحیوة الدنیا الاّ متاع الغرور»

Ayah

۞ لَتُبْلَوُنَّ فِىٓ أَمْوَٰلِكُمْ وَأَنفُسِكُمْ وَلَتَسْمَعُنَّ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ وَمِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوٓا۟ أَذًى كَثِيرًا ۚ وَإِن تَصْبِرُوا۟ وَتَتَّقُوا۟ فَإِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ عَزْمِ ٱلْأُمُورِ (۱۸٦)
You will surely be tested in your possessions and your souls, and you will surely hear much affront from those who were given the Book before you and from the polytheists; but if you are patient and Godwary, that is indeed the steadiest of courses. (186)

Key Points

When the Muslims migrated from Mecca to Medina, the polytheists began seizing their properties, just as they would harm anyone they could lay their hands on. On the other hand, in Medina, the Jews would also verbally abuse them, and some, with complete shamelessness, would compose love poems (ghazals) about Muslim women and girls or satirize (hajo) them. The leader of this movement was a person named Ka'b ibn al-Ashraf, whom the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله ordered to be killed. This Ayah, while offering solace to the Muslims, asks them to adopt patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) in the face of the enemies' harassment, which becomes the source of their steadfastness in faith. Imam Reza عليه السلام considered the payment of Zakat (alms) to be one of the instances of the trial concerning wealth mentioned in this Ayah.[205]

205) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. Trial (testing) is a serious Divine tradition; we must prepare ourselves. (The letter lām at the beginning and the emphasized nūn at the end of "لتبلونّ" indicate the seriousness of the matter.)

  2. Remembering death and the transience of the world makes matters easy for a person. "كل نفس ذائقة الموت... لتبلونّ فى اموالكم"

  3. The primary tools of trial are wealth and life (self). "اموالكم و انفسكم"

  4. In addition to accepting the risk to life and wealth, one must prepare oneself to hear all kinds of stings and humiliations. "ولتسمعنّ..."

  5. Hearing humiliation and satire, and suffering harassment from the enemy, is one of the means of trial. "لتبلونّ... ولتسمعنّ... اذىً كثیراً"

  6. To achieve sacred goals, sometimes one must endure all kinds of hardships: damage to wealth, life, dignity, and honor. "اموالكم و انفسكم ولتسمعنّ... اذىً كثیراً"

  7. Expecting verbal abuse from opponents leads to the preparedness of Muslims. "لتسمعنّ..."

  8. Opponents of Islam, in striking Muslims, have unity in purpose and sometimes unity in method. "لتسمعنّ من الّذین اوتوا الكتاب... و من الّذین اشركوا"

  9. The enemy is not satisfied with little. "اذىً كثیراً"

  10. Patience (sabr) and piety (taqwa) together are the key to success. Steadfastness without piety can also be found in obstinate individuals. "تصبروا و تتّقوا"

  11. Patience and piety are inseparable. The word "ذلك" (that) is singular, even though it refers to patience and piety, which are two things.

Ayah

وَإِذْ أَخَذَ ٱللَّهُ مِيثَـٰقَ ٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ لَتُبَيِّنُنَّهُۥ لِلنَّاسِ وَلَا تَكْتُمُونَهُۥ فَنَبَذُوهُ وَرَآءَ ظُهُورِهِمْ وَٱشْتَرَوْا۟ بِهِۦ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۖ فَبِئْسَ مَا يَشْتَرُونَ (۱۸۷)
When Allah made a covenant with those who were given the Book: ‘You shall explain it for the people, and you shall not conceal it,’ they cast it behind their backs and sold it for a paltry gain. How evil is what they buy! (187)

Key Points

| Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: Allah took a covenant from the People of the Book that they should declare to the people what was revealed concerning Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله in the Torah and the Gospel, and that they should not conceal it. [206] If today there are millions of Christians, Jews, and Zoroastrians (Magians) in the world, it is all due to the inappropriate silence of their scholars. According to the author of Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan, more than sixty instances of glad tidings regarding Islam and the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله are mentioned in the Old and New Testaments (the Two Covenants), but the scholars of the People of the Book have abandoned them all.

206) Bihar, Vol. 9, p. 192; Tafsir Qummi, Vol. 1, p. 128. (Quoted from Tafsir Rahnamā)

Messages

1- For the guidance of the people, Allah has taken a specific covenant from the scholars. «اخذ اللّه میثاق الّذین اوتوا الكتاب لتبیّننّه للناس»

2- The declaration of truths must be such that nothing remains hidden from the people. «لتبیّننّه للناس و لاتكتمونه»

3- The duty of scholars is to clarify the Divine Book for the people. «لتبیّننّه للنّاس»

4- The motive for scholars' silence regarding the declaration of truth is the attainment or preservation of wealth and status. «واشتروا به ثمناً قلیلاً»

5- Knowledge alone is not sufficient for felicity and salvation; indifference to wealth and status is also necessary. «اوتوا الكتاب... اشتروا به ثمناً قلیلا»

6- Whatever is taken in exchange for concealing the Divine verses is meager. «...اشتروابه ثمناً قلیلا»

7- Adherence to religion and Divine commitments holds high value. If we attain any status or wealth but lose that commitment, we have lost. «ثمناً قلیلا بئس ما یشترون»

Ayah

لَا تَحْسَبَنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْرَحُونَ بِمَآ أَتَوا۟ وَّيُحِبُّونَ أَن يُحْمَدُوا۟ بِمَا لَمْ يَفْعَلُوا۟ فَلَا تَحْسَبَنَّهُم بِمَفَازَةٍ مِّنَ ٱلْعَذَابِ ۖ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۱۸۸)
Do not suppose those who brag about what they have done, and love to be praised for what they have not done—do not suppose them saved from punishment and there is a painful punishment for them. (188)

Key Points

People are of three categories: 1. A group that works and does not expect reward or thanks from people. «لانرید منكم جزاء و لاشكورا» (207) 2. A group that works so that people may know and praise them. «رئاء الناس» (208) 3. A group that expects praise from people without having worked. «یحمدوا بمالم یفعلوا...» One of the instances where backbiting (ghaybah) is permissible is concerning someone who claims a position, specialization, or responsibility for which he is not qualified. «یحبّون ان یحمدوا بما لم یفعلوا» In order to curb this improper tendency—praise without action—all forms of flattery and sycophancy are forbidden in Islam.

207) Insan, 9. 208) Nisa, 38.

Messages

  1. Moral vices, such as self-admiration (‘ujb), arrogance, and expecting flattery from people, lead to certain destruction. «یفرحون... یحبّون... فلا تحسبنّهم بمفازة»

  2. The Quran condemns any improper analysis or interpretation regarding any person or matter. (209) «لاتحسبنّ... فلا تحسبنّهم»

  3. Allah holds people accountable not only for their actions but also for their good and evil desires. (210) «یحبّون ان یحمدوا... لهم عذاب الیم»

  4. What is more dangerous is the expectation of hamd (praise), rather than the expectation of shukr (thanks) or madh (commendation). This is because hamd contains a type of praise mixed with worship. «یحبّون ان یحمدوا»

  5. A sinner might regret his actions, repent, and be saved, but arrogant individuals do not even attempt to repent; therefore, there is no hope for their salvation. «ولاتحسبنّهم بمفازة من العذاب»

  6. Those who are trapped in the delusions of self-worship and imprisoned by the cage of "selfhood" (maniyyat) in this world will also be captive and afflicted by the Divine punishment in the Hereafter. «و لهم عذاب الیم»

209) The verses that begin with the words «لاتحسبنّ», «لایحسبنّ», «أفَحسبتم» express this very meaning.

210) Elsewhere, He also promises punishment to those who love for indecency to spread in society. «یحبّون ان تشیع الفاحشة... لهم عذاب الیم» Nur, 19.

Ayah

وَلِلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۱۸۹)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and Allah has power over all things. (189)

Key Points

Allah's sovereignty over the universe is real, not conventional, because the existence and survival of everything is solely in His hand. For creation, He says: «كن فیكون» (Be, and it is) [211], and for annihilation, He says: «ان یشأ یذهبكم و یأت بخلقٍ جدید» (If He wills, He can do away with you and bring forth a new creation) [212]. Allah's rule is permanent and eternal, but non-divine governments last only for a short while. «تلك الایام نداولها بین الناس» (And these days [of varying fortunes] We alternate among the people) [213]. Yes, this type of real and permanent sovereignty is exclusively for Allah. «وللّه مُلكُ السموات والارض»

211) Baqarah, 117. 212) Fatir, 16. 213) Aal-i Imran, 140.

Messages

1- Wherever the Quran speaks of Divine sovereignty, the heavens are mentioned before the earth, indicating the vastness and grandeur of the heavens. «ملك السموات والارض»

2- Many individuals hold power, but they cannot realize their desires, whereas Allah both possesses sovereignty and is capable of doing everything. «ملك السموات... على كل شى ءٍ قدیر»

Ayah

إِنَّ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱخْتِلَـٰفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّأُو۟لِى ٱلْأَلْبَـٰبِ (۱۹٠)
Indeed in the creation of the heavens and the earth and the alternation of night and day, there are signs for those who possess intellect. (190)

Key Points

It is narrated in the commentaries of Fakhr Razi, Qurtubi, and Maraghi: Aisha was asked: What is the best memory you have of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله? She said: All the actions of the Prophet were astonishing, but the most important thing for me was that the Messenger of Allah was resting in my house one night. He had not yet settled down when He arose, performed ablution (wudu), and stood for prayer. He wept so much that the front of His garment became wet. Then He went into prostration (sujud) and wept so much that the ground became wet. When Bilal came in the morning and asked the reason for all this weeping, He said: Verses from the Quran were revealed to Me last night. He then recited verses 190 to 194 of Surah Aal-i-Imran and said: Woe to the one who reads these verses and does not reflect upon them. In Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr Razi and Majma' al-Bayan, it is narrated from Hadrat Ali عليه السلام that he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله always recited these verses before the Night Prayer (Salat al-Layl). And it is recommended in the narrations that we should also recite these verses. In Tafsir Namuneh, it is narrated from "Nawf al-Bakkali," a special companion of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام, that one night I was in the presence of Hadrat Ali عليه السلام. The Imam rose from His bed and recited these verses. Then He asked me: Are you asleep or awake? I replied: I am awake. He said: Blessed are those who did not accept the pollutions of the earth and who travel in the heavens. The word "al-albāb" (أَلْبَاب) is the plural of "lubb" (لُبّ), meaning pure intellect, far removed from illusion and fantasy.

Messages

1- The creation of the universe has a purpose. «خلق السموات... لایات لاولى الالباب»

2- Cosmology (knowledge of existence) is the prelude to theology (knowledge of God). «خلق السموات...لایات»

In the eye of the wise, the green leaves of the trees, every single page is a register of the Creator's knowledge.

3- The variation in the hours of night and day throughout the year is not accidental in the view of the wise. «و اختلاف اللیل و النّهار لَایات»

4- The Quran encourages people to reflect upon creation. «خلق... لَایات لاولى الالباب»

5- Whoever is wiser must perceive more signs. «لَایات لاولى الالباب»

6- Creation is full of secrets, mysteries, subtlety, and precision, which only the wise have access to understanding. «لَایات لاولى الالباب»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يَذْكُرُونَ ٱللَّهَ قِيَـٰمًا وَقُعُودًا وَعَلَىٰ جُنُوبِهِمْ وَيَتَفَكَّرُونَ فِى خَلْقِ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ رَبَّنَا مَا خَلَقْتَ هَـٰذَا بَـٰطِلًا سُبْحَـٰنَكَ فَقِنَا عَذَابَ ٱلنَّارِ (۱۹۱)
Those who remember Allah standing, sitting, and lying on their sides, and reflect on the creation of the heavens and the earth [and say], ‘Our Lord, You have not created this in vain! Immaculate are You! Save us from the punishment of the Fire. (191)

Key Points

According to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, healthy individuals perform the prayer standing, sick individuals sitting, and those unable (to sit or stand), lying on their side. [214]

214) Tafsir Burhan, Vol. 1, p. 333. (Quoted from Tafsir Rahnamā)

Messages

1- The sign of wisdom is the remembrance of Allah in every state. «اولوا الالباب الّذین یذكرون اللّه قیاماً و قعوداً و على جنوبهم»

2- People of contemplation must be people of remembrance (Dhikr). «اولوا الالباب الّذین یذكرون ... و یتفكّرون»

3- Faith based on thought and reflection is valuable. «یتفكّرون فى خلق السموات و الارض ربّنا ما خلقت هذا باطلاً»

4- Faith and confession with one's entire being are valuable. One should not suffice with mere verbal confession. «یتفكّرون... ربّنا ما خلقت...»

5- Remembrance (Dhikr) and contemplation (Fikr) are valuable when accompanied by one another. Unfortunately, some people engage in remembrance but are not people of contemplation, and some are people of contemplation but are not people of remembrance. «یذكرون... یتفكّرون»

6- What causes growth and proximity (to Allah) is continuous, not seasonal, remembrance and contemplation. «یذكرون» and «یتفكّرون» are present tense verbs, indicating continuity.

7- Sensory knowledge of nature is not sufficient; intellect and contemplation are necessary. «یتفكّرون فى خلق السموات والارض»

8- Whoever contemplates the creation of the heavens and the earth realizes that the Creator of existence is also our Lord. «یتفكّرون... ربّنا»

9- Creation is not futile, even if we do not comprehend all its secrets. «ماخلقت هذاباطلاً»

10- First, intellect and reflection; then, faith and gnosis (Irfan); and finally, supplication and intimate prayer (Munajat). «یتفكّرون... ربّنا... فقنا»

11- If existence is not vain and futile, then we should not be vain and futile either. «ماخلقت هذا باطلاً... فقنا عذاب النار»

12- Existence is purposeful; therefore, the further we move away from the Divine purpose, the closer we get to Hellfire. «فقنا عذاب النّار»

13- Before supplicating, we should praise Allah. «سبحانك فقنا...»

14- The fruit of intellect and wisdom is the fear of the Day of Judgment. «اولوا الالباب... فقنا عذاب النار»

Ayah

رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ مَن تُدْخِلِ ٱلنَّارَ فَقَدْ أَخْزَيْتَهُۥ ۖ وَمَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مِنْ أَنصَارٍ (۱۹۲)
Our Lord, whoever that You make enter the Fire will surely have been disgraced by You, and the wrongdoers will have no helpers. (192)

Key Points

They asked Imam Baqir عليه السلام concerning the verse, "و ما للظالمین من انصار" (and the wrongdoers will have no helpers). He said: It means they have no Imams whom they can call upon by name (and who can intercede for them). (215)

215) Tafsir Ayyashi, Vol. 1, p. 211.

Messages

1- The Fire of the Day of Judgment is severe, but for ulul al-bab (those of understanding) who desire human dignity, the humiliation and disgrace of the Day of Judgment is more painful than the Fire. "من تدخل النّار فقد اخزیته"

2- The wrongdoers are deprived of the intercession of others. "ما للظالمین من انصار"

Ayah

رَّبَّنَآ إِنَّنَا سَمِعْنَا مُنَادِيًا يُنَادِى لِلْإِيمَـٰنِ أَنْ ءَامِنُوا۟ بِرَبِّكُمْ فَـَٔامَنَّا ۚ رَبَّنَا فَٱغْفِرْ لَنَا ذُنُوبَنَا وَكَفِّرْ عَنَّا سَيِّـَٔاتِنَا وَتَوَفَّنَا مَعَ ٱلْأَبْرَارِ (۱۹۳)
Our Lord, we have indeed heard a summoner calling to faith, declaring, ‘‘Have faith in your Lord!’’ So we believed. Our Lord, forgive us our sins and absolve us of our misdeeds, and make us die with the pious. (193)

Key Points

Perhaps the intent of dhunūb (sins) is major sins, and the intent of sayyi’āt (evil deeds) is minor sins, just as in the noble Ayah, «ان تجتنبوا كبائر ما تنهون عنه نكفّر عنكم سیّئاتكم...» (216), sayyi’āt is mentioned opposite kabā’ir (major sins). It is also possible that the intent of sayyi’āt is the effects (or consequences) of sin. In contrast to the wise people (possessors of intellect) who respond to the call of faith and say, «سَمِعنا» (We have heard), there are individuals who disregard this call and will say on the Day of Judgment with complete regret: «لوكنّا نسمع او نعقل ما كنّا فى اصحاب السعیر» (217).

216) Nisa, 31. 217) Mulk, 10.

Messages

1- The wise are ready to accept the truth, and alongside responding to the call of nature (fitrah), they respond to the call of the prophets, scholars, and martyrs. «انّنا سمعنا منادیاً ینادى للایمان...»

2- Seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) and confession are signs of intellect. «اولواالالباب... ربّنا فاغفر لنا»

3- Among the etiquettes of supplication (Du'a) that pave the way for Divine pardon is attention to Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّنا فاغفر لنا»

4- Faith is the prerequisite for receiving forgiveness (Maghfirah). «امنّا، فاغفر لنا»

5 - We should include others in our supplication. «فاغفر لنا»

6- Concealment (covering faults) and pardon are among the functions of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) and methods of nurturing (Tarbiya). «ربّنا... كفّرعنّا»

7- The death of man is by Divine Will. «توفّنا»

8 - Far-sighted wise people desire death alongside the righteous and are concerned with a good ultimate outcome (Husn al-'Aqibah). «توفّنا مع الابرار»

9- The righteous (Abrār) hold a status that the wise people desire. «اولواالالباب...توفّنا مع الابرار»

Ayah

رَبَّنَا وَءَاتِنَا مَا وَعَدتَّنَا عَلَىٰ رُسُلِكَ وَلَا تُخْزِنَا يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ إِنَّكَ لَا تُخْلِفُ ٱلْمِيعَادَ (۱۹٤)
Our Lord, give us what You have promised us through Your apostles, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed You do not break Your promise.’ (194)

Key Points

In these few verses, Allah the Almighty outlines the path of growth for the wise and the possessors of understanding (Ulul al-Albab) as follows: remembrance of Allah, contemplation, attaining wisdom, submission to the prophets, seeking forgiveness (Istighfar), dying well, anticipating divine favors, and salvation from disgrace and humiliation. In the previous verses, the faith of the wise in Allah and the Day of Judgment was discussed, and in this verse, faith in prophethood is also mentioned. «ما وعدتنا على رسلك» (What You promised us through Your Messengers). The repetition of «ربّنا» (Our Lord) in the speech of Ulul al-Albab indicates their profound devotion to the divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). Ulul al-Albab desire both the honor of this world and the honor of the Hereafter. The phrase «آتنا ما وعدتنا على رسلك» (Grant us what You promised us through Your Messengers) relates to this world, as Allah has promised victory to the people of truth: «انّا لننصر رسلنا» (Indeed, We will surely support Our Messengers) (218). And the phrase «و لاتخزنا» (and do not disgrace us) relates to honor in the Hereafter.

218) Ghafir, 51.

Messages

1- Fulfilling promises is among the attributes of divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «ربّنا اتنا ما وعدتنا» (Our Lord, grant us what You promised us).

2- Although Allah fulfills His promises, we must also supplicate. «اتنا ما وعدتنا» (Grant us what You promised us).

3- The wise believe in all the prophets and all divine promises. «ماوعدتنا على رسلك» (What You promised us through Your Messengers).

4- The ultimate desire of the wise is to attain divine favors and salvation from the Fire and disgrace on the Day of Resurrection. «اتنا ما وعدتنا، ولاتخزنا یوم القیمة» (Grant us what You promised us, and do not disgrace us on the Day of Resurrection).

5- Fear and hope must exist side by side. «اتنا ما وعدتنا... ولاتخزنا» (Grant us what You promised us... and do not disgrace us).

Ayah

فَٱسْتَجَابَ لَهُمْ رَبُّهُمْ أَنِّى لَآ أُضِيعُ عَمَلَ عَـٰمِلٍ مِّنكُم مِّن ذَكَرٍ أَوْ أُنثَىٰ ۖ بَعْضُكُم مِّنۢ بَعْضٍ ۖ فَٱلَّذِينَ هَاجَرُوا۟ وَأُخْرِجُوا۟ مِن دِيَـٰرِهِمْ وَأُوذُوا۟ فِى سَبِيلِى وَقَـٰتَلُوا۟ وَقُتِلُوا۟ لَأُكَفِّرَنَّ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ وَلَأُدْخِلَنَّهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ ثَوَابًا مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ عِندَهُۥ حُسْنُ ٱلثَّوَابِ (۱۹٥)
Then their Lord answered them, ‘I do not waste the work of any worker among you, whether male or female; you are all on the same footing. So those who migrated and were expelled from their homes, and were tormented in My way, and those who fought and were killed—I will surely absolve them of their misdeeds and I will admit them into gardens with streams running in them, as a reward from Allah, and Allah—with Him is the best of rewards.’ (195)

Messages

1- Heartfelt prayer guarantees acceptance. A prayer accompanied by constant remembrance of Allah (یَذكرون), contemplation (یتفكّرون), and glorification of Allah (سبحانك) is certainly answered. (فاستجاب لهم ربّهم)

2- The prayer of the people of contemplation and remembrance is answered quickly. (فاستجاب)

3- The answering of prayer is a manifestation of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). (فاستجاب لهم ربّهم)

4- In the Divine worldview, no deed is without reward. (لا اُضیع عمل عاملٍ)

5- A prayer is answered when it is accompanied by the action and effort of the supplicant, although prayer itself is a type of action. (فاستجاب لهم... لااضیع عمل عاملٍ)

6- Both the deed (action) and the doer (agent) are given attention. Both the goodness of the act (Husn Fi'li) and the goodness of the agent (Husn Fā'ili) are considered. (لا اُضیع عمل عامل)

7- The reward for deeds is given only to the believers, because the deeds of the disbelievers are rendered void (habṭ). (عمل عامل منكم)

8- In the Divine perspective, men and women are equal in attaining the high station of humanity. (من ذكرٍ او اُنثى)

9- The male gender is not superior to the female. (من ذكر او اُنثى بعضكم من بعض)

10- In the Islamic society, all Muslims are from one another. (بعضكم من بعض)

11- No one enters Paradise until sins and faults are erased. (لاكفّرن... لادخلّنهم)

12- Migration (Hijrah), striving (Jihad), exile, and enduring harm in the way of Allah are means of Allah's forgiveness. (هاجروا، اخرجوا من دیارهم و... لاكفّرن عنهم...)

13- Love of homeland is a recognized Quranic right, and taking away this right is oppression. (اخرجوا من دیارهم)

14- In traversing the path of Allah, migration, exile, torture, striving (Jihad), and martyrdom are necessary. (فى سبیلى)

15- Although martyrdom is the highest rank, resistance against the harassment and persecution by the enemy requires greater patience. Therefore, the phrase فى سبیلى (in My way) is mentioned alongside اوذوا (were harmed). Indeed, veterans and disabled war heroes must endure and be patient more than the martyrs.

16- We should not reject individuals because of certain slips. In this Ayah, the wise people, who are even among the friends of Allah (Awliyā') and the inhabitants of Paradise, have a history of slips, but Allah covers them up. (لاُكفرنّ)

17- Allah's special favor and attention are for the believing wise people. (لااُضیع... لاُكفرنّ... لاُدخلنّ...) All these verbs are in the first-person singular form. Furthermore, the word ربّهم (their Lord) is a sign of Allah's special favor toward the Ulul Albāb (those of understanding).

18- Do not harbor an iota of doubt or suspicion regarding Allah's promises. All promises are stated with emphasis. (انّى... لاُكفّرنّ... لاُدخلنّ)

19- Any reward might lose its value with a change in place and time, but the Divine rewards are in accordance with human nature (fiṭrah) and creation, and their value is never diminished. (جنات تجرى من ...)

20- The rivers of Paradise flow both beneath its trees (جنات تجرى من تحتها) and beneath the dwelling places of the inhabitants of Paradise. (من تحتهم)

21- Forgiveness and Paradise are general rewards, but the reward that is worthy of Allah's grace and the believers is another reward that is only with Allah, and we have no knowledge of it. (واللّه عنده حسن الثواب)

22- The Divine rewards cannot be fully described for people; let it suffice to know that they are better than any other reward. (واللّه عنده حسن الثّواب)

23- The Divine rewards are accompanied by various forms of honor and dignity (karāmāt). (واللّه عنده حسن الثواب)

Ayahs

لَا يَغُرَّنَّكَ تَقَلُّبُ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْبِلَـٰدِ (۱۹٦)
Never be misled by the bustle of the faithless in the towns. (196)
مَتَـٰعٌ قَلِيلٌ ثُمَّ مَأْوَىٰهُمْ جَهَنَّمُ ۚ وَبِئْسَ ٱلْمِهَادُ (۱۹۷)
It is a trivial enjoyment; then their refuge is hell, and it is an evil resting place. (197)

Key Points

The polytheists of Mecca and the Jews of Medina were well-established due to their trade journeys, but the Muslims were under pressure and hardship because of their migration (Hijra) to Medina, abandoning their wealth, possessions, and lives in Mecca, as well as the economic blockade. This Ayah is a source of comfort for them. In a narration from Imam Ali عليه السلام, it is stated: «ماخیر بخیر بعدها النّار»[219] Goodness and joys followed by the Fire are worthless. Short-term pleasures and eternal torment are for the disbelievers, but temporary hardships and eternal peace and comfort belong to the believers.

219) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 199.

Messages

  1. Even the Prophets need warnings so that they are not exposed to influence. «لایغرّنّك»

  2. Do not be deceived by the dispatch of political, economic, or military delegations, the holding of meetings, or interviews by the enemy. «لایغرّنّك تقلّب الّذین كفروا»

  3. Material successes, no matter how great, are limited and insignificant. «متاع قلیل»

  4. The successes of the disbelievers are not a sign of their righteousness or popularity. «مأواهم جهنّم»

Ayah

لَـٰكِنِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّقَوْا۟ رَبَّهُمْ لَهُمْ جَنَّـٰتٌ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا نُزُلًا مِّنْ عِندِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ لِّلْأَبْرَارِ (۱۹۸)
But those who are wary of their Lord—for them shall be gardens with streams running in them, to remain in them [forever], a hospitality from Allah; and what is with Allah is better for the pious. (198)

Key Points

The word "Nuzul" refers to the first thing used to welcome a guest, such as a drink or fruit. It is as if, through this expression, He is saying: In contrast to the commercial travels of the disbelievers, you should not deviate from the path of piety and faith, because the gardens of Paradise will be the preliminary welcome for you, not the main reward itself.

Messages

1- Worldly blessings are worthless compared to the blessings of the Hereafter. «متاع قلیل... ما عنداللّه خیر»

2- Effort and travel for the sake of worldly gain are permissible, provided piety is maintained and attention is paid to the benefits of the Hereafter. «لایغرّنّك تقلّب الّذین كفروا فى البلاد.... لكن الّذین اتّقوا»

3- Attention to the eternal blessings of Paradise leads to inclination toward piety and prevents being deceived by the meager benefits of the world. «اتّقوا ربّهم لهم جنّات»

4- The rank of the righteous (Al-Abrar) is higher than that of the pious (Al-Muttaqin). «اتقوا، لهم جنات، و ما عنداللَّه خیر للابرار»

Ayah

وَإِنَّ مِنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَمَن يُؤْمِنُ بِٱللَّهِ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ وَمَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ خَـٰشِعِينَ لِلَّهِ لَا يَشْتَرُونَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ثَمَنًا قَلِيلًا ۗ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ أَجْرُهُمْ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَرِيعُ ٱلْحِسَابِ (۱۹۹)
Indeed among the People of the Book there are some who have faith in Allah and in what has been sent down to you, and in what has been sent down to them. Humble toward Allah, they do not sell the signs of Allah for a paltry gain. They shall have their reward near their Lord; indeed Allah is swift at reckoning. (199)

Key Points

Some commentators believe that this Ayah was revealed concerning those among the People of the Book (Ahl al-Kitab) who embraced Islam. They consisted of forty people from Najran, thirty-two from Abyssinia (Habasha), and eight from Rome. [220] However, other commentators have said that the Ayah concerns Negus (Najashi), the King of Abyssinia, who passed away in the ninth year of the Hijra in the month of Rajab. When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله was informed, He told the people: "One of your brothers outside of the Hijaz has passed away. Gather so that we may offer prayers for him in recognition of his services." They asked: "Who is he?" He replied: "Negus." Then the Muslims, accompanied by the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, went to the Baqi' cemetery and offered prayers for him. [221]

220) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan and Kabir Fakhr Razi. 221) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur, Vol. 2, p. 416.

Messages

1- Fairness must be observed, and the virtuous among the People of the Book should be praised. «و انّ من اهل الكتاب لمن یؤمن...»

2- Every school of thought experiences decline and growth. If one group does not believe and conceals the truths, in contrast, another group believes with humility. «انّ من اهل الكتاب لمن یؤمن»

3- Faith is valuable when it is humble, «خاشعین» (khashi'in), comprehensive, «ما انزل الیكم وما انزل الیهم» (what was revealed to you and what was revealed to them), and enduring, not changing due to material gains. «لایشترون بایات اللَّه ثمناً قلیلاً»

4- Any price paid in exchange for selling one's religion is small and insignificant. «و لایشترون بایات اللَّه ثمناً قلیلاً»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱصْبِرُوا۟ وَصَابِرُوا۟ وَرَابِطُوا۟ وَٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۲٠٠)
O you who have faith! Be patient, stand firm, and close [your] ranks, and be wary of Allah so that you may be felicitous. (200)

Key Points

This Ayah recommends patience (sabr) in the face of all types of events and calamities. In the first stage, be patient regarding personal hardships and desires. «اصبروا» In the second stage, show greater resistance against the pressures of the disbelievers. «صابروا» In the third stage, strive to protect geographical borders from enemy invasion, protect ideological and intellectual borders through scholarly discussions, and protect the borders of the hearts from the onslaught of temptations. «رابطوا» The word «رابطوا» stems from the root ‘ribāt’, meaning to tie something down in a place. A caravanserai is also called ribāt because caravans stop there and keep their merchandise, horses, and camels there. Similarly, a heart that is firm and bound to the grace of Allah is called ribāt. The words ‘irtibāt’ (connection), ‘marbūt’ (connected), and ‘ribāt’ share a single root. In narrations, «رابطوا» has been interpreted as awaiting the establishment of prayer (salat).[222] It is as if Muslims strengthen their hearts and souls through the bond created in prayer. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «اصبروا على الفرائض» (Be patient regarding the obligatory duties). «صابروا على المصائب» (Be patient regarding calamities). «ورابطوا على الائمّه» (Defend your Imams/leaders).[223] The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «اصبروا على الصَلَوات الخمس وصابروا على قتال عدوّكم بالسیف ورابطوا فى سبیل اللَّه لعلّكم تفلحون» (Be steadfast in the five daily prayers, be active in fighting your enemy with the sword, and be coordinated with one another in the way of Allah, so that you may succeed).[224]

222) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 223) Kāfī, vol. 2, p. 81. 224) Durr al-Manthūr, vol. 2, p. 418.

Messages

1- You attain perfection in the shade of faith. «یا ایها الّذین امنوا اصبروا و صابروا...»

2- Unless you are patient (sābir) in individual hardships, you cannot resist the enemies of the religion. First «اصبروا», then «صابروا».

3- Do not lag behind others; if the disbelievers resist in their disbelief, give lives, and spend wealth, you too must be steadfast with your lives and wealth in the way of Allah. «صابروا»

4- Muslims must endure each other's difficulties and enjoin patience upon one another. «صابروا»

5- Islam is the religion of connection (communication): connection with people, with Allah, and with the Prophets. «رابطوا»

6- Patience (sabr), mutual steadfastness (musābarah), and vigilance (murābaṭah) must be goal-oriented and placed in the path of piety (taqwa) and Divine pleasure; otherwise, the disbelievers also possess these qualities. «صابروا و رابطوا واتقوااللَّه»

7- Islam is a comprehensive religion; patience and piety are mentioned alongside attention to borders. «اصبروا... رابطوا»

8- Piety (taqwa) is a rank higher than faith (īmān). «امنوا... واتقوا»

9- Patience is the foundation for achieving prosperity and victory. «لعلّكم تفلحون»

Portrait of Surah An-Nisa

This Surah has 176 Ayahs and was revealed in Medina. Its content includes the call to faith and justice, taking lessons from previous nations, severing friendly ties with the enemies of Allah, supporting orphans, rulings on marriage and inheritance, the necessity of obeying the Divine leader, migration (hijra), striving (jihad) in the way of Allah, and so forth. And because the first thirty-five Ayahs of this Surah concern family matters, it has been named Surah An-Nisa (The Women).
